aaaADomainRef.label: Reference to Domain Tag for Parent Object
aaaADomainRef.comment: This object is generated and used only by internal processes.

aaaADomainRefTask.comment: This is generated and used only by internal processes.

aaaAProvider.comment: An abstract class that is the superclass for the Radius/Tacacs/Ldap provider classes.

aaaARbacRule.label: RBAC Rule
aaaARbacRule.comment: This is generated and used only by internal processes.

aaaARetP.label: Record Retention Policy
aaaARetP.comment: The audit log retention policy specifies the maximum number of audit log records to be retained and the maximum number of audit log records to be deleted in a 30-second period. Note that this is an abstract class and cannot be instantiated.

aaaAppToken.label: Request webtoken for an App
aaaAppToken.comment: This is an internal use model to represent the RequestAppToken request schema

aaaAuthMethod.comment: The generic security authentication method. This is an abstract class and cannot be instantiated.

aaaAuthRealm.label: AAA Authentication
aaaAuthRealm.comment: An authentication realm provides authentication to verify the identity of an entity (person or device) accessing fabric devices. The authentication is based on the user ID and password combination provided by the entity trying to access the fabric. Authentication can be performed locally, using the local lookup database, or by remote, using one or more RADIUS or TACACS+ servers.

aaaBanner.comment: An abstract class that contains login banners and cannot be instantiated.

aaaBlockLoginProfile.label: Block User Logins Policy
aaaBlockLoginProfile.comment: This MO stores config for blocking users that fail login continuously

aaaBlockedUser.label: Users that are blocked after continuous failures
aaaBlockedUser.comment: Users that are blocked after continuous failures

aaaCertGenReq.label: Cert Generation Request
aaaCertGenReq.comment: This is an internal use model to represent the CertGen request schema

aaaCertGenRes.label: Cert Generation Response
aaaCertGenRes.comment: This is an internal use model to represent the CertGen response schema

aaaChangeSshKey.label: Change Ssh Key Method
aaaChangeSshKey.comment: The schema of the ChangeSshKey REST method contents. The contents of this method enables users to change their own SSH public key.

aaaChangeX509Cert.label: Change X509 Cert Method
aaaChangeX509Cert.comment: The schema of the ChangeX509Cert REST method contents. The contents of this method enables users to change their own x509 certificate

aaaConfig.comment: The generic security authentication configuration. This is an abstract class and cannot be instantiated.

aaaConsoleAuth.label: Console Authentication Method
aaaConsoleAuth.comment: The authentication configuration for console login.

aaaCtrlrRetP.label: Controller Audit Log Retention Policy
aaaCtrlrRetP.comment: The controller audit log retention policy specifies the maximum number of controller audit log records to be retained and the maximum number of controller audit log records to be deleted in a 30-second period.

aaaDefaultAuth.label: Default Authentication Method for all Logins
aaaDefaultAuth.comment: The default authentication configuration for all login methods.

aaaDefinition.comment: The AAA policy definition. This is an abstract class and cannot be instantiated.

aaaDomain.label: Security Domain
aaaDomain.comment: An AAA domain is the AAA security method for processing authentication requests.

aaaDomainAuth.label: Authentication Method for the Domain
aaaDomainAuth.comment: The authentication configuration for a domain login.

aaaDomainRef.label: Reference to Domain Tag for Parent Object
aaaDomainRef.comment: A reference to the domain that the parent object belongs to.

aaaDomainRolesTuple.label: Internal RBAC Container
aaaDomainRolesTuple.comment: This object is managed internally and should not be modified by the user.

aaaEp.comment: The base class for an AAA endpoint is an abstract class and cannot be instantiated.

aaaFailedLogin.label: User's failed login record
aaaFailedLogin.comment: MO to record each failed login for a user. Records both domain and username, so that remote users are also taken care

aaaFailedLoginUser.label: User that fail login
aaaFailedLoginUser.comment: MO to represent users that fail login

aaaIDomainRef.label: Reference to Domain Tag for Parent Object
aaaIDomainRef.comment: This object is generated and used only by internal processes.

aaaIPRbacRule.label: Implicitly created Partial RBAC Rule
aaaIPRbacRule.comment: IPRbacRule mos are created under aaaRbacEp as a side-effect of the creation of PRbacRule under fv:Tenant

aaaIRbacRule.label: RBAC Rule
aaaIRbacRule.comment: This is generated and used only by internal processes.

aaaLdapEp.label: Default LDAP Authentication Settings
aaaLdapEp.comment: The global security management properties for LDAP endpoints and LDAP provider groups.

aaaLdapGroupMap.label: LDAP Group Map
aaaLdapGroupMap.comment: The MO represents an LDAP Group Map

aaaLdapGroupMapRule.label: LDAP Group Map Rule
aaaLdapGroupMapRule.comment: The MO represents an LDAP Group Map Rule The actual Map consisting of Domains and Roles

aaaLdapGroupMapRuleRef.label: LDAP Group Map rule ref
aaaLdapGroupMapRuleRef.comment: The MO points to actual Ldap Rules

aaaLdapProvider.label: LDAP Provider
aaaLdapProvider.comment: An LDAP provider is a remote server supporting the LDAP protocol that will be used for authentication.

aaaLdapProviderGroup.label: LDAP Provider Group
aaaLdapProviderGroup.comment: An LDAP provider group is a group of remote servers supporting the LDAP protocol for authentication.

aaaLoginDomain.label: Login Domain
aaaLoginDomain.comment: An AAA login domain for authentication and authorization. The AAA configuration can be configured per domain.

aaaModLR.label: Audit Log
aaaModLR.comment: An AAA audit log record is automatically generated whenever a user modifies an object.

aaaNodeCertReq.label: Node Cert Request
aaaNodeCertReq.comment: NodeCertReq - only for data transfer of cert request from nodes like switch

aaaNodeCertResp.label: Node Cert Response
aaaNodeCertResp.comment: NodeCertReq mo only for data transfer of cert request from nodes like switch

aaaOauthProviderGroup.label: OAuth2 Provider Group
aaaOauthProviderGroup.comment: This MO represents a group of AAA OAuth2 servers.

aaaPreLoginBanner.label: Pre Login Banner
aaaPreLoginBanner.comment: A GUI banner is the informational banner to be displayed before user login authentication.

aaaProviderGroup.label: Provider Group Supporting Same AAA Protocol
aaaProviderGroup.comment: A provider group is a set of providers that will be used by the system during the authentication process. During authentication, all the providers within a provider group are tried in order. If all of the configured servers are unavailable or unreachable, the system manager automatically falls back to the local authentication method using the local username and password.

aaaProviderRef.label: Provider Group Member
aaaProviderRef.comment: A member of an AAA provider group.

aaaPwdProfile.label: Password Change/Expiration Policy
aaaPwdProfile.comment: The password profile contains the information about password constraints that apply to all local users.

aaaRadiusEp.label: Default Radius Authentication Settings
aaaRadiusEp.comment: The RADIUS endpoint policy is the global security management properties for RADIUS endpoints and RADIUS provider groups.

aaaRadiusProvider.label: RADIUS Provider
aaaRadiusProvider.comment: A RADIUS provider is a remote server supporting the RADIUS protocol that will be used for authentication.

aaaRadiusProviderGroup.label: RADIUS Provider Group
aaaRadiusProviderGroup.comment: A RADIUS provider group is a group of remote servers supporting the RADIUS protocol for authentication.

aaaRbacAnnotation.label: Annotation to capture rbac info
aaaRbacAnnotation.comment: RbacAnnotation is used for capturing rbac properties of any apic object Objects can append rbacannotations as Object->RbacAnnotation which is then checked for domain eligibility

aaaRbacCheckReq.label: Rbac check API Request
aaaRbacCheckReq.comment: This is an internal use model to represent the Rbac request schema

aaaRbacEp.label: RBAC Rules Database
aaaRbacEp.comment: This is generated and used only by internal processes.

aaaRbacEpRelnHolder.label: Rbac Relations Holder
aaaRbacEpRelnHolder.comment: This is generated and used only by internal processes.

aaaRbacRule.label: RBAC Rule
aaaRbacRule.comment: A role based access control (RBAC) rule allows users from a security domain to read the subtree starting at a specific object.

aaaRealm.comment: The AAA realm is the security method for processing authentication and authorization requests. The realm allows the protected resources on the associated server to be partitioned into a set of protection spaces, each with its own authentication authorization database. This is an abstract class and cannot be instantiated.

aaaRole.label: Role
aaaRole.comment: An AAA role is a set of attributes and privileges that describe what a user is authorized to perform.

aaaRsDomainRef.label: Reference to Domain Tag for Parent Object
aaaRsDomainRef.comment: A source relation to an AAA domain reference.

aaaRsFvEppInband.label: In-band Management Endpoint Profile
aaaRsFvEppInband.comment: A source relation to an in-band management endpoint profile for a fabric node management endpoint group.

aaaRsFvEppOob.label: Out-of-band Management Endpoint Profile
aaaRsFvEppOob.comment: A source relation to an out-of-band management endpoint profile for a fabric node management endpoint group.

aaaRsLoginDomain.label: Login Domain
aaaRsLoginDomain.comment: A source relation to an AAA login domain for authentication and authorization.

aaaRsPol.label: Communication Policy
aaaRsPol.comment: A source relation to the communication policy.

aaaRsPreLoginBanner.label: Pre Login Banner
aaaRsPreLoginBanner.comment: A source relation to the informational banner displayed before user login authentication.

aaaRsSecProvToEpg.label: Attachable Target Group
aaaRsSecProvToEpg.comment: A source relation to the endpoint group through which the provider server is reachable.

aaaRsToSnInbEpg.label: Inband Management to be used for talking to service engine

aaaRsUserEp.label: User Management
aaaRsUserEp.comment: A source relation to a user endpoint. This object is managed internally and should not be modified by the user.

aaaSnCertDownloadStatus.label: Status of Service Node Certs Download

aaaSwRetP.comment: The switch AAA audit log retention policy specifies the maximum number of AAA audit log records to be retained and the maximum number of AAA audit log records to be deleted in a 30-second period.

aaaTacacsPlusEp.label: Default TACACS+ Authentication Settings
aaaTacacsPlusEp.comment: The TACACS+ endpoint policy is the global security management properties for TACACS+ endpoints and TACACS+ provider groups.

aaaTacacsPlusProviderGroup.comment: A TACACS+ provider group is a group of remote servers supporting the TACACS+ protocol for authentication.

aaaUserDomain.label: User Domain

aaaUserEp.comment: A user endpoint is a local user. A user is assigned a role determines the user's privileges, and belongs to a security domain, which determines the user's scope of control

acBank.label: Bank

bgpAAsP.label: Abstraction of Autonomous System Profile
bgpAAsP.comment: The profile of an autonomous system (AS) is a set of routers under a single technical administration, using an interior gateway protocol (IGP) and common metrics to determine how to route packets within the AS, and using an inter-AS routing protocol to determine how to route packets to other autonomous systems.

bgpAdminDist.comment: The administrative distance is used by routers to select the best path when there are two or more different routes to the same destination from two different routing protocols.

bgpCktEpEvi.label: Circuit Endpoint Ethernet VPN Instance
bgpCktEpEvi.comment: This object holds Circuit Endpoint (EPG) Ethernet VPN instance information

bgpComm.label: Community
bgpComm.comment: A list of regular and extended communities.

bgpCtxAfDef.label: Address Family  Context  Definition
bgpCtxAfDef.comment: The BGP address family context definition.

bgpCtxDef.label: Context Policy Definition
bgpCtxDef.comment: An internal object for the BGP context-level policy definition.

bgpCtxPol.label: BGP Timers Policy
bgpCtxPol.comment: The BGP timers policy uses timers to control periodic activities such as the frequency keepalive messages that are sent to its peer, the amount of time the system waits to declare a peer dead after keepalive messages stop being received, and the amount of time before restarting a dead peer. The BGP timer policy enables you to specify the intervals for the periodic activities and supplies two options for graceful restart control: the graceful restart helper option, which configures the local BGP router to support the graceful restart of a remote BGP peer, and the complete graceful restart option, which allows BGP graceful restart to be enabled or disable for an individual neighbor.

bgpDom.label: Domain
bgpDom.comment: The object that represents all the BGP domain (VRF) information.

bgpDomClearDomLTask.comment: The domain clear domain level task.

bgpDomEvi.label: Domain Ethernet VPN Instance
bgpDomEvi.comment: This object holds Domain Ethernet VPN instance information

bgpEVpnImetRoute.label: EVPN Inclusive Multicast Ethernet Tag Route
bgpEVpnImetRoute.comment: This object holds the bgp evpn imet route information

bgpEVpnMacIpRoute.label: EVPN MAC-IP Route
bgpEVpnMacIpRoute.comment: This object holds the bgp evpn mac-ip route information

bgpEVpnPfxRoute.label: EVPN IP Prefix Route
bgpEVpnPfxRoute.comment: This object holds the bgp evpn ip prefix route information

bgpEncapEviBase.label: Ethernet VPN Instance per Encap
bgpEncapEviBase.comment: This object contains information for an Ethernet VPN instance that is defined for an encap

bgpEncapGroupEvi.label: EncapGroup Ethernet VPN Instance
bgpEncapGroupEvi.comment: This object holds EncapGroup Ethernet VPN instance information which carries routes for a Group of Encaps

bgpEntity.label: BGP Entity
bgpEntity.comment: The BGP control plane entity information.

bgpEviBase.label: Ethernet VPN Instance Base
bgpEviBase.comment: This object holds Base Ethernet VPN instance information

bgpExtDef.label: External Profile Definition
bgpExtDef.comment: An internal object for the BGP external profile definition.

bgpExtP.label: External Profile
bgpExtP.comment: When created, this profile indicates that IBGP will be configured for the endpoint groups in this external network.

bgpGrSt.label: Graceful Restart State
bgpGrSt.comment: The per-neighbor graceful restart operational information.

bgpHostLeakP.label: COOP/L2RIB to BGP Host Route Leak Policy
bgpHostLeakP.comment: COOP/L2RIB to BGP host route leak policy. This defines policy to control the distribution of host routes from COOP/L2RIB to BGP

bgpInfraL3out.label: Infra L3out
bgpInfraL3out.comment: BGP Loop prevention and Infra L3out RT features

bgpInst.label: BGP Instance
bgpInst.comment: The per BGP instance information. There is only instance supported in BGP.

bgpInstPol.label: BGP Route Reflector Policy
bgpInstPol.comment: The BGP Instance level policy is used to configure MP-BGP policies inside the fabric.

bgpInterLeakP.label: Inter Protocol Leak Policy
bgpInterLeakP.comment: A policy that defines distribution of routes from one protocol to another protocol.

bgpLocalAsn.label: Local AS
bgpLocalAsn.comment: The local autonomous system (AS) information pertaining to a peer.

bgpLocalAsnDef.label: Local Autonomous System Profile Definition
bgpLocalAsnDef.comment: The local autonomous system number (ASN) definition.

bgpLocalAsnP.label: Local Autonomous System Profile
bgpLocalAsnP.comment: The local autonomous system information pertaining to a peer.

bgpMaxPfxP.label: Maximum Prefix Policy
bgpMaxPfxP.comment: The maximum prefix policy specifies the action to be taken when the number of prefixes advertised by the peer crosses a specified maximum limit. This policy is used as a defensive mechanism to protect resources on the router.

bgpNextHop.label: Nexthop
bgpNextHop.comment: The BGP route information for the next hop.

bgpPathCtrl.label: Path Control
bgpPathCtrl.comment: This object holds global BGP bestpath knobs at Dom level

bgpPeer.label: BGP Peer
bgpPeer.comment: The BGP information pertaining to a peer.

bgpPeerAf.label: Peer Address Family
bgpPeerAf.comment: The BGP peer information for a BGP peer address family. Each address family maintains a separate BGP database, which allows you to configure BGP policy on per-address family basis.

bgpPeerAfEntry.label: BGP Peer Address Family Entry
bgpPeerAfEntry.comment: The operational status information for a BGP peer address family. Each address family maintains a separate BGP database, which allows you to configure BGP policy on per-address family basis.

bgpPeerDef.comment: An internal object for the BGP peer connectivity definition.

bgpPeerEntry.label: BGP Peer Entry
bgpPeerEntry.comment: The BGP peer status specifies the status of a relationship between BGP speakers. A BGP speaker does not discover another BGP speaker automatically. You must configure the relationships between BGP speakers. A BGP peer is a BGP speaker that has an active TCP connection to another BGP speaker.

bgpPeerEntryClearPeerRslt.comment: The Peer Entry Clear Peer Result.

bgpPeerP.label: Peer Connectivity Profile
bgpPeerP.comment: The BGP peer connectivity profile contains the peer IP address and defines the peer connectivity control settings. These values are for BGP routers, which can only exchange routing information when they establish a peer connection between them.

bgpPeerPfxPol.label: BGP Peer Prefix Policy
bgpPeerPfxPol.comment: The peer prefix policy defines how many prefixes can be received from a neighbor and the action to take when the number of allowed prefixes is exceeded. This feature is commonly used for external BGP peers, but can also be applied to internal BGP peers.

bgpPfxLeakCtrlP.label: Prefix Route Leak Control Policy
bgpPfxLeakCtrlP.comment: This object holds route control policy for all networks defined by PfxLeakP in that domain

bgpPfxLeakP.label: Prefix Route Leak Policy
bgpPfxLeakP.comment: This objects holds route leak policy for a given network

bgpPfxRoute.label: Route
bgpPfxRoute.comment: Abstract class for a bgp route

bgpRRNodePEp.label: Route Reflector Node Policy Ep
bgpRRNodePEp.comment: The MP-BGP Route Reflector End Point Policy is used to specify Route Reflector Nodes/Spines for the MP-BGP running inside the fabric.

bgpRRP.label: Route Reflector Profile
bgpRRP.comment: The BGP route reflector profile information.

bgpRibLeakP.label: BGP to RIB Route Leak Policy

bgpRoute.label: Route
bgpRoute.comment: The BGP route table for a particular address family (IPv4 unicast and IPv6 unicast), which contains all the routes advertised by peers and also redistributed into BGP from other routing protocols. This route table is per tenant context (per VRF).

bgpRsBestPathCtrlPol.label: AS Path policy
bgpRsBestPathCtrlPol.comment: Relationship to the BGP AS Path policy

bgpRsBgpNodeCtxPol.label: BGP Context Policy Per Profile
bgpRsBgpNodeCtxPol.comment: Relationship to the BGP context policy

bgpRsPeerPfxPol.label: Peer Prefix Policy
bgpRsPeerPfxPol.comment: The source relation to the peer prefix policy.

bgpRsPeerToProfile.label: Route Control Profile
bgpRsPeerToProfile.comment: Bgp Peer Relation to Routing Policy

bgpRtBestPathCtrlPol.label: Protocol Profile
bgpRtBestPathCtrlPol.comment: Relationship to the BGP AS Path policy

bgpRtBgpCtxPol.label: VRF
bgpRtBgpCtxPol.comment: A target relation to the BGP timers policy.

bgpRtBgpNodeCtxPol.label: Protocol Profile
bgpRtBgpNodeCtxPol.comment: Relationship to the BGP context policy

bgpRtCtrlMapP.label: Route Control Map Policy
bgpRtCtrlMapP.comment: Route control map policy for routes imported/exported into an AF. Control is through route maps.

bgpRtCtrlP.label: Route Control Policy
bgpRtCtrlP.comment: The route control policy for routes coming/going to peers. There are few ways to apply this policy.

bgpRtCtxToBgpCtxAfPol.label: VRF
bgpRtCtxToBgpCtxAfPol.comment: A target relation to the per-address family BGP context policy.

bgpRtEppBgpCtxAfPol.label: Routed External EpP
bgpRtEppBgpCtxAfPol.comment: A target relation to the per-address family BGP context policy.

bgpRtEppBgpCtxPol.label: Routed External EpP
bgpRtEppBgpCtxPol.comment: A target relation to the BGP timers policy.

bgpRtExpP.label: Route Export Policy
bgpRtExpP.comment: Route export policy to control whether to export routes into a different address family. Destination address family is specified in the object. Object may be nested within peer Address family (AF) to subject only those peer's particular AF routes to export. Object can also be under a domain Address family in which case it is applicable to all peers and local routes

bgpRtP.label: Route Policy
bgpRtP.comment: Route policy holds all route targets and route controls

bgpRtPeerPfxPol.label: Abstraction of Peer Connectivity Profile
bgpRtPeerPfxPol.comment: A target relation to the peer prefix policy.

bgpRtPodPGrpBGPRRP.label: Pod Policy Group
bgpRtPodPGrpBGPRRP.comment: A target relation to the BGP instance policy.

bgpRtSummPolDef.label: BGP route summarization Internal policy
bgpRtSummPolDef.comment: Route summarization internal policy for BGP

bgpRtTargetInstrPDef.label: Route Target Instrumentation Profile Definition
bgpRtTargetInstrPDef.comment: BGP Route Target Instrumentation Profile Definition

bgpVni.label: EncapGroup Ethernet VPN Instance
bgpVni.comment: This object holds VNI information for multi-site routes

bgpVpnCtrlP.label: Virtual Private Network Control Policy
bgpVpnCtrlP.comment: This object holds policy to control vpn af information for a given vrf

bgpVpnRoute.label: VPN Route
bgpVpnRoute.comment: The BGP route table for a VPN address family (VPNv4 unicast and VPNv6 unicast). The VPN address family routes are exchanged within the fabric over MP-BGP sessions between spines and leafs. Routes are advertised from border leafs to non-border leafs with spines acting as route reflectors.

callhomeADest.label: Abstract Class for Callhome Destination
callhomeADest.comment: Dest indentifies a single destination to which a callhome message is sent. Multipe destinations can be configured in a single callhome Group and a called message will be sent to all destinations that are part of that Group (depending on the severity level of the destinations and the message).

callhomeAGroup.label: Abstract Callhome Destination Group
callhomeAGroup.comment: CallHome Group brings together all information needed to send CallHome messages to a set of destinations. It is referenced by mon:Src, which determines what information is sent

callhomeASrc.label: Abstract Class for Callhome Source
callhomeASrc.comment: Abstract Callhome source used for callhome and smart callhome

callhomeDest.label: Callhome Destination
callhomeDest.comment: The Call Home destination profile contains the delivery information for receiving email-based alert notifications of critical system policies. A range of message formats are available for compatibility with pager services or XML-based automated parsing applications. You can use this feature to page a network support engineer, email a Network Operations Center, or use Cisco Smart Call Home services to generate a case with the Technical Assistance Center.

callhomeDestReachState.label: Callhome Destination State
callhomeDestReachState.comment: CallHome Dest state Per Apic Node

callhomeGroup.label: Callhome Destination Group
callhomeGroup.comment: Call Home provides an email-based notification for critical system policies. A range of message formats are available for compatibility with pager services or XML-based automated parsing applications. You can use this feature to page a network support engineer, email a Network Operations Center, or use Cisco Smart Call Home services to generate a case with the Technical Assistance Center.

callhomeInvP.label: Callhome Inventory Policy
callhomeInvP.comment: The call home inventory policy is used to send Call Home messages with system inventory information.

callhomeInvTrig.label: Inventory Triggerable
callhomeInvTrig.comment: When you manually trigger an inventory alert group message and do not specify a destination profile name, a message is sent to all active profiles that have either a normal or periodic subscription to the specified alert group.

callhomeProf.label: Callhome protocol profile
callhomeProf.comment: Contains all site information and the address of the SMTP server used to send emails.

callhomeQuery.label: Query
callhomeQuery.comment: A query. This is a query object representing all information on returned objects.

callhomeQueryGroup.label: Callhome Query Group
callhomeQueryGroup.comment: A Call Home query group is a set of Call Home query messages to be sent on returned objects.

callhomeRsDestGroupRel.label: Relation to Callhome Destination Group
callhomeRsDestGroupRel.comment: The name of the node destination group. This is the full path to the organization. The maximum supported string length is 255 ASCII characters.

callhomeRsInvScheduler.label: Relation to Callhome Inventory Scheduler
callhomeRsInvScheduler.comment: A source relation to the scheduler policy.

callhomeRsQueryGroupRel.label: Relation to Callhome Querygroup
callhomeRsQueryGroupRel.comment: A source relation to a call home query group.

callhomeRsSmartdestGroup.label: Relation to CallHome Destination Smart Group

callhomeRtCallhomeInvPol.comment: A target relation to the call home inventory policy.

callhomeRtDestGroup.label: Callhome Source
callhomeRtDestGroup.comment: A target relation to the Call Home group.

callhomeRtDestGroupRel.label: Callhome Inventory Policy
callhomeRtDestGroupRel.comment: A target relation to the Call Home group.

callhomeRtInvPRef.label: Relation Holder
callhomeRtInvPRef.comment: A target relation to the call home inventory policy.

callhomeRtInvPRefEvent.label: Policy holder container
callhomeRtInvPRefEvent.comment: A target relation to the call home inventory policy.

callhomeRtQueryGroupRel.label: Abstract Class for Callhome Source
callhomeRtQueryGroupRel.comment: A target relation to a call home query group.

callhomeSmartCallhomeFault.label: Fault MO for Smart Callhome faults

callhomeSmtpServer.label: SMTP Server for Callhome
callhomeSmtpServer.comment: The hostname or IP for export destination. Call Home sends email messages to either the IP address or hostname, and the associated port number.

callhomeSrc.label: Callhome Source
callhomeSrc.comment: The call home source profile determines the information to deliver and the urgency level for sending email-based alert notifications to a call home destination. A range of message formats are available for compatibility with pager services or XML-based automated parsing applications. You can use this feature to page a network support engineer, email a Network Operations Center, or use Cisco Smart Call Home services to generate a case with the Technical Assistance Center.

capProv.comment: Represents a provider of capability or capacity information.

capProvider.comment: Represents a provider of capability or capacity information.

capRule.comment: Represents a capability or capacity provider rule. Every Rule has a subject that represents the entity which consumes the capability. Namespaces that have concrete rules define the subject property and its types.

cdpAIfPol.comment: The CDP Interface Policy parameters. CDP is primarily used to obtain protocol addresses of neighboring devices and discover the platform of those devices. CDP can also be used to display information about the interfaces your router uses. CDP is media- and protocol-independent, and runs on all Cisco-manufactured equipment including routers, bridges, access servers, and switches.

cdpAdjEp.label: CDP Adjacency
cdpAdjEp.comment: The CDP neighbor information. CDP is enabled by default on all supported interfaces.

cdpEntity.label: CDP Entity
cdpEntity.comment: Holds cdp control plane entity information

cdpIf.label: CDP Interface
cdpIf.comment: CDP information that is operated at a interface level. CDP is used to obtain protocol addresses of neighboring devices and discover the platform of those devices. CDP is also used to display information about the neighboring interfaces/devices.

cdpIfClearIfLTask.comment: A local task to clear the CDP cache for the interface.

cdpIfClearIfRslt.comment: The result of a local task to clear interface counters.

cdpIfPol.label: CDP Interface Policy
cdpIfPol.comment: The CDP interface policy, which is primarily used to obtain protocol addresses of neighboring devices and discover the platform of those devices. CDP can also be used to display information about the interfaces your router uses. CDP is media- and protocol-independent, and runs on all Cisco-manufactured equipment including routers, bridges, access servers, and switches.

cdpIfPolDef.label: CDP Policy
cdpIfPolDef.comment: The read only copy of CDP interface policy.

cdpInst.label: CDP Instance
cdpInst.comment: The object that represents the CDP instance information. Currently only one CDP instance is supported.

cdpIntfAddr.label: Interface Address
cdpIntfAddr.comment: The CDP interface address received from CDP neighbor.

cdpMgmtAddr.label: Management Address
cdpMgmtAddr.comment: The management device IP address received from CDP neighbor.

cdpRtCdpIfPol.label: Abstraction of Access Policy Group
cdpRtCdpIfPol.comment: A target relation to physical interface configuration policy.

cdpRtCdpIfPolCons.label: Ethernet Interface
cdpRtCdpIfPolCons.comment: A target relation to a CDP interface policy.

cdpRtDefaultCdpIfPol.label: VMM Domain
cdpRtDefaultCdpIfPol.comment: A target relation to the CDP interface policy.

cdpRtLeafPGrpToCdpIfPol.label: Access Switch Policy Group
cdpRtLeafPGrpToCdpIfPol.comment: Relationship to cdp interface policy for mgmt port

cdpRtOverrideCdpIfPol.label: Access Attachable Policy Group
cdpRtOverrideCdpIfPol.comment: A target relation to the CDP interface policy.

cdpRtResCdpIfPol.label: Access Instance
cdpRtResCdpIfPol.comment: A target relation to the CDP interface policy.

cdpRtSpinePGrpToCdpIfPol.label: Spine Switch Policy Group
cdpRtSpinePGrpToCdpIfPol.comment: Relationship to cdp interface policy for mgmt port

cdpRtSysCdpInstPolCons.label: System
cdpRtSysCdpInstPolCons.comment: Relationship with the cdp instance policy consumed by this node

cdpRtVswitchOverrideCdpIfPol.label: VSwitch Policy Group
cdpRtVswitchOverrideCdpIfPol.comment: Relationship to policy providing physical configuration of the interfaces

certEntity.comment: Holds rpm control plane entity information

cloudCredentials.comment: Credentials to access the cloud APIs

cloudDefCont.comment: Container for all the cloud:DefCont, under top, to have them neatly organized

cloudResourceGroup.comment: Domainable container for resources that can be shared accros tenants

cloudRtNodeInstToCloudLDev.comment: Relation to clould logical device used for this node instance

cloudRtNodeToCloudLDev.comment: Relation from AbsNode to clould LDev

compHpNic.label: Hypervisor Interface

compHpNicTask.comment: A task to pull physical interface information. This is used internally by the system.

compHvAdjTaskCont.comment: This is a container to hold Hvs that need to retrieve adjacency from vCenter.

compHvClusterRule.label: Cluster affinity rule
compHvClusterRule.comment: Cluster affinity rules, defining VM placement preference [VM Group] Must/Should [not] run on [Host Group]

compHvRef.comment: This reference MO holds the OID of Hv

compHvTask.comment: An internal task to collect hypervisor information from the external system, such as a VMware vCenter. This object is generated and used only by internal process.

compInstPol.label: L4-L7 device Policy
compInstPol.comment: L4-L7 device policy for the controller

compLastEventInfo.label: Last Event Info
compLastEventInfo.comment: Holds info about Last Processed Event Key, TimeStamp and MapId

compLastEventInfoList.label: Last Event Set Info
compLastEventInfoList.comment: Holds info about Last Processed Event Key, TimeStamp and MapId

compLsNodeRef.label: Reference MO to track loose node

compMgmtNic.label: Management Inteface
compMgmtNic.comment: An endpoint or NIC used for management connectivity on the host. For example, vmk interface of a VMware vCenter.

compMgmtNicTask.comment: An internal task to collect management NIC information from the external system. For example, a VMware vCenter. This object is generated and used only by internal process

compMigratingVm.label: pointer to migrating Vm
compMigratingVm.comment: This is the Ep that is yet to be resolved/verified

compMigratingVmCont.label: Migrating VM Container
compMigratingVmCont.comment: This is a container to hold Vms that is in the process of cross dataCenter migration within the same VCenter.

compNic.label: Abstraction of a Nic Interface
compNic.comment: An abstraction of all the network interface cards (NIC) objects.

compNodeCont.label: Fabric Node Container
compNodeCont.comment: Node Container to hold fabric nodes

compObj.comment: An abstraction of compute objects in the system. This object is generated and used only by internal process.

compPHost.label: Abstraction of a Physical Host
compPHost.comment: An abstraction of a physical host.

compPNic.label: Abstraction of a Physical Interface
compPNic.comment: An abstraction of physical network interface card object.

compPendDpVerify.label: Pending Data path verification Container
compPendDpVerify.comment: This is a container to hold Eps that need to be verified. We need to verify an Ep when the corresponding EpPConn Mo is deleted due to DELETE_NON_PRESENT.

compPendEp.label: Pending Ep Resolve
compPendEp.comment: This is the Ep that is yet to be resolved/verified

compPendEpCont.label: Pending Ep Container
compPendEpCont.comment: This is a container to hold MOs that need to be verified or resolved. This container contains compPendDpVerify and compPendResEpCont. compPendDpVerify contains compTenantCont, which contains compPendEp. compPendResEpCont contains compPendEp.

compPendResEpCont.label: Pending Resolve Container
compPendResEpCont.comment: This is a container to hold MOs that need to be resolved.

compPhys.label: Physical Host
compPhys.comment: A physical host or a compute server.

compPolCont.label: Controller Policy Container
compPolCont.comment: An abstraction of the container for various policies. This object is generated and used only by internal process.

compPolContTask.comment: An internal task to send a policy container from one process to another. This object is generated and used only by internal process.

compPpNic.label: Physical Interface on a Physical Host
compPpNic.comment: A physical interface on the physical host.

compProv.label: Provider
compProv.comment: The VM provider inventory is the inventory of a VM provider such as VMware or Microsoft. The VM provider profile policies determine the VM inventory that is collected.

compProvP.label: Abstraction of Provider Profile
compProvP.comment: The VM provider profile, which enables you to configure policies for a VM provider such as VMware or Microsoft.

compRsDlPol.label: Portgroup
compRsDlPol.comment: A source relation to the common policies for the interface connected to the node. Note that this relation is an internal object.

compRsHv.label: Hypervisor

compRsNicAdj.label: Adjacency

compRsODevKeys.label: OpflexKeys

compRsToEdm.label: Relation to edmMgr
compRsToEdm.comment: Association from proxy to actual edmMgr

compRsToInstPCont.label: Relation to pull the l3extinstp cont to compEpPd Shard

compRsTopSystem.label: Relation to copy of topSystem in vmmmgr

compRsUlPol.label: Portgroup
compRsUlPol.comment: A source relation to the common policies for the interface connected to the node. Note that this relation is an internal object.

compRtContGrpDepl.label: Pod

compRtEpPD.label: Portgroup

datetimeANtpAuthKey.label: Client Authentication Key
datetimeANtpAuthKey.comment: This object is a digital signature. The usual data packet plus the key is used to build a non-reversible number that is appended to the packet. The receiver (having the same key) does the same computation and compares the result. If the results match, authentication succeeds.

datetimeANtpProv.label: Datetime Providers
datetimeANtpProv.comment: These user-configured NTP provider names are listed as host names/IP addresses and can be between 1 and 64 alphanumeric characters. These names cannot be changed after the objects have been saved.

datetimeAPol.label: Date and Time Policy
datetimeAPol.comment: The date time policy is based on international time zones and a defined NTP server. Before configuring a date and time policy under a domain group, this policy must first be created. Policies under the Domain Groups root were already created by the system and ready to configure.

datetimeClkPol.label: Date Time Policy
datetimeClkPol.comment: Represents the date/time clock policy information.

datetimeFormat.label: Datetime Format
datetimeFormat.comment: The date/time format policy defines the time-zone for the entire fabric.

datetimeNtpAuth.label: Authentication Key
datetimeNtpAuth.comment: The NTP client authentication configures the device to authenticate the time sources to which the local clock is synchronized. When you enable NTP authentication, the device synchronizes to a time source only if the source carries one of the authentication keys specified by the **ntp trusted-key** command. The device drops any packets that fail the authentication check and prevents them from updating the local clock. NTP authentication is disabled by default.

datetimeNtpAuthKey.label: Datetime Client Authentication Key
datetimeNtpAuthKey.comment: The date/time NTP authentication key is used to define a client authentication key if your NTP server requires authentication.

datetimeNtpProv.label: Providers
datetimeNtpProv.comment: The date/time NTP provider is an NTP server.

datetimeNtpProvider.label: NTP Server
datetimeNtpProvider.comment: Represents the date/time NTP provider information for the switch.

datetimeNtpq.label: Ntp Concrete Details
datetimeNtpq.comment: The Network Time Protocol (NTP) synchronizes the time of day among a set of distributed time servers and clients so that you can correlate events when you receive system logs and other time-specific events from multiple network devices. NTP uses the User Datagram Protocol (UDP) as its transport protocol. All NTP communications use Coordinated Universal Time (UTC).

datetimePol.label: Date and Time Policy
datetimePol.comment: The date time policy is based on international time zones and a defined NTP server. Before configuring a date and time policy under a domain group, this policy must first be created. Policies under the Domain Groups root were already created by the system and ready to configure.

datetimeRsNtpProvToEpg.label: Relation to Reachability Epg
datetimeRsNtpProvToEpg.comment: A source relation to the base class for the Attachable Target Group.

datetimeRsNtpProvToEpp.label: Relation to Datetime Provider Reachability EPP
datetimeRsNtpProvToEpp.comment: A source relation to the abstract representation of the resolvable endpoint profile. This is an abstract class and cannot be instantiated.

datetimeRsNtpProvToNtpAuthKey.label: Relation to Datetime Authentication Key
datetimeRsNtpProvToNtpAuthKey.comment: The authentication key to apply to a specific provider. Keys can be shared with different providers.

datetimeRsNtpProvToNtpAuthKey2.label: New (n-to-m) Relation to Datetime Authentication Key

datetimeRsNtpProvToNtpIFFClientKey.label: Relation to Datetime Secure Session Key
datetimeRsNtpProvToNtpIFFClientKey.comment: Relation From Ntp Provider to Secure Session Key1-n defined. 1-1 needs to enforced in code. As per UCSM guidelines.

datetimeRsNtpProviderToNTPIffKey.label: Ntp Security Session Attachment
datetimeRsNtpProviderToNTPIffKey.comment: Relation to Secure Session keyCardinality is 1-to-n but 1-to-1 should be enforced

datetimeRsNtpProviderToNtpAuth.label: Ntp Authentication Attachment
datetimeRsNtpProviderToNtpAuth.comment: A source relation to the date/time NTP authorization.

datetimeRtCtrlrDatetimeFormat.label: Relation Holder
datetimeRtCtrlrDatetimeFormat.comment: A target relation to the date/time format policy.

datetimeRtFormatPol.label: Relation Holder
datetimeRtFormatPol.comment: The format of the date/time values is defined for the entire fabric and the global date/time format policy defines the time-zone.

datetimeRtNtpProviderToNTPIffKey.comment: Relation to Secure Session keyCardinality is 1-to-n but 1-to-1 should be enforced

datetimeRtNtpProviderToNtpAuth.comment: A target relation to the NTP client authentication.

datetimeRtResDatetimeFormat.comment: A target relation to the date/time format policy.

dbgAcLinkA.label: On-Going Link Atomic Counter Stats
dbgAcLinkA.comment: These are not atomic counters for the physical link. They are for traffic over the corresponding physical link for the trail that contains this object.

dbgAcLinkS2T.label: Atomic Counter for Relation to a Link
dbgAcLinkS2T.comment: The atomic counter trail link counter. There could be multiple physical links between two adjacent nodes in a trail. There is a relation object from the trail to each of these links. AcLinkRel is the set of atomic counters that maintains the amount of traffic going via the physical link that belongs to the trail and hence to the path. This works well for one level of spines. With two levels of spine, the data would indicate the traffic per physical link per path and not per trail. As then the physical link may belong to multiple trails in the same path. With one spine level, a physical link can belong to only one trail in a path. Note that this is not an atomic counter for the entire physical link, hence not naming the managed object as AcLink, but naming as AcTlink for AcTrailLink.

dbgAcOdE.label: Abstract Atomic Counter Entry Stats
dbgAcOdE.comment: Statistics of one atomic counter entry. These managed objects are contained within atomic counter banks. For example, if an instance of this managed object is contained by ODACB0Tx, then the counter values in this managed object instance would indicate the transmit statistics for Bank 0. One of these managed objects would be instantiated for each atomic counter for each entry for each bank in each direction (Tx and Rx) Abstract managed object.

dbgAcOgE.label: Abstract Atomic Counter Entry Stats
dbgAcOgE.comment: Statistics of on-going atomic counter entry. These managed objects are contained within atomic counter banks. For example if an instance of this managed object is contained by OGACB0, then the counter values in this managed object instance would indicate the transmit stats for Bank 0. One of this mo would be instantiated for each atomic counter for each entry for each bank in each direction (Tx and Rx) abstract managed object.

dhcpALbl.label: DHCP Relay Label
dhcpALbl.comment: The identification of the DHCP provider. If the owner is the tenant, then the label is matched with the DHCP label present under the bridge domain (BD). If the owner is the infra, then the label is matched with the DHCP label present under the infra (and associated with the node). If n providers match the label, then all of them get configured as relay.

dhcpLbl.label: DHCP Relay Label
dhcpLbl.comment: A DHCP relay label contains a name for the label, the scope, and a DHCP option policy. The scope is the owner of the relay server and the DHCP option policy supplies DHCP clients with configuration parameters such as domain, nameserver, and subnet router addresses.

dhcpNodeConvP.label: Fabric Node Standalone to ACI conversion

dhcpOption.label: DHCP Option
dhcpOption.comment: The DHCP option is used to supply DHCP clients with configuration parameters such as a domain, name server, subnet, and network address. DHCP provides a framework for passing configuration information to clients on a TCP/IP network. The configuration parameters, and other control information, are carried in tagged data items that are stored in the options field of a DHCP message. The data items themselves are also called options. You can view, set, unset, and edit DHCP option values. When you set an option value, the DHCP server replaces any existing value or creates a new one as needed for the given option name.

dhcpOptionDef.label: DHCP Option
dhcpOptionDef.comment: The option definition. Each DHCP option is defined by a unique name, ID, and option data.

dhcpOptionPol.label: DHCP Option Policy
dhcpOptionPol.comment: The DHCP option policy, which defines lease duration, gateway routers, and other configuration parameters in what are called DHCP options. Every DHCP server must have one or more policies defined for it. The policies are especially useful if you have multiple scopes because you only need to define a policy once and apply it to the multiple scopes. You can define named policies with specific option definitions or you can use system defaults. Note that there are three types of policies—system default, named, and embedded policies: System default - Provides a single location for setting default values on certain options for all scopes. Use the system default policy to define standard DHCP options that have common values for all clients on all the networks that the DHCP server supports. Named - Policies you explicitly define by name. Named policies are usually named after their associated scope or client grouping. For example, they might be assigned options that are unique to a subnet, such as for its routers, and then be assigned to the appropriate scope. Network Registrar includes a policy named default when you install the DHCP server. The server assigns this policy to newly created scopes. You cannot delete this default policy. Embedded - A policy embedded in (and limited to) a named scope, client, or client-class. An embedded policy is implicitly created (or removed) when you add (or remove) the corresponding object, such as a scope. Embedded policy options have no default values and are initially undefined.

dnsAProv.label: DNS Provider
dnsAProv.comment: Sets the DNS provider parameters. DNS uses a hierarchical scheme for establishing host names for network nodes, which allows local control of the segments of the network through a client-server scheme. The DNS system can locate a network device by translating the hostname of the device into its associated IP address.

dnsDom.label: Domain
dnsDom.comment: The DNS domain uses a hierarchical scheme for establishing host names for network nodes and allows local control of the segments of the network through a client-server scheme. The DNS system can locate a network device by translating the hostname of the device into its associated IP address.

dnsDomain.label: DNS Domain
dnsDomain.comment: The DNS domain uses a hierarchical scheme for establishing host names for network nodes and allows local control of the segments of the network through a client-server scheme. The DNS system can locate a network device by translating the hostname of the device into its associated IP address.

dnsEntity.label: DNS Entity

dnsLbl.comment: The network domain name label. Labels enable classifying which objects can and cannot communicate with one another.

dnsProfile.label: DNS Profile
dnsProfile.comment: The DNS profile defines a set of DNS providers and can be deployed to a switch for tenant contexts. To deploy a DNS profile on a switch, the appropriate label has to be defined for the context deployed on switch.

dnsProv.label: DNS Provider
dnsProv.comment: The DNS provider is a DNS server that uses a hierarchical scheme for establishing host names for network nodes, which local control of the segments of the network through a client-server scheme. The DNS system can locate a network device by translating the hostname of the device into its associated IP address.

dnsProvider.label: DNS Provider
dnsProvider.comment: The DNS provider is a DNS server that uses a hierarchical scheme for establishing host names for network nodes, which local control of the segments of the network through a client-server scheme. edrErrDisRecoverPol.label: Error Disabled Recovery Policy
edrErrDisRecoverPol.comment: The error disabled recovery policy specifies the policy for re-enabling a port that was disabled due to one or more pre-defined error conditions.

edrEventP.label: Error Disabled Recovery Event
edrEventP.comment: The per event recovery policy specifies from which error event a port is allowed to recover. The supported error events are bpdu guard, mcp loop, and ep move.

edrRtErrDisRecoverPolCons.label: Error Disable Recovery Policy
edrRtErrDisRecoverPolCons.comment: A target relation to the error disable recovery policy.

edrRtResErrDisRecoverPol.label: Access Instance
edrRtResErrDisRecoverPol.comment: A target relation to the error disable recovery policy.

edrRtSysErrDisRecoverPolCons.label: System
edrRtSysErrDisRecoverPolCons.comment: Relationship with the Error Disable Recovery policy consumed by this node

eigrpACtxAfPol.label: Abstraction of EIGRP Context Policy
eigrpACtxAfPol.comment: The abstraction of the context-level EIGRP policy, which contains the configuration for an address family on a context on the node. The EIGRP policy is configured under the tenant protocol policies and can be applied to one or more contexts (private domains) under the tenant. The EIGRP context policy can be enabled on a context through a relation in the context per address family. If there is no relation to a given address family, or the EIGRP context policy in the relation does not exist, the default context policy created under tenant common will be used for that address family. The allowed configurations include an autonomous system number specifically for EIGRP processes, administrative distance for internal/external route, maximum ECMP paths allowed, active timer interval, and metric version (32 bit/64 bit metrics).

eigrpAExtP.label: Abstraction of External Profile
eigrpAExtP.comment: The abstraction of an EIGRP external profile.

eigrpAIfP.label: Abstraction of Interface Profile
eigrpAIfP.comment: The abstraction of an interface profile.

eigrpAStubP.label: Abstraction of EIGRP Stub Policy
eigrpAStubP.comment: The abstraction of an stub profile.

eigrpASummP.label: Abstraction of Route Summary
eigrpASummP.comment: The abstraction of an EIGRP summary route.

eigrpAf.label: Address Family
eigrpAf.comment: The EIGRP abstraction for an address family domain.

eigrpAuthP.label: Authentication Policy
eigrpAuthP.comment: This object holds authentication policy information

eigrpCtxAfDef.label: Address Family  Context  Definition
eigrpCtxAfDef.comment: The context-level EIGRP address family definition.

eigrpCtxAfPol.label: EIGRP Address Family Context Policy
eigrpCtxAfPol.comment: An EIGRP context policy can be applied on one or more contexts under the tenant. EIGRP context policies can be enabled on a context through a relation in the context per address family. If there is no relation to a given address family such as IPv6 or the EIGRP context policy mentioned in the relation doesn't exist, then the default context policy created under Tenant Common will be used for that address family.

eigrpDbRec.label: Database Record
eigrpDbRec.comment: An abstraction for an EIGRP database record.

eigrpDomAf.label: Address Family Domain
eigrpDomAf.comment: The EIGRP address family domain (VRF) information.

eigrpDomAfStats.label: Dom AF Statistics
eigrpDomAfStats.comment: The EIGRP address family domain statistics.

eigrpEntity.label: EIGRP Entity
eigrpEntity.comment: The EIGRP control plane entity information.

eigrpExtCommNhRec.label: Extended Community Nexthop Record
eigrpExtCommNhRec.comment: The EIGRP nexthop extended community information.

eigrpExtProtNhRec.label: External protocol Nexthop Record
eigrpExtProtNhRec.comment: The EIGRP nexthop information for external routes. External routes are routes from a different protocol or from a different EIGRP AS.

eigrpIf.label: EIGRP Interface
eigrpIf.comment: An EIGRP configured interface combines the benefits of distance vector protocols with the features of link-state protocols.

eigrpIfP.label: Interface Profile
eigrpIfP.comment: The EIGRP interface profile, which enables EIGRP on the interface. Optionally, the EIGRP interface profile can have a relation to an EIGRP interface policy for more granular control over interface proprieties.

eigrpIfPol.label: EIGRP Interface Policy
eigrpIfPol.comment: The EIGRP interface policy, which defines a common configuration that will apply to one or more EIGRP interfaces.

eigrpInterLeakP.label: Inter Protocol Leak Policy
eigrpInterLeakP.comment: The inter protocol route leak policy, which defines the distribution of routes from one protocol to another.

eigrpRibLeakP.label: EIGRP to RIB Route Leak Policy

eigrpRsIfDefToEigrpIf.label: EIGRP Interface
eigrpRsIfDefToEigrpIf.comment: A source relation to EIGRP interfaces.

eigrpRsIfPol.label: Interface Policy
eigrpRsIfPol.comment: A source relation to the EIGRP interface policy.

eigrpRsKeyChainPol.label: KeyChain Policy
eigrpRsKeyChainPol.comment: Relationship to the Tenant level keychain policy

eigrpRtCtrlP.label: Route Control Policy
eigrpRtCtrlP.comment: The route control policy for routes coming/going over interfaces. There are few ways to apply this policy, controlling through route maps or prefix lists. The direction specifies whether to apply this policy in the incoming or outgoing direction

eigrpRtCtxToEigrpCtxAfPol.label: VRF
eigrpRtCtxToEigrpCtxAfPol.comment: A target relation to the per-address family EIGRP context policy.

eigrpRtEppEigrpCtxAfPol.label: Routed External EpP
eigrpRtEppEigrpCtxAfPol.comment: A target relation to the per-address family EIGRP context policy.

eigrpRtEppEigrpIfPol.label: Routed External EpP
eigrpRtEppEigrpIfPol.comment: A target relation to the EIGRP interface policy.

eigrpRtIfDefToEigrpIf.label: Interface Profile Definition
eigrpRtIfDefToEigrpIf.comment: A target relation to EIGRP interfaces.

eigrpRtIfPol.label: Interface Profile
eigrpRtIfPol.comment: A target relation to the EIGRP interface policy.

eigrpRtMetricAlterP.label: Route Metric Alteration Policy
eigrpRtMetricAlterP.comment: Metric Alteration policy for EIGRP routes. Selection of routes for which metric has to be adjusted, can be done through route maps or prefix lists. The direction specifies whether to apply this policy for incoming or outgoing routes

epLoopProtectP.label: EP Loop Protection Policy
epLoopProtectP.comment: The endpoint loop protection policy specifies how loops detected by frequent mac moves are handled.

eqptCard.label: Module
eqptCard.comment: A container for different cards such as line cards and fabric cards.

eqptCh.label: Chassis
eqptCh.comment: A hardware chassis container contains chassis properties such as its role in the fabric (spine/tor) and a description of switch.

eqptChLocateLTask.comment: A local task to turn on the beacon LED on the chassis.

eqptChLocateRslt.comment: The result object created by the switch in response to the Chassis Locate Task to turn on the beacon LED on the chassis. This object indicates whether the chassis locate task succeeded or failed.

eqptChOutOfServiceLTask.comment: A local task to decommission the switch.

eqptChOutOfServiceRslt.comment: A chassis out of service result in response to the decommission request. It indicates the success or failure of the request.

eqptChReloadLTask.comment: A local task to reload the chassis.

eqptChReloadRslt.comment: The chassis reload result, indicating the success or failure of the reload action.

eqptComp.label: Component
eqptComp.comment: An equipment component such as a card, port, transceiver, or ASIC.

eqptIntP.label: Internal Port
eqptIntP.comment: The abstraction of an internal port. Internal ports are internal constructs that are invisible from outside.

eqptIoP.label: IO Port
eqptIoP.comment: The abstraction of an external IO port.

eqptItem.label: Item
eqptItem.comment: The abstraction for any equipment component/container in the system.

eqptLC.label: Line Module
eqptLC.comment: A line card (IO card) contains IO ports and stores various line card related state mac addresses assigned to this card, such as whether the card went online.

eqptLCLocateLTask.comment: A task to locate the line card.

eqptLCLocateRslt.comment: The result of the task to locate the line card.

eqptLCReloadLTask.comment: A task to reload the line card.

eqptLCReloadRslt.comment: The result of the task to reload the line card.

eqptLCSlot.label: Line Module Slot
eqptLCSlot.comment: The slot for the module card.

eqptLEM.label: The line card information
eqptLEM.comment: LEM Card (IO card), containing io ports

eqptLeafP.label: Access Port
eqptLeafP.comment: A leaf port is an external IO port on a leaf.

eqptLed.label: LED
eqptLed.comment: The abstraction of an LED. This is a superclass for LED types, such as an indicator LED.

eqptLocLed.label: Locator LED
eqptLocLed.comment: A locator LED on chassis components.

eqptdiagpASynthObj.label: Abstract Synthetic MO to Contain Bit Masks for the Diag Tests
eqptdiagpASynthObj.comment: An internal object that contains the bitmask for diagnostic tests.

eqptdiagpCardTestSetOd.label: On-Demand Diag Policy Set for the Specified Card Type
eqptdiagpCardTestSetOd.comment: The on-demand abstract diagnostic policy for cards.

eqptdiagpExtChCardTsOd.label: On-Demand Diag Policy Set for the Extended Chassis Card Type
eqptdiagpExtChCardTsOd.comment: An on-demand diagnostic policy for extended chassis cards.

eqptdiagpFcTsOd.label: On-Demand Diag Policy Set for the Fabric Controller Card Type
eqptdiagpFcTsOd.comment: An on-demand abstract diagnostic policy for fabric cards.

eqptdiagpFpTsOd.label: On-Demand Diag Policy Set for Specified Fabric Port
eqptdiagpFpTsOd.comment: The on-demand abstract diagnostic policy for fabric ports.

eqptdiagpGrpTests.label: Diag Tests in a Diag Group
eqptdiagpGrpTests.comment: The diagnostic group to diagnostic test mappings.

eqptdiagpLcTsOd.label: On-Demand Diag Policy Set for the IO Line Card Type
eqptdiagpLcTsOd.comment: An on-demand abstract diagnostic policy for line cards.

eqptdiagpLeTsBtEcc.label: Leaf Bootup Diag Policy Set for the Extended Chassis Card Type
eqptdiagpLeTsBtEcc.comment: The diagnostic test set for leaf fabric nodes to run at bootup on extended chassis cards (FEXes).

eqptdiagpLeTsBtLc.label: Leaf Linecard Bootup Diags
eqptdiagpLeTsBtLc.comment: The diagnostic test set for leaf fabric nodes to run at bootup on line cards (I/O cards).

eqptdiagpLeTsBtSc.label: Bootup Diag Policy for the Leaf Node Supervisor Line Cards
eqptdiagpLeTsBtSc.comment: The diagnostic test set for leaf fabric nodes to run at bootup on supervisor cards.

eqptdiagpLeTsHlEcc.label: Leaf Health Diag Policy Set for the Extended Chassis Card Type
eqptdiagpLeTsHlEcc.comment: The diagnostic test set for leaf fabric nodes to run on extended chassis cards (FEXes).

eqptdiagpLeTsHlLc.label: Leaf Linecard Health Diags
eqptdiagpLeTsHlLc.comment: The diagnostic ongoing health test set for leaf fabric nodes to run on line cards (I/O cards).

eqptdiagpLeTsHlSc.label: Leaf Health Diag Policy Set for the IO Line Card Type
eqptdiagpLeTsHlSc.comment: The ongoing diagnostic health test set for leaf fabric nodes to run on supervisor cards.

eqptdiagpLeTsOdEcc.label: FEX On-demand Diags
eqptdiagpLeTsOdEcc.comment: The diagnostic test set for leaf fabric nodes to run on extended chassis cards (FEXes).

eqptdiagpLeTsOdLc.label: Leaf Linecard On-demand Diags
eqptdiagpLeTsOdLc.comment: The diagnostic on-demand test set for leaf fabric nodes to run on line cards (I/O cards).

eqptdiagpLeTsOdSc.label: Leaf Line Card On-demand
eqptdiagpLeTsOdSc.comment: The diagnostic on-demand test set for leaf fabric nodes to run on supervisor cards.

eqptdiagpLpTsOd.label: On-Demand Diag Policy Set for Specified Leaf Port
eqptdiagpLpTsOd.comment: An on-demand abstract diagnostic policy for leaf ports.

eqptdiagpPol.label: Diag Policy Set for the Specified Card Type
eqptdiagpPol.comment: The abstract diagnostic policy for fabric nodes.

eqptdiagpPortTestSetBt.label: On-Demand Diag Policy Set for the Specified Card Type
eqptdiagpPortTestSetBt.comment: The boot abstract diagnostic policy for ports.

eqptdiagpPortTestSetHl.label: On-Demand Diag Policy Set for the Specified Card Type
eqptdiagpPortTestSetHl.comment: The health abstract diagnostic policy for ports.

eqptdiagpPortTestSetOd.comment: The abstract classes for port-level diagnostic policies.

eqptdiagpRsExtChCardOdDiag.label: Relation to Extended Chassis Module
eqptdiagpRsExtChCardOdDiag.comment: A source relation to the extended chassis. An extended chassis is an extension of the fabric. Note that this relation is an internal object.

eventRecord.label: Event
eventRecord.comment: Represents an immutable object that records a state transition for an event instance object. Record creation is triggered by event:Inst object creation or deletion, or by modification of key properties (for example, severity, lifecycle, or acknowledgement). Because an object of this class is immutable, all properties are set at the time the object is created. A record contains a complete snapshot of the event instance object and is logically organized as a flat list under a single container. The record is meant to be queried using time-based filters and possibly prop-filters for severity, affected DN, or other criteria. Inherited properties, as well as severity (original,highest,previous), acknowledgement, occurrance, and lifecycle provide a snapshot of the event instance. The record object contains properties from the corresponding instance object (event:Inst) as well as other properties that provide a snapshot of the event instance and the nature and time of its change. The transition time, affected object, trigger, action indicator, and record ID, in addition to properties from the instance object, characterize the event record. NOTE: The properties of the event record object must be kept in sync with the properties of the event instance object.

faultARetP.label: Fault Retention Policy
faultARetP.comment: The fault record retention policy specifies the maximum number of fault history records to be retained on the node or controller and the maximum number of fault history records to be deleted in a 30-second period. Note that this is an abstract class and cannot be instantiated.

faultARsToRemote.label: Super Class for Relation used to pull policies to Event Manager
faultARsToRemote.comment: Super class for all relations pulling policies down to event mgr

faultAThrValue.comment: The threshold value container is used between the observer and the eventmgr communication when reporting threshold values. Concrete subclasses are populated during ngen for every numeric data type.

faultCont.comment: A container class for fault instance objects.

faultCounts.label: Fault Counts
faultCounts.comment: An immutable object that provides the number of critical, major, minor, and warning faults raised on its parent object and its subtree.

faultCtrlrRetP.label: Controller Fault Retention Policy
faultCtrlrRetP.comment: The controller fault record retention policy specifies the maximum number of controller fault records to be retained and the maximum number of controller fault records to be deleted in a 30-second period.

faultDelegate.comment: Exposes internal faults to the user. A fault delegate object can be defined on IFC (for example, for an endpoint group) and when the fault is raised (for example, under an endpoint policy on a switch), a fault delegate object is created on IFC under the specified object. A fault delegate object follows the lifecycle of the original fault instance object, being created, modified, or deleted based on the changes of the original fault.

faultDomainCounts.label: Domain Fault Counts
faultDomainCounts.comment: The number of faults for each level of a given domain.

faultEventMgrCont.label: Container for Event Mgr Policies
faultEventMgrCont.comment: Container for per pod policies, owned by AE. This is used internally for pushing policies.

faultInfo.comment: An abnormal condition or defect at the application, component, equipment, or sub-system level that may lead to a failure, as defined in ISO/CD 10303-226. The fault:Inst and fault:Delegate objects inherit their properties from this object.

faultInst.comment: Contains detailed information of a fault. This object is attached as a child of the object on which the fault condition occurred. One instance object is created for each fault condition of the parent object. A fault instance object is identified by a fault code.

faultLcP.label: Lifecycle Policy
faultLcP.comment: The fault lifecycle policy specifies the timer intervals of faults in their lifecycle. This policy can be specified in a common, default, or custom monitoring policy. Custom and default monitoring policies can be applied globally, per monitoring object or to a group of monitoring objects.

faultRecord.label: Fault Record
faultRecord.comment: An immutable object that records a state transition for a fault instance object. Record creation is triggered by fault:Inst object creation or deletion, or by modification of key properties (for example, severity, lifecycle, or acknowledgement). Because an object of this class is immutable, all properties are set at the time the object is created. A record contains a complete snapshot of the fault instance object and is logically organized as a flat list under a single container. The record object contains properties from the corresponding instance object (fault:Inst) such as severity (original, highest, and previous), acknowledgement, occurrence, and lifecycle as well as inherited properties that provide a snapshot of the fault instance and the nature and time of its change. The record is meant to be queried using time-based filters or property filters for severity, affected DN, or other criteria. NOTE: The properties of the fault record object must be kept in sync with the properties of the fault instance object.

faultRsAaaCtrlrRetP.label: Controller Audit Record Retention Policy
faultRsAaaCtrlrRetP.comment: A source relation to the controller audit log retention policy.

faultRsEventCtrlrRetP.label: Controller Event Record Retention Policy
faultRsEventCtrlrRetP.comment: A source relation to the controller event record retention policy.

faultRtNodeFaultRecRetP.label: Historical Record Node Policy Group
faultRtNodeFaultRecRetP.comment: A target relation to the fault record retention policy.

faultRtResFaultSwRetP.label: Fabric Instance

faultSevAsnP.label: Fault Severity Assignment Policy
faultSevAsnP.comment: The initial and target severities for a specific fault can be specified in a custom or default monitoring policy directly under an observable target object.

faultSubj.comment: An internal class used for maintaining internal representation of the fault instance objects.

faultSwRetP.label: Switch Fault Retention Policy
faultSwRetP.comment: Specifies the maximum number of fault records to be retained and the maximum number of fault records to be deleted in a 30-second period. These settings can be changed either by creating a custom policy or editing the default policy.

faultThrValueSint16.comment: The threshold value Service Interface Notification Statistics (SINT) 16.

faultThrValueSint32.comment: The threshold value Service Interface Notification Statistics (SINT) 32.

faultThrValueSint64.comment: The threshold value Service Interface Notification Statistics (SINT) 64.

fcAPinningLbl.label: Abstract Fibre Channel Uplink Pinning Label
fcAPinningLbl.comment: Fiber Channel Pinning Label. Its used for pinning host interfaces to uplink interfaces. This label should match with the name of a fc:PinningP configured this tenant or tenant-commmon. Once this label is configured under a fv:RsFcPathAtt, that host interface will get pinned to the uplink interfaces specified in the Pinning profile.

fvBDHolder.label: Bridge Domain Holder
fvBDHolder.comment: The bridge domain (BD) holder contains bridge domain related information. For example, in a same shared service scenario, when a context is deployed on a node, the PE needs to get all subnets of all the associated BDs. In this case, the private Layer 3 network context contains DNs of all associated BDs, and with that info, the node pulls down the corresponding bridge domain holders of each of the associated BDs. The bridge domain holder contains all subnets of a given BD. This allows node to have some BD information without pulling down entire BD.

fvConnInstrPol.label: Connectivity Instrumentation Policy
fvConnInstrPol.comment: Every endpoint group should have a relation set to its bridge domain. If not set by the user, then the relation is set to the default Bridge Domain and the Connectivity Instrumentation Policy determines whether or not traffic will be allowed to flow to/from that EPG. This applies to all EPGs regardless of use (VMM, baremetal, L2ext, L3ext). There is also a relation from the Bridge Domain to the VRF. If this is not set by the user, then the default VRF is used and the Connectivity Instrumentation Policy determines whether traffic will be allowed to flow within any EPGs using that Bridge Domain.

fvQinqFabEncap.label: QinqFabEncap Refcount
fvQinqFabEncap.comment: In the case of EPG to Port assciation with double encap, since there is no concept of qinq pool and still we support more than one qinq FD,we need to a get a unique fabencap per FD and not per EPG (if we give one Fabencap for all the FDs under that EPG) Ep learning will break and the vpc sync will break) So this is the MO which keeps track of the all the double encaps under a given EPG and which acts like a reference to keep a track of all the qinq double encaps under an EPG and also to keep a refcount of all the FabEncaps.

Override on a per-epg level is possible using protByExclude relations on the EPGrel-def name="CtxProtBy" to="vz:Taboo" cardinality="n-to-m" owner="management" mod="explicit" label="Taboo Contract" /Relationship to RtCtrl Profile

fvRsToCtrct.comment: An internal used for security rule deployment. This is an internal object.

This config pull will be triggered by behavioural code on PE when handling changes on the netflow:MonitorPolHolder Mo We need to bring in the entire subtree since the subtree may contain Exporter and Record Def Mos

haHaTest.comment: High availability (HA)is critical to maximize network and system uptime, thereby meeting predefined SLAs. Each layer in the EttF solution is verified for HA functionality and recovery times. Simulated Layer 3 traffic is traversing end-to-end from the cell/area zone through the manufacturing zone, and from the cell/area zone through the DMZ to the outside world. Various failures are triggered at each layer, and the convergence time is measured to characterize and quantify the impact on availability.

igmpSnoopDef.label: IGMP Snoop Policy
igmpSnoopDef.comment: The process of listening to Internet Group Management Protocol (IGMP) network traffic. The feature allows a network switch to listen in on the IGMP conversation between hosts and routers. By listening to these conversations the switch maintains a map of which links need which IP multicast streams.

igmpSnoopPol.label: IGMP Snoop Policy
igmpSnoopPol.comment: The IGMP snooping policy streamlines multicast traffic handling for VLANs. By examining (snooping) IGMP membership report messages from interested hosts, multicast traffic is limited to the subset of VLAN interfaces on which the hosts reside.

igmpSnoopStaticGroup.label: IGMP Snooping Static Membership configuration
igmpSnoopStaticGroup.comment: Representation of an IGMP snooping static (*,G) or (S,G) membership

igmpSnoopStaticGroupDef.label: IGMP Snooping Static Membership configuration
igmpSnoopStaticGroupDef.comment: In case the static group membership is at the fvAEPg levelFor future releasesInternal representation of the group memebership

igmpStRepP.label: Static Report Policy
igmpStRepP.comment: This object holds static igmp reports policy state per Interface

igmpsnoopACtrlP.label: Access Control Policy
igmpsnoopACtrlP.comment: Route control policy for igmp multicast snoop.

igmpsnoopDb.label: Database
igmpsnoopDb.comment: Represents all the IGMP Snooping database information.

igmpsnoopDom.label: IGMP Snooping Domain
igmpsnoopDom.comment: An IGMP snooping domain, which is a bridge domain (BD) in which IGMP snooping is enabled. When an IGMP querier is in the network, it sends periodic IGMP queries that trigger IGMP report messages from hosts that want to receive IP multicast traffic. IGMP snooping listens to these IGMP reports to establish appropriate forwarding

igmpsnoopDomStats.label: Domain Statistics
igmpsnoopDomStats.comment: Use the **show ip IGMP snooping statistics vlan** command to display IGMP snooping statistics. You can see the virtual port channel (vPC) statistics in this output. Use the **clear ip igmp snooping statistics vlan** command to clear IGMP snooping statistics.

igmpsnoopEntity.label: IGMP Snooping Entity
igmpsnoopEntity.comment: Holds IGMP control plane entity information.

igmpsnoopEpgRec.label: Endpoint Group Record

igmpsnoopInstStats.label: Instance Statistics
igmpsnoopInstStats.comment: Holds per instance statistics operational information. This is a singleton within instance

igmpsnoopMcGrpRec.label: Multicast Group Record

igmpsnoopOIFRec.label: OIF Record

igmpsnoopQuerierP.label: Querier Policy
igmpsnoopQuerierP.comment: When an IGMP snooping querier is enabled on a leaf, it sends out periodic IGMP queries on front panel ports of a BD on that leaf.

infraAccBndlSubgrp.label: Override Policy Group

infraAttEntityP.comment: The attached entity profile provides a template to deploy hypervisor policies on a large set of leaf ports. This also provides the association of a Virtual Machine Management (VMM) domain and the physical network infrastructure.

infraAttPolicyGroup.label: Access Attachable Policy Group
infraAttPolicyGroup.comment: The attachable policy group acts as an override of the policies given at the AccBaseGrp for the ports associated with the Attachable Entity Profile.

infraConnFexBlk.label: Connectivity Fex Block
infraConnFexBlk.comment: A block used for grouping fex for connecting hosts (such as hypervisor).

infraConnFexS.label: Connectivity Fex Selector
infraConnFexS.comment: The Connectivity FEX selector is used for grouping ports between the FEX and the host (such as a hypervisor).

infraConnNodeBlk.label: Connectivity Node Block
infraConnNodeBlk.comment: The Connectivity Node Block is used for grouping nodes for connecting hosts (such as hypervisor).

infraConnNodeS.label: Access Connectivity Node Selector
infraConnNodeS.comment: The connectivity selector is used for grouping ports between the FEX and the host (such as hypervisor).

infraConnPortBlk.label: Connectivity Port Block
infraConnPortBlk.comment: The connectivity port block is used for grouping ports between the node and the host (such as hypervisor).

infraCont.label: Cluster
infraCont.comment: An APIC cluster is comprised of multiple APIC controllers that provide operators with a unified real time monitoring, diagnostic, and configuration management capability for the ACI fabric.

infraContNS.label: Access Container Namespace
infraContNS.comment: An internal object that contains all relations to namespaces.

infraDefaultInterfacePolicies.label: Fabric-Wide Link Level Policies creation status

infraDomP.label: Access Domain Profile
infraDomP.comment: An abstraction of an infrastructure domain profile associated with application endpoint groups. This is used for carving the infra elements including, but not limited to, port, VLAN, etc.

infraDomainToNs.label: Super Class for Each Domain to Namespace Relation
infraDomainToNs.comment: The superclass for all relations from domain to namespace.

infraEPg.label: End Point Group
infraEPg.comment: Abstraction of an infrastructure endpoint group.

infraEncap.label: Encap
infraEncap.comment: The encaps associated with the functions.

infraEncapDef.comment: The encapsulation information. This object is generated and used only by internal processes.

infraEpPD.label: Portgroup
infraEpPD.comment: The endpoint group policy descriptor for infrastructure policies. For example, endpoint groups under tenant infra. This object is generated and used only by internal processes.

infraEpPDDef.label: Endpoint Policy Description
infraEpPDDef.comment: The endpoint group policy descriptor for infrastructure policies. For example, endpoint groups under tenant infra. This object is generated and used only by internal processes.

infraEpPDTask.comment: A task to send an infrastructure endpoint group policy descriptor from one process to another. This is object is generated and used only by internal process

infraExP.comment: An abstraction of an external profile.

infraFcAccBndlGrp.label: Leaf Access FC-PC Policy Group
infraFcAccBndlGrp.comment: Used for representing leaf FC port-channels

infraFcAccBndlPolGrp.label: Leaf Access FC-PC Policy override Group
infraFcAccBndlPolGrp.comment: Used for FC Port Channel overrides

infraFcAccGrp.label: Abstraction of Leaf FC Access Policy Group
infraFcAccGrp.comment: Used for respresenting the leaf FC port functions

infraFcAccPortGrp.label: Leaf Access FC Port Policy Group
infraFcAccPortGrp.comment: Used for representing leaf singular FC ports

infraFexGrp.comment: An abstraction of a FEX policy group.

infraFexP.label: FEX Profile
infraFexP.comment: The FEX profile enables you to configure FEX interfaces.

infraFuncP.label: Function Profile
infraFuncP.comment: The hypervisor management function provides the policies used for hypervisor management and connectivity. For example, an endpoint group and encap VLAN.

infraGeNode.label: Generic Controller
infraGeNode.comment: This object is generated and used only by internal processes.

infraGeneric.label: Access Generic
infraGeneric.comment: The hypervisor generic function provides policies used for any generic hypervisor functionality. This can be used when any other pre-defined functions are not sufficient. The VLAN and endpoint group policies associated with this function are deployed on the leaf ports.

infraHConnPortS.label: Connectivity Port Selector
infraHConnPortS.comment: The host connectivity port selector is used for grouping ports between the node and the host (such as hypervisor).

infraHPortS.label: Access Port Selector
infraHPortS.comment: The Host Port Selector is used for grouping ports between the node and the host (such as hypervisor).

infraIDDef.comment: An internal object for holding ID allocation ranges.

infraIfLblDef.label: Interface Label
infraIfLblDef.comment: The tenant/provider's external connection characteristics of the port.

infraIlClMsgSrc.label: Controller - Source of Illegal Cluster Messages
infraIlClMsgSrc.comment: The source of illegal cluster messages, which are discarded by LoNode.

infraImage.label: Access Image Download
