infraInfra.label: Access Instance
infraInfra.comment: A container for all tenant infra configurations.

infraLbl.comment: The tenant or provider characteristics of the port.

infraLeafS.label: Switch Association
infraLeafS.comment: The leaf selector enables you to select the interface to configure.

infraLoNode.label: Controller Local View
infraLoNode.comment: This object is generated and used only by internal processes

infraLocation.comment: This object is generated and used only by internal processes

infraMgmt.label: Access Management
infraMgmt.comment: The Vcenter management connectivity. The Vcenter management encap is a regular VLAN.

infraNode.label: Node where EPg will be Deployed
infraNode.comment: The node where the endpoint group will be deployed.

infraNodeBlk.label: Node Block
infraNodeBlk.comment: A node block is a range of nodes. Each node block begins with the first port and ends with the last port.

infraNodeCfg.label: Access Node Configuration
infraNodeCfg.comment: An internal object used by APIC to keep track of the infra configuration per node.

infraNodeCfgCont.label: Access Node Configuration Container
infraNodeCfgCont.comment: A container of all objects present in APIC that are used to track infra configurations per node. This is an internal object.

infraNodeDef.label: Node where EPg will be Deployed

infraNodeLblDef.label: Switch Label
infraNodeLblDef.comment: The tenant or provider's external connection characteristics of the port.

infraNodeP.label: Leaf Profile

infraNsIssues.label: Access Namespace Configuration Issues

infraOSpineS.label: Override Spine Selector
infraOSpineS.comment: Override Spine Selector @@@ Its not configurable because theres no spine policy group in infra.

infraPeNode.comment: This object is generated and used only by internal processes.

infraPeReplica.label: Peer Replica
infraPeReplica.comment: This object is generated and used only by internal processes.

infraPodP.label: Pod Profile
infraPodP.comment: Pod Profile: It represents the template used for deploying POD level configuration

infraPortBlk.label: Access Port Block
infraPortBlk.comment: The port block enables you to specify a range of interfaces.

infraPortS.comment: An abstraction of access interface selectors.

infraProfile.comment: An abstraction of the access policy group.

infraProfileIssues.label: Infrastructure Profile Configuration Issues
infraProfileIssues.comment: Infrastructure Profile Configuration Issues. The delegatable class is infra:Profile, which should be a super class of all infra profiles such as Attachable Profile, Node Profile, Port Profile, Function Profile, etc.

infraProvAcc.label: Vlan Encapsulation for Vxlan Traffic
infraProvAcc.comment: The provider access function is defined when the hypervisor is using an encapsulation protocol, such as VxLAN/NVGRE, and provides the policies to impacting VxLAN/NvGRE packets from NVGRE. You do not need to provide the encapsulation VLAN and endpoint for the provider access function. The system uses the Infra VLAN (provided during APIC bootup) as the encapsulation and associates it to the default endpoint group under the default tenant.

infraProvFloatingSvi.label: Floatign L3out Provisioning
infraProvFloatingSvi.comment: Function for ports being used by floating svi physical dom

l1AOpticsZR.label: ZR Optics
l1AOpticsZR.comment: This is the abstract class for QDD-400G-ZR transceiver.

l1AOpticsZRP.label: ZRP Optics
l1AOpticsZRP.comment: This is the abstract class for QDD-400G-ZRP transceiver.

l1EthIf.label: Ethernet Interface
l1EthIf.comment: An abstraction of Ethernet interface properties that are common between Ethernet physical interfaces and aggregated interfaces.

l1EthIfSetInServiceLTask.comment: A task object to put a port in-service (allow it to forward data traffic) or out-of-service (prevent it from forwarding any data traffic).

l1EthIfSetInServiceRslt.comment: An internal object containing the result of the task that allows/prevents a port from forwarding data.

l1FcIf.label: Fiber Channel Interface
l1FcIf.comment: Abstraction for a l1 fiber channel interface in the system. The properties apply to all types of fiber channel interfaces

l1If.label: Interface
l1If.comment: An abstraction for a layer-1 interface in the system. The properties apply to all types of interfaces.

l1LinkLevelFlowCtrlP.label: Link Level flow Control Policy
l1LinkLevelFlowCtrlP.comment: MO for LLFC mode. This is a child policy of if.

l1ObservedEthIf.label: Process Node Attachment
l1ObservedEthIf.comment: Internal MO used to associate with all the Leaf/Fex EthIfs present on the node. Whoever associates with this MO will start getting reported healthscores from all the Leaf/Fex EthIfs present on this node.

l1OpticsBase.label: Optics base class
l1OpticsBase.comment: This is the base class for Optics related information.

l1PhysIf.label: Layer 1 Physical Interface Configuration
l1PhysIf.comment: The object that represents the Layer 1 physical Ethernet interface information object.

l1PhysIfClearCountersLTask.comment: A task object to clear counters of a port.

l1PhysIfClearCountersRslt.comment: An internal object that contains the result of task that clears a port counter.

l1PhysIfLocateLTask.comment: A task object to set a beacon or port-locator LED to on or off for a port.

l1PhysIfLocateRslt.comment: An internal object that contains the result of the task that sets a beacon or port-locator LED to on or off for a port.

l1PhysIfResetLTask.comment: A task object to reset a port. i.e. stimulate a port shut-no-shut action

l1PhysIfResetRslt.comment: An internal object that contains the result of the task that resets a port.

l1PrioFlowCtrlP.label: Priority flow Control Policy
l1PrioFlowCtrlP.comment: MO for slow-drain policy and PFC mode. This is a child policy of if.

l1ProcessNodeAtt.label: Process Node Attachment
l1ProcessNodeAtt.comment: Mo for indicating that the port needs to be processed for Node Attachment

l1RsCdpIfPolCons.label: CDP Policy Attachment
l1RsCdpIfPolCons.comment: A source relation to CDP interface policy parameters. Note that this relation is an internal object.

l1RsCoppIfPolCons.label: coppIf Policy Attachment
l1RsCoppIfPolCons.comment: Relationship with the coppIf policy consumed by this physical interface

l1RsFIfPolCons.label: FIF Policy Attachment
l1RsFIfPolCons.comment: Relationship with the FIfPol policy consumed by this physical interface

l1RsFcIfPolCons.label: FC Policy Attachment
l1RsFcIfPolCons.comment: Relationship with the fcIfPol policy consumed by this physical interface

l1RsHIfPolCons.label: HIF Policy Attachment
l1RsHIfPolCons.comment: A source relation to the host interface policy. Note that this relation is an internal object.

l1RsIpv4MonitorPolCons.label: Ipv4 Monitor Policy Attachment
l1RsIpv4MonitorPolCons.comment: Relationship with the Monitor policy consumed by this physical interface

l1RsL2PortAuthCons.label: L2 PortAuth Policy Attachment
l1RsL2PortAuthCons.comment: Relationship with the Port Authentication policy consumed by this physical interface

l1RsL2PortSecurityCons.label: L2 PortSecurity Policy Attachment
l1RsL2PortSecurityCons.comment: Relationship with the Port Security policy consumed by this physical interface

l1RsL3IfPolCons.label: L3 Interface Policy Attachment
l1RsL3IfPolCons.comment: Relationship with the L3 interface policy consumed by this physical interface

l1RsLacpIfPolCons.label: LACP Policy Attachment
l1RsLacpIfPolCons.comment: A source relation to the port-Level LACP member policy. Note that this relation is an internal object.

l1RsLldpIfPolCons.label: LLDP Policy Attachment
l1RsLldpIfPolCons.comment: A source relation to the LLDP policy parameters for the interface. Note that this relation is an internal object.

l1RsMacsecPolCons.label: MACSEC Policy Attachment
l1RsMacsecPolCons.comment: Relationship with the cdp/lacp/lldp if policy consumed by this physical interface

l1RsMonPolIfPolCons.label: Monitoring Policy Attachment
l1RsMonPolIfPolCons.comment: A source relation to the base monitoring policy model. Note that this relation is an internal object.

l1RsNfcIfPolCons.label: Native FC Interface Policy Attachment
l1RsNfcIfPolCons.comment: Relationship with the fc Interface policy consumed by this physical interface

l1RsPoeIfPolCons.label: POE Policy Attachment
l1RsPoeIfPolCons.comment: Relationship with the poe policy consumed by this physical interface

l1RsQosEgressDppIfPolCons.label: QOS Dpp Policy Attachment Egress
l1RsQosEgressDppIfPolCons.comment: Relationship with the qosDppPol consumed by the physical interface in Egress

l1RsQosIngressDppIfPolCons.label: QOS Dpp Policy Attachment Ingress
l1RsQosIngressDppIfPolCons.comment: Relationship with the qosDppPol consumed by the physical interface in Ingress

l1RsQosLlfcIfPolCons.label: QoS LLFC Policy Attachment
l1RsQosLlfcIfPolCons.comment: Relationship with the qosLlfcPol policy consumed by this physical interface

l1RsQosPfcIfPolCons.label: QoS PFC Policy Attachment
l1RsQosPfcIfPolCons.comment: Relationship with the qosPfcPol policy consumed by this physical interface

l1RsQosSdIfPolCons.label: QoS Slow Drain Policy Attachment
l1RsQosSdIfPolCons.comment: Relationship with the SdIfPol policy consumed by this physical interface

l1RsStpIfPolCons.label: STP Policy Attachment
l1RsStpIfPolCons.comment: A source relation to the Spanning-Tree Protocol (STP) policy. Note that this relation is an internal object.

l1RsSynceEthIfPolCons.label: Synce Policy Attachment
l1RsSynceEthIfPolCons.comment: Relationship with the synce policy consumed by this physical interface

l1RsXcvrOpticsFabIfPolCons.label: Fabric Transceiver Optics Policy Attachment
l1RsXcvrOpticsFabIfPolCons.comment: Relationship with the xcvrOpticsFabIfPol policy consumed by this physical interface if fabric port

l1RsXcvrOpticsIfPolCons.label: Transceiver Optics Policy Attachment
l1RsXcvrOpticsIfPolCons.comment: Relationship with the xcvrOpticsIfPol policy consumed by this physical interface if access port

l1RtEncPhysRtdConf.label: Encapsulated Routed Interface
l1RtEncPhysRtdConf.comment: A target relation to an abstraction of Ethernet interface properties that are common between Ethernet physical and aggregated interfaces. Note that this relation is an internal object.

l1RtEthIf.comment: A target relation to an abstraction of Ethernet interface properties that are common between Ethernet physical and aggregated interfaces. Note that this relation is an internal object.

l1RtLabelEpToAllowedEthIf.comment: Associations to the allowed interfaces for the label

l1RtLabelEpToConsumerEthIf.comment: Associations to the interfaces consuming the label

l1capRule.label: L1 Capability Rule
l1capRule.comment: An L1 capability rule. This property defines hardware or software capabilities of the system.

l2ADom.label: Domain

l2AnchorBD.label: External Bridge domain in anchoring node

l2BD.comment: The Layer 2 Bridge-domain identifies the boundary of a tenant’s bridged/layer 2 traffic. This is similar to a VLAN in a traditional layer 2 switched network.

l2BDClearEpLTask.comment: The task to clear all endpoints under a given bridge domain.

l2BDClearEpRslt.comment: The result of the task to clear all endpoints under a given bridge domain.

l2BDExtn.label: Bridge Domain Extension

l2CktEpExtn.label: Circuit Endpoint Extension
l2CktEpExtn.comment: Abstraction of a circuit endpoint extension

l2Cons.label: Consumer Reference
l2Cons.comment: Used for reference counting the consumers corresponding to the RsPathDomAtt relation. Requirement from switch team: If an circuit is associated to port-x, then the BD should also get associated with the same port. This managed object is used for reference counting the circuit endpoints that need the BD-to-port association.

l2Dom.label: Domain
l2Dom.comment: A generic layer 2 flood domain is independent of such technology as 802.1q.

l2DomMbrIf.label: Domain Member Interface
l2DomMbrIf.comment: The layer to the attachment to the bridge interface.

l2EncapRef.label: Encap Reference
l2EncapRef.comment: An encap reference. This is used for referencing current user of an encap.

l2EncapUni.label: Encap Universe Definition
l2EncapUni.comment: An encap universe: one per node. This is a container of the encap references.

l2EpRetPol.label: Endpoint Retention Policy
l2EpRetPol.comment: An endpoint retention policy identifying the duration of how long information is kept about the remote endpoint. This is expressed through a set of triggering and ageing control parameters.

l2FcAggrIf.label: San Port Channel Interface
l2FcAggrIf.comment: A san port channel interface properties

l2FcIf.label: Fibre Channel Interface
l2FcIf.comment: Abstraction for a fiber channel interface in the system. The properties apply to all types of fiber channel interfaces

l2If.label: Interface
l2If.comment: The layer 2 configured interface allows traffic of multiple VLANs over a single link and also extends VLANs across the network. All Layer 2 switching ports maintain MAC address tables.

l2InstPol.label: Fabric L2 MTU Policy
l2InstPol.comment: The Layer 2 instance policy is used for configuring fabric-wide layer 2 settings. Currently, this policy contains only fabric MTU and management MTU configuration.

l2InstPolDef.label: Fabric L2 MTU Policy
l2InstPolDef.comment: A read-only copy of the fabric MTU policy.

l2LPort.label: Local Port
l2LPort.comment: The holder of nxosmock-specific information of the port.

l2MpBd.label: MultiPod Bridge Domain property
l2MpBd.comment: Layer2 Bridge-domain more properties used in multipod caseNot added to BD mo because in single pod case these new added properties are not used.

l2RogueExpMac.label: MAC address for Rogue Exception List

l2RsBrConf.label: Eth Interface Attachment
l2RsBrConf.comment: A source relation to an L1 interface.

l2RsDot1pRuleAtt.label: Dot1p Rule Attachment
l2RsDot1pRuleAtt.comment: A source relation to the set of Dot1P rules. This is an internal object.

l2RsDscpRuleAtt.label: DSCP Rule Attachment
l2RsDscpRuleAtt.comment: A source relation to a set of DSCP rules. This is an internal object.

l2RsEthIf.label: Ethernet Interface
l2RsEthIf.comment: A source relation to an L1 interface. This is an internal object.

l2RsExtBD.label: External BD Attachment
l2RsExtBD.comment: A source relation to the interface connectivity. This is an internal object.

l2RsExtConf.label: Eth Interface Attachment
l2RsExtConf.comment: A source relation to an L1 interface. This is an internal object

l2RsInbandConf.label: Inband Attachment
l2RsInbandConf.comment: A source relation to an L1 Ethernet interface. This is an internal object.

l2RsPathDomAtt.label: Port Domain Attachment
l2RsPathDomAtt.comment: A source relation to the ports on this domain is allowed. This is an internal object.

l2RtAeCtrlrL2InstPol.label: Relation Holder
l2RtAeCtrlrL2InstPol.comment: A target relation to L2 instance policy information. This is an internal object.

l2RtDefaultL2InstPol.label: VMM Domain
l2RtDefaultL2InstPol.comment: A target relation to L2 instance policy information. This is an internal object.

l2RtDomIfConn.label: FC Interface Connectivity
l2RtDomIfConn.comment: A target relation to the layer 2 flood domain.

l2RtEPgDefToL2Dom.comment: A target relation to the layer 2 flood domain.

l2RtL2IfPol.label: Abstraction of Leaf Access Policy Group

l2RtL2InstPol.label: Abstraction of Leaf Access Policy Group
l2RtL2InstPol.comment: A target relation to a policy providing physical configuration of the interfaces. This is an internal object.

l2RtL2PortAuthCons.label: Ethernet Interface
l2RtL2PortAuthCons.comment: Relationship with the Port Authentication policy consumed by this physical interface

l2RtL2PortAuthPol.label: Abstraction of Leaf Access Policy Group

l2RtL2PortSecurityCons.label: Ethernet Interface
l2RtL2PortSecurityCons.comment: Relationship with the Port Security policy consumed by this physical interface

l2RtL2PortSecurityPol.label: Abstraction of Leaf Access Policy Group
l2RtL2PortSecurityPol.comment: Relationship to policy providing L2 PortSecurity configuration

l2RtLabelL2IfPolCons.label: Network Label Endpoint
l2RtLabelL2IfPolCons.comment: Relationship with the L2 interface policy consumed by this label

l2RtResL2InstPol.label: Fabric Instance
l2RtResL2InstPol.comment: A target relation to the layer 2 instance policy information. This is an internal object.

l2RtSpanSrcToL2CktEpAtt.label: Fabric Span Source
l2RtSpanSrcToL2CktEpAtt.comment: A target relation to the set of circuits. This is an internal object.

l2RtSysL2NodePolAuthCons.label: System
l2RtSysL2NodePolAuthCons.comment: Relationship to 802.1x policy consumed by this node

l2RtVswitchOverrideMtuPol.label: VSwitch Policy Group
l2RtVswitchOverrideMtuPol.comment: Relationship to policy providing physical configuration of the interfaces

l2capProv.label: L2 Capability Provider
l2capProv.comment: An L1 capability provider that consumes a capability rule and tracks the software or hardware capabilities of the system. For example, this property provides you total and remaining count on the leaf.

l2capRule.label: L2 Capability Rule
l2capRule.comment: An l2 capability rule. This property defines hardware or software capabilities of the system.

l2extADomP.label: Abstraction of Outside Domain Profile
l2extADomP.comment: The abstraction of the external routed domain profile is a policy for managing the physical infrastructure, such as ports/VLANS, that can be used by an L3 routed outside network.

l2extAIfP.label: Abstraction of Interface Profile
l2extAIfP.comment: The abstraction of an interface profile.

l2extAInstPSubnet.label: Abstraction of External Network Instance
l2extAInstPSubnet.comment: An abstraction of an external network instance.

l2extALNodeP.label: Abstraction of Logical Node Profile
l2extALNodeP.comment: An abstract logical node profile. This defines the characteristics to be applied to resources that match with the profile name.

l2extDomDef.label: L2 Domain Profile Definition
l2extDomDef.comment: This is generated and used only by internal processes.

l2extDomP.label: L2 Domain Profile
l2extDomP.comment: The external bridged domain profile is a policy for managing the physical infrastructure, such as ports/VLANS, that can be used by an L2 network bridged outside the fabric.

l2extInstP.label: External Network Instance Profile
l2extInstP.comment: The external network instance profile represents a group of external subnets that have the same security behavior. These subnets inherit contract profiles applied to the parent instance profile. Each subnet can also associate to route control profiles, which defines the routing behavior for that subnet.

l2extLIfP.label: Logical Interface Profile
l2extLIfP.comment: The logical interface profile defines a common configuration that can be applied to one or more interfaces.

l2extLIfPDef.label: Logical Interface Profile Definition
l2extLIfPDef.comment: The interface identifiers attached to the node profile.

l2extLNodeP.label: Logical Node Profile
l2extLNodeP.comment: The logical node profile defines a common configuration that can be applied to one or more leaf nodes.

l2extLNodePDef.label: Logical Node Profile Definition
l2extLNodePDef.comment: The logical node profile definition. This defines the characteristics to be applied to resources that match with the profile name.

l2extOut.label: L2 Outside
l2extOut.comment: The L2 outside policy controls connectivity to the outside.

l2extRsEBd.label: Bridge Domain
l2extRsEBd.comment: A source relation to the private layer 2 bridge domain.

l2extRsL2DomAtt.label: Domain Profile
l2extRsL2DomAtt.comment: A source relation to the external bridged domain profile.

l2extRsL2InstPToDomP.label: Domain Profile
l2extRsL2InstPToDomP.comment: A source relation to the external bridged domain profile.

l2extRsPathDefL2OutAtt.label: Leaf Port
l2extRsPathDefL2OutAtt.comment: The object used to create a static association with a leaf host port. The existence of this object implies that the corresponding set of policies will be resolved into the node pointed to by the relationship.

l2extRtL2DomAtt.label: L2 Outside
l2extRtL2DomAtt.comment: A target relation to the external bridged domain profile.

l2extRtL2InstPToDomP.label: External Network Instance Profile
l2extRtL2InstPToDomP.comment: A target relation to the external bridged domain profile.

l3Cons.label: Consumer Reference
l3Cons.comment: Represents a consumer of a resource.

l3Ctx.label: Context
l3Ctx.comment: The tenant context information is equivalent to a virtual routing and forwarding (VRF) instance created for the tenant's L3 network. Similar to a VRF in traditional Cisco routers, the tenant context isolates the IP addresses of the tenant, allowing different tenants to have overlapping IP addresses.

l3CtxClearEpLTask.comment: The task to clear all endpoints under a given bridge domain.

l3CtxClearEpRslt.comment: The result of the task to clear all endpoints under a given bridge domain.

l3CtxExtn.label: Context Extension
l3CtxExtn.comment: Tenant context extension in a Multi pod case

l3CtxSubstitute.label: Context
l3CtxSubstitute.comment: Tenant context object substitute. Its needed for internal PE purposes but it doesnt instantiate any VRF in the node.

l3DomMbrIf.label: Domain Member Interface
l3DomMbrIf.comment: The runtime/operational state for interfaces maintained by VRF management processes. This is an internal object that tracks the state used by the nxos process for the operations.

l3EgrBytes.label: current mpls vrf egress bytes stats
l3EgrBytes.comment: Egress byte. A super class that represents the most current statistics for mpls vrf egress bytes.

l3EgrBytes15min.label: current mpls vrf egress bytes stats in 15 minute

l3EncRtdIf.label: Encapsulated Routed Interface
l3EncRtdIf.comment: The routed interface corresponds to a sub-interface in Cisco’s routing terminology. A sub-interface is a logical L3 interface created on an underlying physical L3 port (the parent interface). Each sub-interface is associated with an 802.1Q VLAN tag. The traffic that comes on the parent interface with that tag is considered for that sub-interface. The existence of a routed interface under a VRF or infra VRF also implies that the interface belongs to that VRF. An L3 interface can only belong to one VRF at a time.

l3ProtDbRec.label: Protocol Database Record
l3ProtDbRec.comment: A layer 3 protocol database record abstraction.

l3RsCtxToEpP.label: Epp Attachment
l3RsCtxToEpP.comment: A source relation to an abstract representation of the resolvable endpoint profile. This is an internal object.

l3RsEncPhysRtdConf.label: Parent If
l3RsEncPhysRtdConf.comment: A source relation to an abstraction of Ethernet interface properties that are common between Ethernet physical interfaces and aggregated interfaces. This is an internal object.

l3RsL3dot1pRuleAtt.label: Dot1p Rule Attachment
l3RsL3dot1pRuleAtt.comment: Relation to the set of Dot1P rules

l3RsL3dscpRuleAtt.label: DSCP Rule Attachment
l3RsL3dscpRuleAtt.comment: Relation to the set of DSCP rules

l3RsLbIfToLocale.label: Router Id
l3RsLbIfToLocale.comment: A loopback created for the router ID. This relation is un-enforced because the code tries to access the real-time monitoring tool.

l3RsPhysRtdConf.label: Physical Routed Attachment
l3RsPhysRtdConf.comment: A source relation to an abstraction of Ethernet interface properties that are common between Ethernet physical interfaces and aggregated interfaces. This is an internal object.

l3RsProtLbIf.label: Protection Group
l3RsProtLbIf.comment: A loopback created for protection groups.

l3RtEPgDefToL3Dom.comment: A target relation to the layer 3 domain (VRF).

l3RtL3IfPolCons.label: Ethernet Interface
l3RtL3IfPolCons.comment: Relationship with the L3 interface policy consumed by this physical interface

l3RtPseudoIf.label: DHCP Client Interface
l3RtPseudoIf.comment: A target relation to the abstract layer 3 interface. If the mode is pseudo interface, the list of all interfaces the DHCP requests is flooded.

l3RtTenConn.label: Access Control Scope
l3RtTenConn.comment: A target relation to the tenant context. This is an internal object.

l3RtUserCtx.label: Abstract Class for Export Policy
l3RtUserCtx.comment: A target relation to tenant context information. The tenant context is equivalent to a virtual routing and forwarding (VRF) instance created for the tenant's L3 network. Similar to a VRF in traditional Cisco routers, the tenant context isolates the IP addresses of the tenant, allowing different tenants to have overlapping IP addresses.

l3RtdIf.label: Routed Interface
l3RtdIf.comment: A target relation to the routed concrete interface. This corresponds to a physical L3 interface. The existence of a routed concrete interface under a VRF or infra VRF also implies that interface belongs to that VRF. An L3 interface can belong to only one VRF at a time.

l3SiteCont.label: Site Container for vrf in mixed mode

l3capProv.label: L3 Capability Provider
l3capProv.comment: An L1 capability provider that consumes a capability rule and tracks the software or hardware capabilities of the system. For example, this property provides you total and remaining count on the leaf.

l3capRule.label: L3 Capability Rule
l3capRule.comment: An L3 capability rule, which defines hardware or software capabilities of the system.

l3extAConsLbl.label: Abstraction of External Connectivity Consumer Label
l3extAConsLbl.comment: Represents Abstraction of Logical Outside Profile Consumer Label. Defines the characteristics that will be applied to Layer3 Outside that matches with the label name

l3extADomP.label: Abstraction of Outside Domain Profile
l3extADomP.comment: The abstraction of the external routed domain profile, which is a policy for managing the physical infrastructure, such as ports/VLANS, that can be used by an L3 routed outside network.

l3extAExtEncapAllocator.comment: An internal object for managing encap allocation for an external SVI.

l3extAFabricExtRoutingP.label: Abstraction of Fabric External Routing Profile
l3extAFabricExtRoutingP.comment: Abstraction of Fabric External Routing Profile

l3extAIfP.label: Abstraction of Logical Interface Profile
l3extAIfP.comment: An abstract interface profile. This encapsulates common behavior / configuration that will apply to one or more L3 external interfaces.

l3extAInstPSubnet.label: Abstraction of External Network Instance
l3extAInstPSubnet.comment: An abstract logical node profile. This defines the characteristics to be applied to resources that match with the profile name.

l3extAIp.comment: The abstraction of a secondary IP address.

l3extALNodeP.label: Abstraction of Logical Node Profile
l3extALNodeP.comment: An abstract logical node profile. This defines the characteristics to be applied to resources that match with the profile name.

l3extAMember.label: Abstraction of Member Node Configuration
l3extAMember.comment: The member. The member is used for providing per node IP address configuration.

l3extAOutRefSrcCont.comment: Abstraction of Container for L3 Out Policy Sources ( L3 OutsRef )

l3extAProvLbl.label: Abstraction of External Connectivity Provider Label
l3extAProvLbl.comment: Represents Abstraction of Logical Outside Profile Provider Label. Defines the characteristics that will be applied to Layer3 Outside that matches with the label name

l3extBdProfileEncapAllocator.label: Ext Bd Group Encap
l3extBdProfileEncapAllocator.comment: External Grouped BD Encap Id Allocator

l3extDomDef.label: Outside L3 Domain

l3extDomP.label: L3 Domain Profile
l3extDomP.comment: The external routed domain profile is a policy for managing the physical infrastructure, such as ports/VLANS, that can be used by an L3 routed outside network.

l3extEigrpPolSrc.comment: Ospf Role Policy Source L3 Out(Ref)

l3extEigrpPolSrcCont.comment: Per Adress Family Container for EIGRP Policy Sources ( L3 OutsRef )

l3extEncapLocaleContext.comment: The internal context used by tasks of the fabric node representation where the extended encap is present.

l3extEncapRequestor.comment: Represents an Out in a given Context requesting encap

l3extException.label: Subnet Exception
l3extException.comment: The exception from the external subnet.

l3extFailedRtdOutDef.label: Represents a Failed RtdOutDef or its Child

l3extFailedRtdOutDefCont.label: Container for Tracking Failed RtdOutDefs
l3extFailedRtdOutDefCont.comment: Internal MO used for tracking config-failed RtdOutDef/Path-Bindings etc.

l3extInfraOutExp.label: Infra Outside policy Export

l3extInstP.comment: The external network instance profile represents a group of external subnets that have the same security behavior. These subnets inherit contract profiles applied to the parent instance profile. Each subnet can also associate to route control profiles, which defines the routing behavior for that subnet.

l3extIntersiteLoopBackIfPDef.label: Intersite Loop Back Interface Profile
l3extIntersiteLoopBackIfPDef.comment: Intersite Loop Back Interface Profile Definition

l3extIpDef.label: Secondary IP address definition
l3extIpDef.comment: The definition of a secondary IP address policy.

l3extLIfP.label: Logical Interface Profile
l3extLIfP.comment: The logical interface profile, which defines a common configuration that can be applied to one or more interfaces.

l3extLIfPDef.label: Logical Interface Profile
l3extLIfPDef.comment: The interface identifiers attached to the node profile.

l3extLNodeP.label: Logical Node Profile
l3extLNodeP.comment: The logical node profile defines a common configuration that can be applied to one or more leaf nodes.

l3extLNodePDef.label: Logical Node Profile
l3extLNodePDef.comment: The logical node profile definition. This defines the characteristics to be applied to resources that match with the profile name.

l3extMember.label: Member Node Configuration
l3extMember.comment: The member. The member is used for providing per node IP address configuration.

l3extMemberDef.label: Member Node Configuration Definition
l3extMemberDef.comment: The member definition. The member is used for providing per node IP address configuration. This is used internally.

l3extRtdNodeDef.comment: Represents a fabric node where this EPP is present @@@ Any configuration contained by Locale which is part of @@@ dynamic attach/detach should not be deleted in doCleanup

l3extRtdOutDefRef.comment: Reference to the Routed Out Definition associated with this RtdEpP. Existence of this mo under RtdEpP is also used as an indicator that the RtdEpP is version 2 (i.e. pruned) RtdEpP.

l3extSubnet.label: Subnet
l3extSubnet.comment: The network visibility of the domain.

l3extSubnetDef.label: Abstraction of External Network Instance
l3extSubnetDef.comment: An internal object that represents subnets defined under an L3 outside.

l3vmCtx.label: Vrf Entity
l3vmCtx.comment: This objects hold runtime information on l3vm context

l3vmEntity.label: L3VM Entity

l3vmInst.label: L3VM Instance

l3vmTbl.label: Table Entity

lacpLagPol.comment: The PortChannel policy enables you to bundle several physical ports together to form a single port channel. LACP enables a node to negotiate an automatic bundling of links by sending LACP packets to the peer node.

latencyOdEntry.label: Abstract Latency Entry Stats
latencyOdEntry.comment: Container MO for on-demand latency stats

latencyPath.label: On-Going Path Latency data
latencyPath.comment: MOs containing latency data for path including VPC

lbpPol.label: Load Balance Policy
lbpPol.comment: The load balancing policy options for balancing traffic among the available uplink ports. Static hash load balancing is the traditional load balancing mechanism used in networks where each flow is allocated to an uplink based on a hash of its 5-tuple. This load balancing gives a distribution of flows across the available links that is roughly even. Usually, with a large number of flows, the even distribution of flows results in an even distribution of bandwidth as well. However, if a few flows are much larger than the rest, static load balancing might give suboptimal results.

lbpRtResLbPol.label: Fabric Instance
lbpRtResLbPol.comment: A target relation to the dynamic load balancing policy.

leakContDef.label: Leak Container Def per shard for Route Leak
leakContDef.comment: Resolved Route leak model sectionThis MO is created for each Shard and hold the RouteFromToCont information that need to be configured

leakInternalSubnet.label: Inter-VRF Leaked Subnet (EPG/BD in case of APIC, cloudSubnet/CloudCidr in case of CAPIC)

leakRouteCont.label: Implicit Route Container Mo Used to Keep track of the Routes to be leaked in the VRF
leakRouteCont.comment: This MO is created under the "from fvCtxDef" with a reference to the "from fvCtxDef" and contain the list of routes to be leaked when using Shared Services and ESg.

leakRouteExtPfx.label: Implicit External Subnet to be Leaked

leakRouteFromToCont.label: Implicit Route FromTo Container Mo Used to Keep track of the Routes to be leaked in the VRF
leakRouteFromToCont.comment: This MO is created under the leakContDef and describe the route leak for each To/From CtxDef pair. When the leakTo configuration is created, the to CtxDef might not have been created/configured yet. This Mo holds the information until the correspondent CtxDef is created

leakRoutes.label: Inter-VRF Leaked Routes Container
leakRoutes.comment: Logical Route leak Model sectionThis MO contain a list of routes to be leaked when using Shared Services and ESg. With the ESg construct, there is no automatic route leaking based on contract. Explicit route leaking is needed.

leakRtRouteToLeakRouteIntPfx.label: Static Route
leakRtRouteToLeakRouteIntPfx.comment: Relation to the leakRouteIntPfx, which is used to ensure that a route is reference counted when coming from an explicit route-map for leak services, configured by the user

leakTo.label: Tenant and VRF destination for Inter-VRF Leaked Routes

leqptLooseNodeTask.comment: An internal task object used by an unmanaged node.

leqptRsLsAttLink.label: Unmanaged Fabric Node
leqptRsLsAttLink.comment: An internal relation for policy resolution.

leqptRsLsNodeToIf.label: Ethernet Interface
leqptRsLsNodeToIf.comment: A source relation to an abstraction of Ethernet interface properties that are common between Ethernet physical interfaces and aggregated interfaces.

leqptRtLsNodeAtt.label: EPG to Path Attachment
leqptRtLsNodeAtt.comment: A target relation to the external unmanaged node.

leqptRtTunnelToLooseNode.label: Tunnel Interface
leqptRtTunnelToLooseNode.comment: A target relation to the external unmanaged node.

licenseApicNode.comment: APIC controller node class. Information is obtained from topomgr inventory.

licenseBaseNode.comment: Abstract base class for keeping apic, leaf and spine node information, which is obtained from topomgr inventory.

licenseCatalog.label: Catalog
licenseCatalog.comment: Catalog class is a singleton class that contains license catalog MOs.

licenseConsumed.label: Entitlement Consumed
licenseConsumed.comment: Consumed class represents a license entitlement consumed. This class is used internally for application team to report license consumption and release. Consumed MO contains ConsumedInst that represents an individual license consumed for the entitlement.

licenseConsumedAppInst.label: Plugin Application License Entitlement
licenseConsumedAppInst.comment: ConsumedAppInst class represents a license entitlement for a specific dynamic plugin application. The license count denotes the count of licenses consumed.

licenseConsumedEPsInst.comment: For cAPIC, license is counted based on EP numbers for each leaf.

licenseConsumedInst.label: Entitlement Instance Consumed
licenseConsumedInst.comment: ConsumedInst class represents an individual license consumed for a specific entitlement. If five ConsumedInst MOs are created, it indicates five licenses are consumed or in use for a specific entitlement.

licenseCont.label: License Cont
licenseCont.comment: Cont class is a container class that holds license related MOs in LicenseMgr.

licenseEntitlement.label: Entitlement
licenseEntitlement.comment: Entitlement class is to model license usage for a specific entitlement. Entilement class will be exposed to end user for users to see license usage per entitlement.

licenseEntitlementDef.label: Entitlement Definition
licenseEntitlementDef.comment: EntitlementDef is to model the definition of an entitlement.

licenseEntitlementDetail.label: Entitlement Detail
licenseEntitlementDetail.comment: EntitlementDetail class is to model license usage for a specific entitlement per device node. EntitlementDetail class is read-only, allowing a user to see the license consumption details per device node.

licenseEvaluation.label: Evaluation
licenseEvaluation.comment: Evaluation class is to model license evaluation mode. Evaluation mode is a temporary mode (30 days) before a user registers product or installs permanent license.

licenseFeatureEntitlement.label: Entitlement Definition
licenseFeatureEntitlement.comment: Feature Entitlement is to model the definition of an entitlement.

licenseHolder.label: License Holder
licenseHolder.comment: Holder class is a container class that holds license:Consumed and license:ConsumedInst MOs reported by application teams.

licenseLeafNode.comment: Leaf node class. Information is obtained from topomgr inventory.

licenseLicPolicy.label: License Policy
licenseLicPolicy.comment: LicPolicy class represents license policy encapsulates user's configuration data used in smart licensing management. Because user's configuration data shall support export/import, the owner of LicensePolicy is PM.

licenseManager.label: License Manager
licenseManager.comment: Manager class represents a remote smart license manager.

licensePermLicReserve.label: Permanent License Reservation
licensePermLicReserve.comment: PermLicReserve is to model a permanent license reservation (PLR).

licenseProductProvider.label: Product Provider
licenseProductProvider.comment: ProductProvider is to model a product for smart licensing.

licenseSpineNode.comment: Spine node class. Information is obtained from topomgr inventory.

lldpAIfPol.label: Abstraction of LLDP Interface Policy
lldpAIfPol.comment: A summary of the interface policy. We recommend you include information about where and when the policy should be used. The abstraction can be up to 128 characters.

lldpAdjEp.label: LLDP Adjacency Endpoint
lldpAdjEp.comment: The LLDP neighbors, which contains the information regarding the neighbors.

lldpAdjStats.label: Adjacency Statistics
lldpAdjStats.comment: The LLDP adjacency statistics operational information. This is a singleton within an adjacency.

lldpCtrlrAdjEp.label: Controller Adjacency
lldpCtrlrAdjEp.comment: The controller adjacency EP information, which is used by PE to cache. Controller adjEP information handles adjacency deletes.

lldpEntity.label: LLDP Entity
lldpEntity.comment: LLDP allows network devices to advertise information about themselves to other devices on the network. This protocol runs over the data-link layer, which allows two systems running different network layer protocols to learn about each other.

lldpIf.label: LLDP Interface
lldpIf.comment: The LLDP interface, which holds the operational states.

lldpIfPol.label: LLDP Interface Policy
lldpIfPol.comment: The LLDP interface policy, which defines a common configuration that will apply to one or more LLDP interfaces. LLDP uses the logical link control (LLC) services to transmit and receive information to and from other LLDP agents.

lldpIfPolDef.label: LLDP Interface Policy Definition
lldpIfPolDef.comment: Read-only copy of the LLDP interface policy.

lldpInst.label: LLDP Instance
lldpInst.comment: Link Layer Discovery Protocol (LLDP) supports a set of attributes that it uses to discover neighbor devices. These attributes contain type, length, and value descriptions and are referred to as TLVs. LLDP supported devices can use TLVs to receive and send information to their neighbors. Details such as configuration information, device capabilities, and device identity can be advertised using this protocol.

maintAMaintP.label: Abstract Maintenance Policy
maintAMaintP.comment: The maintenance policy specifies when and how maintenance takes place.

maintCatMaintP.comment: The catalog maintenance policy specifies when catalog maintenance takes place.

maintCatUpgJob.comment: Specification for catalog policy enforcement status, which exists on every node where the policy is enforced.

maintMaintGrp.label: POD Maintenance Group
maintMaintGrp.comment: The maintenance group is a set of nodes to which a maintenance policy may be applied. The maintenance policy determines the pre-defined action to take when there is a disruptive change made to the service profile associated with the node group.

maintMaintP.label: Maintenance Policy
maintMaintP.comment: The maintenance policy determines the pre-defined action to take when there is a disruptive change made to the service profile associated with the node or set of nodes.

maintMaintPOnD.label: On-Demand Maintenance Policy
maintMaintPOnD.comment: The On Demand Maintenance policy specification for the node, which describes when and how maintenance takes place.

maintMaintTrig.label: Maintenance Triggerable
maintMaintTrig.comment: Triggerable object on which the scheduler triggers a callback for maintenance.

maintNodeInMaint.label: Node in Maintenance
maintNodeInMaint.comment: A scheduler keeps a list consisting only of nodes with an upgrade in progress. This object is used by the scheduler to keep track of nodes in the installation and only exists on the controller.

maintPodMaintGrp.label: POD Maintenance Group
maintPodMaintGrp.comment: POD Maintenance Group. A set of PODs to which a maintenance policy may be applied.

maintRsPolCatalogScheduler.comment: A source relation to the scheduler policy.

maintRsPolCtrlrScheduler.label: Relation to Controller Schedule Policy
maintRsPolCtrlrScheduler.comment: A source relation to the scheduler policy.

maintRsPolNotif.label: Relation to Maintenance Notification Policy
maintRsPolNotif.comment: A source relation to the notification specification. Specifies a notification for maintenance policy.

maintRsPolScheduler.label: Relation to Maintenence Scheduler Policy
maintRsPolScheduler.comment: A source relation to the scheduler policy.

maintRsWindowStarted.label: Relation to Window
maintRsWindowStarted.comment: A source relation to the container for scheduled windows.

maintRtAecatmaintp.comment: A target relation to the catalog maintenance policy.

maintRtAectrlrmaintp.label: Relation Holder
maintRtAectrlrmaintp.comment: A target relation to the controller maintenance policy.

maintRtMgrpp.label: POD Maintenance Group
maintRtMgrpp.comment: A target relation to the maintenance policy.

maintRtPolNotif.label: Maintenance Policy
maintRtPolNotif.comment: A target relation to the user notification.

maintUpgJob.comment: Specification for the maintenance policy enforcement status on a node. This object exists on every node where the policy is enforced.

maintUpgStatus.comment: Scheduler - one per maintenance policy. The Administrator can change the status from paused to running. This object exists only on the controller.

maintUpgStatusCont.label: Container for Upgrade Status
maintUpgStatusCont.comment: Container for maintenance policy scheduler status objects.

maintUpgWindowStats.label: Upgrade Window Statistics
maintUpgWindowStats.comment: The upgrade-related statistics on a per time window basis for the maintenance policy scheduler associated to a set of nodes.

mcpIf.comment: The Mis-cabling Protocol (MCP) configured interface. The interface type can be physical Ethernet Interface or the Port-Channel Interface. The MCP packets are sent over the logic interfaces.

mgmtConnectivityPrefs.label: mgmt connectivity preference
mgmtConnectivityPrefs.comment: ConnectivityPrefs has the information about routing interface(oob/inb)

mgmtExtMgmtEntity.label: External Entities
mgmtExtMgmtEntity.comment: The external entity management. The external entities (hosts) can communicate with nodes that are part of the out-of-band (OOB) management endpoint group. To enable this communication, hosts are connected to the OOB management port of the nodes.

mgmtGrp.label: Managed Node Connectivity Group
mgmtGrp.comment: The managed node connectivity group captures the set of nodes that are going to participate in the management network. All the nodes, a range of nodes, or a specific node can be selected to participate in a given group. This is controlled by the type field.

mgmtInB.label: In-Band Management EPg
mgmtInB.comment: The in-band management endpoint group consists of switches (leaves/spines) and APICs. Each node in the group is assigned an IP address that is dynamically allocated from the address pool associated with the corresponding in-band management zone. The allocated IP address is then configured as the in-band management access IP address on the corresponding node. Any host that is part of another endpoint group can communicate with the nodes in the in-band management endpoint group using contacts.

mgmtInstP.label: External Management Network Instance Profile
mgmtInstP.comment: The external management entity instance profile communicates with nodes that are part of out-of-band management endpoint group. To enable this communication, a contract is required between the instance profile and the out-of-band management endpoint group.

mgmtMgmtIfClearCountersLTask.comment: The management interface clear counters local task. This is a direct operation to be executed on the switch.

mgmtMgmtIfClearCountersRslt.comment: The management interface clear counters result.

mgmtMgmtP.label: Management Profile
mgmtMgmtP.comment: The in-band and out-of-band management endpoint groups consists of switches (leaves/spines) and APICs. Each node in the group is assigned an IP address that is dynamically allocated from the address pool associated with the corresponding in-band or out-of-band management zone.

mgmtNodeDef.label: Node where EPg will be Deployed

mgmtNodeGrp.label: Node Management Address
mgmtNodeGrp.comment: The managed node group captures the set of nodes that will participate in the management network. All the nodes, a range of nodes, or a specific node can be selected to participate in a given managed node group.

mgmtOoB.label: Out-Of-Band Management EPg
mgmtOoB.comment: An out-of-band management endpoint group, which consists of switches (leaves/spines) and APICs that are part of the associated out-of-band management zone. Each node in the group is assigned an IP address that is dynamically allocated from the address pool associated with the corresponding out-of-band management zone. The allocated IP address is then configured on the out-of-band management port of the corresponding node. Hosts that are part of regular endpoint groups cannot communicate with the nodes in the out-of-band management endpoint group. Any host that is part of a special group known as the instance profile can communicate with the nodes in an out-of-band management endpoint group using special out-of-band contracts. Regular contracts cannot be used with this endpoint group.

mgmtRsAddrInst.label: Relation to IP Address Pool
mgmtRsAddrInst.comment: A source relation to the IP address namespace/IP address range.

mgmtRsGrp.label: Managed Node Connectivity Group
mgmtRsGrp.comment: A source relation to the managed node group.

mgmtRsInbEpg.label: In-Band Management EPg
mgmtRsInbEpg.comment: A source relation to the in-band management endpoint group.

mgmtRsInstPCtx.label: Private Network
mgmtRsInstPCtx.comment: A source relation to the private layer 3 network context.

mgmtRsMgmtBD.label: Bridge Domain
mgmtRsMgmtBD.comment: A source relation to the management bridge domain.

mgmtRsOoBCons.label: Out-Of-Band Contract
mgmtRsOoBCons.comment: An external management entity instance profile to an out-of-band binary contract profile. The instance profiles of external management entities can communicate with nodes that are part of out-of-band management endpoint group. To enable this communication, a contract is required between the instance profile and the out-of-band management endpoint group.

mgmtRsOoBCtx.label: Private Network
mgmtRsOoBCtx.comment: A source relation to the private layer 3 network context.

mgmtRsOoBProv.label: Out-Of-Band Contract
mgmtRsOoBProv.comment: A source relation to an out-of-band contract profile.

mgmtRtInbEpg.comment: A target relation to the in-band management endpoint group.

mgmtRtOobEpg.comment: A target relation to the out-of-band management endpoint group.

mgmtRtRtdMgmtConf.comment: A target relation to the management interface.

mgmtSubnet.comment: The external subnet to be imported. The bridge domain context defines a unique IP address space, which can consist of multiple subnets. Those subnets are defined in one or more bridge domains that reference the corresponding context.

mldASnoopStaticGroup.comment: MLD Snoop Static Group, it's used to allow a tenant admin to statically join a multicast stream on some port,encap or fvAEPG

mldResP.comment: This object holds resource (router state) limit policy to limit amount of multicast routing state on the router

mldSSMXlate.comment: This objects hold per mld domain information

mldSnoopAccessGroup.comment: Representation of an MLD snooping filter

mldSnoopAccessGroupDef.label: MLD Snooping Access Group configuration                Internal representation
mldSnoopAccessGroupDef.comment: In case the filter will take place at the fvAEPg levelFor future releasesInternal Representation of an MLD snooping filter

mldSnoopDef.label: MLD Snoop Policy
mldSnoopDef.comment: The process of listening to Multicast Listener Discovery (MLD) network traffic. The feature allows a network switch to listen in on the IGMP conversation between hosts and routers. By listening to these conversations the switch maintains a map of which links need which IP multicast streams.

mldSnoopPol.label: MLD Snoop Policy
mldSnoopPol.comment: The MLD snooping policy streamlines multicast traffic handling for VLANs. By examining (snooping) MLD membership report messages from interested hosts, multicast traffic is limited to the subset of VLAN interfaces on which the hosts reside.

mldSnoopStaticGroup.label: MLD Snooping Static Membership configuration
mldSnoopStaticGroup.comment: Representation of an MLD snooping static (*,G) or (S,G) membership

mldSnoopStaticGroupDef.label: MLD Snooping Static Membership configuration
mldSnoopStaticGroupDef.comment: In case the static group membership is at the fvAEPg levelFor future releasesInternal representation of the group memebership

mldsnoopDom.comment: This objects hold per mld snooping domain information

mldsnoopEntity.label: MLD Snooping Entity
mldsnoopEntity.comment: Holds mld control plane entity information

mldsnoopEpgRec.label: Endpoint Group Record
mldsnoopEpgRec.comment: EPG entries in mcast group record

mldsnoopHostRec.label: Host Record
mldsnoopHostRec.comment: Host Record List (Used for Explicit Host Tracking)

monATarget.label: Base Monitoring Target
monATarget.comment: The base monitoring target policy model.

monCommonPol.label: Monitoring Policy
monCommonPol.comment: The monitoring policy model for the common semantic scope, which is used when there is no corresponding policy under the more specific infra or tenant scopes. In such cases, these policies are used throughout the fabric except for objects attached to their own specific policies.

monEPGPol.label: Monitoring Policy
monEPGPol.comment: Creates a container for monitoring policies associated with the tenant. This allows you to apply tenant-specific policies related to Stats Collection, Stats Export, Callhome/SNMP/Syslog, Event Severities, Fault Severities, and Fault Lifecycles.

monEPGTarget.label: Monitoring Target
monEPGTarget.comment: Creating a monitoring target will allow you to associate certain monitoring policies to the target object for this tenant. These associated policies can be related to stats collection, stats export, Callhome/SNMP/Syslog, event severities, fault severities, and fault lifecycle policies. The target object will be found in the Monitoring Object drop-down menu in the sub-folders of this monitoring policy, if available.

monExportP.label: Export Policy for a Given User
monExportP.comment: The export data policy for a given user.

monFabricPol.label: Monitoring Policy
monFabricPol.comment: Creates a policy which acts as a container for associated fabric monitoring policies. These can include policies related to Event/Fault severity, the Fault lifecycle, and other such monitoring policies.

monFabricTarget.label: Monitoring Target
monFabricTarget.comment: The monitoring target policy model for the fabric semantic scope.

monInfraPol.label: Monitoring Policy
monInfraPol.comment: Creates a policy which acts as a container for associated fabric monitoring policies. These can include policies related to Event/Fault severity, the Fault lifecycle, and other such monitoring policies.

monInfraTarget.label: Monitoring Target
monInfraTarget.comment: Creating a monitoring target will allow you to associate certain monitoring policies to the target object for the fabric infrastructure. These associated policies can be related to stats collection, stats export, Callhome/SNMP/Syslog, event severities, fault severities, and fault lifecycle policies. The target object will be found in the Monitoring Object drop-down menu in the sub-folders of this monitoring policy, if available.

monProtoP.label: Protocol Profile
monProtoP.comment: The objects in the hierarchy under mon:Pol (Policy, Target Subjects, ...) that define scopes for monitoring activities, including statistics reporting and notification dispatching. To enable notification dispatching, a user attaches a mon:Src object under the object with the desired scope. The source provides an association to a group, which contains all the information needed to dispatch notifications to a set of destinations using a specified protocol. #### A group contains the following: * a protocol profile (port, transport, etc.) * a sec/auth profile (user credentials, encryption parameters, etc.) * a set of destinations and associated filtering criteria, if any The elements of a group must have consistent type, so containment relationships are only specified for concrete groups. Some elements (for example, sec/auth profile) may not be defined for some protocols.

ndRaSubnet.label: ND Proxy Subnet
ndRaSubnet.comment: Neighbor Discovery (ND) uses ICMPv6 messages known as Router advertisement (RA) messages, which have a value of 134 in the Type field of the ICMP packet header, are periodically sent out each configured interface of an IPv6 device. For stateless autoconfiguration to work properly, the advertised prefix length in RA messages must always be 64 bits.

pkiKeyRing.label: Key Ring
pkiKeyRing.comment: A keyring to create and hold an SSL certificate. The SSL certificate contains the public RSA key and signed identity information of a PKI device. The PKI device holds a pair of RSA encryption keys, one kept private and one made public, stored in an internal key ring. The keyring certificate merges into the PKI device keyring to create a trusted relationship.

When participating in secure communications using the public key infrastructure (PKI), a participant can verify the identity of the other party through the CA that signed the other party's public key.

presClass.comment: This is generated and used only by internal processes.

presListener.comment: This is generated and used only by internal processes.

presNodeNotifierCont.comment: Node notification mechanims on PM is dynamic, based on node coming up and registering with APIC. There is no operational state on PD. List of nodes and pods present is read from user configuration (NodeIdentPol, which exposes public methods for that). There is still need for a mechanism to send nodeNotification when configuration changes and a node is added/removed. Following tree is used to keep track of all policies that are interested in node notifications.

presRtdCtxDefProtSupport.comment: When introducing new relation to l3extRtdCtxDef for the existing mos (bgp:CtxAfDef, ospf:CtxDef, ospf:CtxDefAf, eigrp:CtxAfDef), PE registers this class to indicate it's supporting the above new relations.

presRtdEppOptimized.comment: In fv/types.xml, see fv:rtdEppVersion. If a leaf node supports "optimized" version, then the following MO is registered.

procSystem.label: System Process Information
procSystem.comment: A container of processes in the system. This object holds detailed information about various processes running on the switch.

psuInstPol.label: Power Supply Redundancy Policy
psuInstPol.comment: The power redundancy policy is for all power supply units on the fabric nodes (leaves and spines) that are consuming the power supply policy through their respective selector profile policy.

psuRtPsuInstPol.comment: A target relation to the power redundancy policy.

psuRtPsuInstPolCons.label: Power Supply Group Policy
psuRtPsuInstPolCons.comment: A target relation to the power redundancy policy.

psuRtResPsuInstPol.label: Fabric Instance
psuRtResPsuInstPol.comment: A target relation to the power redundancy policy.

psuRtSysPsuInstPolCons.label: System
psuRtSysPsuInstPolCons.comment: Relationship with the psu instance policy consumed by this node

ptpCfgDef.label: PTP Epg configuration
ptpCfgDef.comment: PTP internal representation for ptp Cfg

ptpDomain.label: User configured PTP Domain. will be associated with interface

ptpInst.label: PTP Instance
ptpInst.comment: This object holds PTP instance information.

ptpRtSysPtpInstPolCons.label: System
ptpRtSysPtpInstPolCons.comment: Relationship with the ptp instance policy consumed by this node

ptpUcastIp.label: Unicast IP address
ptpUcastIp.comment: This object holds unicast ip address information

ptpUnicastIp.label: Unicast IP address
ptpUnicastIp.comment: This object holds unicast ip address information

qosABuffer.label: Abstraction of Buffer Policy
qosABuffer.comment: The buffer policy holder describes the minimum guaranteed packet buffer reservations. These are applied per class currently in the system.

qosACong.label: Abstraction of Congestion Policy
qosACong.comment: An abstraction of a congestion management policy holder. This object describes congestion algorithms and ECN information. These are applied at a per class today in the system.

qosADot1PClass.label: Abstraction of Dot1P Class to Priority Mapping Policy
qosADot1PClass.comment: An abstraction of a dot1P class.

qosADppPol.label: Data Plane Policing policy
qosADppPol.comment: Define a Data Plane Policing policy. User is supposed to use this in scenarios where the incoming traffic need to be policed to certain levels

qosADppPolHolder.label: Data Plane Policing policy
qosADppPolHolder.comment: Mo that will be attached to retrieve the DN of the qosDppPolDef that should be downloaded on the leaf in order to program the leaf

qosADscpClass.label: Abstraction of DSCP Class to Priority Mapping Policy
qosADscpClass.comment: An abstraction of a DSCP class.

qosADscpTrans.label: Abstraction of DSCP class-cos translation for L3 traffic to other pods/sites

qosAMplsEgressRule.label: Abstraction of Dscp to Exp Mapping Policy

qosAMplsIngressRule.label: Abstraction of Exp Class to Priority Mapping Policy

qosAQueue.label: Abstraction of Queue Management Policy
qosAQueue.comment: An abstraction of a queue management policy holder. This object describes the queue threshold parameters, such as management method, limits, etc. These are applied at a per class today in the system.

qosASched.label: Abstraction of Schedule Policy
qosASched.comment: An abstraction of a schedule policy holder. This object describes the minimum guaranteed packet buffer reservations. These are applied at a per class today in the system.

qosAUburst.label: Abstraction of Microburst Policy
qosAUburst.comment: Microburst policy holder, describes the threshold units type, up_threshold and down_thresholds in bytes and percenage, etc. These are applied at a per class today in the system

qosBuffer.label: QOS Buffer Policy
qosBuffer.comment: The buffering information of a class, such as the minimum buffer required for a class. This is applied at system level

qosClass.label: QOS Class Policy
qosClass.comment: The QoS classification traffic descriptor and specifications are used to categorize a packet within a specific group and making the packet accessible for QoS handling in the network.

qosClassification.comment: An abstract class for dot1p and DSCP classification rules.

qosCong.label: QOS Congestion Policy
qosCong.comment: The congestion algorithm and ECN information of a class. This is applied at system level

qosCustomPol.label: Custom QOS Policy
qosCustomPol.comment: The custom QoS policy enables different levels of service to be assigned to network traffic, including specifications for the Differentiated Services Code Point (DSCP) value(s), and the 802.1p Dot1p priority.

qosCustomPolDef.label: Custom QOS Policy Definition
qosCustomPolDef.comment: The definition class for a custom QOS policy. Note that this is an internal object.

qosDot1PClass.label: Dot1P Class to Priority Mapping Policy
qosDot1PClass.comment: The class level for dot1P to prioritize the map.

qosDot1PClassDef.label: Dot1P Class to Priority Mapping Policy Definition
qosDot1PClassDef.comment: The definition class to be attached to the endpoint profile. Note that this is an internal object.

qosDppPol.label: Data Plane Policing Policy
qosDppPol.comment: Define a Data Plane Policing policy. User is supposed to use this in scenarios where the incoming traffic need to be policed to certain levels

qosDppPolDef.label: Data Plane Policing policy Extra data
qosDppPolDef.comment: Define the Data Plane Policing MO which contains internal information needed to program the leaf

qosDppPolDefCont.label: Data Plane Policing policy Extra data
qosDppPolDefCont.comment: Container for all the qos:DppPolDef, under top, to have them neatly organized

qosDscpClass.label: DSCP Class to Priority Mapping Policy
qosDscpClass.comment: The class level for DSCP to prioritize the map.

qosDscpClassDef.label: DSCP Class to Priority Mapping Policy Definition
qosDscpClassDef.comment: The definition class to be attached to the endpoint profile. Note that this is an internal object.

qosDscpTransDef.label: DSCP class-cos internal policy for L3 traffic to other pods/sites

qosDscpTransPol.label: DSCP class-cos translation policy for L3 traffic to other pods/sites

qosEpDscpMarking.label: Dscp marking of endpoint egress traffic

qosInstPol.label: QOS Instance Policy
qosInstPol.comment: A QOS instance policy, which is a container for QOS class objects.

qosLlfcIfPol.label: Interface Link Level Flow Control Policy

qosMplsCustomPol.label: MPLS Custom QOS Policy
qosMplsCustomPol.comment: MPLS Custom QOS policy to hold Ingress and Egress Rules

qosMplsEgressRuleDef.label: Dscp to Exp Mapping Policy Definition
qosMplsEgressRuleDef.comment: Definition MPLS class to be attached to epp

qosMplsIngressRule.label: Mpls to Priority Mapping Policy

qosMplsIngressRuleDef.label: Exp Class to Priority Mapping Policy Definition
qosMplsIngressRuleDef.comment: Definition MPLS class to be attached

qosQueue.label: QOS Queue Management Policy
qosQueue.comment: The queuing information, such as the limit and queuing method. This is applied at system level.

qosRequirement.label: Collection of Qos rules that need to be enforced

qosRsDefToCustomPol.label: Custom QOS Policy
qosRsDefToCustomPol.comment: A source relation to a custom QoS policy that enables different levels of service to be assigned to network traffic, including specifications for the Differentiated Services Code Point (DSCP) value(s), and the 802.1p Dot1p priority. Note that this relation is an internal object.

qosRsDefToDot1PClass.label: Dot1P Class to Priority Mapping Policy
qosRsDefToDot1PClass.comment: A source relation to the class for dot1p to priority map. Note that this relation is an internal object.

qosRsDefToDscpClass.label: DSCP Class to Priority Mapping Policy
qosRsDefToDscpClass.comment: A source relation to the class for DSCP to priority map. Note that this relation is an internal object.

qosRsDefToMplsEgressRule.label: Mpls Dscp to Exp Mapping Policy

qosRsEgressDppPol.label: Policing policy that need to enforced in egress direction

qosRsIngressDppPol.label: Policing policy that need to enforced in ingress direction

Note that this relation is an internal object.

qosRtDefToCustomPol.label: Custom QOS Policy Definition

Note that this relation is an internal object.

qosRtLIfPCustQosPol.label: Logical Interface Profile
qosRtLIfPCustQosPol.comment: Relationship for Custom QOS policy for DSCP to prio mapping

qosRtQosEgressDppIfPol.comment: Relation to qosDppPol as Engress policy

qosRtQosEgressDppIfPolCons.comment: Relationship with the qosDppPol consumed by the physical interface in Egress

qosRtQosIngressDppIfPol.label: Abstraction of Leaf Access Policy Group
qosRtQosIngressDppIfPol.comment: Relation to qosDppPol as Ingress policy

qosRtQosIngressDppIfPolCons.label: Ethernet Interface
qosRtQosIngressDppIfPolCons.comment: Relationship with the qosDppPol consumed by the physical interface in Ingress

qosRtResQosInstPol.comment: A target relation to a resolvable QOS instance policy. Note that this relation is an internal object.

qosRtToRemoteQosDppPolDef.comment: Relation that will cause the download of the qos:DppPolDef when required this will be triggered by behavioural code on PE when handling changes on the qosDppPolHolder Mo NOTE: the update-type is 'self' because this MO is very simple and doesn't need to update a subtree, but just itself, hence is much more efficent on framework

qosRtToRemoteQosRequirement.comment: Relation that will cause the download of the qos:Requirement when required this will be triggered by behavioural code on PE when handling changes on the qosDppPolHolder Mo NOTE: the update-type is 'self' because this MO is very simple and doesn't need to update a subtree, but just itself, hence is much more efficent on framework

snmpRtCommToCtxAtt.label: Community Security Policy
snmpRtCommToCtxAtt.comment: A target relation to the SNMP context used to scope the view of the OIDs.

snmpRtDestGroup.label: SNMP Source
snmpRtDestGroup.comment: A target relation to SNMP destination group. This group contains information needed to send traps or informs to a set of destinations

snmpRtEventMgrSnmpPol.label: Container for Event Mgr Policies
snmpRtEventMgrSnmpPol.comment: Relation from polciy container to snmp policies

snmpRtSnmpPol.label: Pod Policy Group
snmpRtSnmpPol.comment: A target relation to an SNMP policy that contains site information and general protocol configuration parameters. Note that this relation is an internal object.

snmpSrc.label: SNMP Source
snmpSrc.comment: The SNMP source profile determines the fault information, severity level, and destination for sending messages to the SNMP destination. SNMP is an application-layer protocol that provides a message format for communication between SNMP managers and agents. SNMP provides a standardized framework and a common language used for the monitoring and management of devices in a network .

snmpTrapDest.label: SNMP Trap Destination
snmpTrapDest.comment: A destination to which traps and informs are sent.

snmpUserP.label: User Profile
snmpUserP.comment: The SNMP user profile is used to associate users with SNMP policies for monitoring devices in a network. SNMP is an application-layer protocol that provides a message format for communication between SNMP managers and agents. SNMP provides a standardized framework and a common language used for the monitoring and management of devices in a network.

snmpUserSecP.label: User Security Policy
snmpUserSecP.comment: An authentication profile using user names.

spanADest.label: SPAN Destination
spanADest.comment: The abstraction of an SPAN destination. The SPAN destination is where network traffic is sent for analysis by a network analyzer. A SPAN destination can be local or remote (ERSPAN). When you create a traffic monitoring session, you must select a SPAN source and a SPAN destination. The type of session (Tenant, Access, or Fabric) determines the allowed types of SPAN sources and destinations. The destination can be either a port or an endpoint group. If the destination is a port, it should not be one that has been configured for other purposes.

spanADestSummary.label: Destination Summary
spanADestSummary.comment: The abstraction of a SPAN destination information summary, which is used for configuring the SPAN destination information summary.

spanAEpgSummary.label: SPAN EPG Summary
spanAEpgSummary.comment: The abstraction of a SPAN destination endpoint group (EPG) summary, which stores EPG information for SPAN.

spanASource.label: Source
spanASource.comment: The abstraction of a SPAN source. A SPAN source is where traffic is sampled. A SPAN source can be an endpoint group (EPG), one or more ports, or port traffic filtered by an EPG (Access SPAN), a Layer 2 bridge domain, or a Layer 3 context (Fabric SPAN). When you create a traffic monitoring session, you must select a SPAN source and a SPAN destination. The type of session (Tenant, Access or fabric) determines the allowed types of SPAN sources and destinations. The destination can be either a port or an EPG. If the destination is a port, it should not be one that has been configured for other purposes.

spanASpanLbl.label: Abstract SPAN Label
spanASpanLbl.comment: The abstraction of a SPAN label.

spanASrc.label: Abstract SPAN Source
spanASrc.comment: The abstraction of an SPAN source. The SPAN source is where traffic is sampled. A source can be an endpoint group (EPG), one or more ports, or port traffic filtered by an EPG (Access SPAN), a Layer 2 bridge domain, or a Layer 3 context (Fabric SPAN). When you create a traffic monitoring session, you must select a SPAN source and a SPAN destination. The type of session (Tenant, Access or fabric) determines the allowed types of SPAN sources and destinations. The destination can be either a port or an EPG. If the destination is a port, it should not be one that has been configured for other purposes.

spanASrcGrp.comment: The abstraction of a SPAN source group. The SPAN source group can contain a group of SPAN sources, which is where network traffic is sampled. A SPAN source can be an endpoint group (EPG), one or more ports, or port traffic filtered by an EPG (Access SPAN), a Layer 2 bridge domain, or a Layer 3 context (Fabric SPAN). When you create a traffic monitoring session, you must select a SPAN source group and a SPAN destination. The type of session (Tenant, Access or fabric) determines the allowed types of span sources and destinations. The destination can be either a port or an EPG. In case of a port, it should not be one that has been configured for other purposes.

spanAToCEp.label: Super class for each span to cep relation

spanAVSrcGrp.comment: The abstraction of a VSPAN source group. The VSPAN source group can contain a group of VSPAN sources. A VSPAN source is where network traffic is sampled. The type of session (Tenant, Access, or fabric) determines the allowed types of VSPAN sources and destinations. The destination can be either a port or an EPG. If the destination is a port, it should not be one that has been configured for other purposes

spanDb.label: Database
spanDb.comment: The abstraction of the SPAN database.

spanDest.label: SPAN Destination
spanDest.comment: The SPAN destination is where network traffic is sent for analysis by a network analyzer. A SPAN destination can be local or remote (ERSPAN). When you create a traffic monitoring session, you must select a SPAN source and a SPAN destination. The type of session (Tenant, Access, or Fabric) determines the allowed types of SPAN sources and destinations. The destination can be either a port or an endpoint group. If the destination is a port, it should not be one that has been configured for other purposes.

spanDestGrp.label: SPAN Destination Group
spanDestGrp.comment: The SPAN destination group contains a group of SPAN destinations. A SPAN destination is where network traffic is sent for analysis by a network analyzer. A SPAN destination can be local or remote (ERSPAN). When you create a traffic monitoring session, you must select a SPAN source and a SPAN destination. The type of session (Tenant, Access, or Fabric) determines the allowed types of SPAN sources and destinations. The destination can be either a port or an EPG. If the destination is a port, it should not be one that has been configured for other purposes.

spanDestination.label: Destination
spanDestination.comment: The SPAN destination, which is where network traffic is sent for analysis by a network analyzer. A SPAN destination can be local or remote (ERSPAN). When you create a traffic monitoring session, you must select a SPAN source and a SPAN destination. The type of session (Tenant, Access, or Fabric) determines the allowed types of SPAN sources and destinations. The destination can be either a port or an endpoint group. If the destination is a port, it should not be one that has been configured for other purposes.

spanERDestination.label: ERSPAN Destination
spanERDestination.comment: The ERSPAN destination, which is where network traffic is sent for analysis by a network analyzer. When you create a traffic monitoring session, you must select an ERSPAN source and ERSPAN destination. The type of session (Tenant, Access, or Fabric) determines the allowed types of ERSPAN sources and destinations. The destination can be either a remote port or endpoint group. If the destination is a port, it should not be one that has been configured for other purposes.

spanEntity.label: SPAN Entity
spanEntity.comment: The SPAN manager control plane entity information.

spanFabSource.label: Source
spanFabSource.comment: The fabric source port (also called a monitored port), which is a switched port that you monitor for network traffic analysis. In a single local SPAN session, you can monitor source port traffic such as received (Rx), transmitted (Tx), or bidirectional (both). The switch supports any number of source ports (up to the maximum number of available ports on the switch).

spanLDestination.label: Local Span Destination
spanLDestination.comment: The local SPAN destination, which is where network traffic is sent for analysis by a network analyzer. When you create a traffic monitoring session, you must select an local SPAN source and destination. The type of session (Tenant, Access, or Fabric) determines the allowed types of local SPAN sources and destinations. The destination can be either a port or endpoint group. If the destination is a port, it should not be one that has been configured for other purposes.

spanRec.label: Database Record
spanRec.comment: The abstract for the SPAN database record.

spanRsDestEpg.label: Relation to SPAN Destination EPG
spanRsDestEpg.comment: A source relation to a set of endpoints.

spanRsDestPathEp.label: Relation to SPAN Destination Path
spanRsDestPathEp.comment: A source relation to an abstraction of a path endpoint.

spanRsDestToVPort.label: Relation to VSPAN Destination Vport
spanRsDestToVPort.comment: A source relation to an endpoint.

spanRsProvDestGrp.label: Relation to SPAN Destination Provider Group
spanRsProvDestGrp.comment: A source relation to the SPAN destination group.

spanRsProvToVDestGrp.label: Relation to VSPAN VDestination Provider to Group
spanRsProvToVDestGrp.comment: A source relation to the name of the group used to match VSPAN sources with VSPAN destination(s).

spanRsSessionToDomainRef.label: Relation from Session To AAA Domain Ref

spanRsSpanSrcToL1IfAtt.label: L1 Interface Attachment
spanRsSpanSrcToL1IfAtt.comment: A source relation to an abstraction for a Layer 1 interface in the system.

spanRsSpanSrcToL2CktEpAtt.label: Relation to Span Circuit Endpoint
spanRsSpanSrcToL2CktEpAtt.comment: A source relation to the abstraction of a circuit endpoint.

spanRsSpanSsnToFltGrpAtt.label: Filter Group Attachment
spanRsSpanSsnToFltGrpAtt.comment: Cardinality is n-to-1 which is the default

spanRsSrcToBD.label: Relation to SPAN Source to Bridge Domain
spanRsSrcToBD.comment: The source relation to a private Layer 2 bridge domain.

spanRsSrcToBDDef.label: Source to BDDef Relation
spanRsSrcToBDDef.comment: A source relation to a private Layer 2 network context that belongs to a specific tenant or context, or is shared.

spanRsSrcToCtx.label: Relation to SPAN Source to Ctx
spanRsSrcToCtx.comment: A source relation to the private Layer 3 network context that belongs to a specific tenant or is shared.

spanRsSrcToEpP.label: Relation to SPAN Source to End Point Profile
spanRsSrcToEpP.comment: Relation to EpP created implicitly when srcToEpg is created under infra:Infra heirarchy

spanRsSrcToEpg.label: Relation to SPAN Source to EPG

spanRsSrcToFvIfConn.label: Relation to SPAN Source to external L3 interface

spanRsSrcToL3extOut.label: Relation to SPAN Source to l3extOut

spanRsSrcToNode.label: Relation to SPAN Source to Node

spanRsSrcToPathEp.label: Relation to SPAN Source to Path Endpoint

spanRsSrcToVPort.label: Relation to SPAN Source to Port
spanRsSrcToVPort.comment: A source relation to an endpoint.

spanRsSrcToVPortDef.label: Relation to SPAN Source to Port

spanRsVSrcGrpToFilterGrp.label: Relation from VSPAN Source Grp to span FilterGrp
spanRsVSrcGrpToFilterGrp.comment: Relation to FilterGrp. Used by OVS agent

spanRsVsrcToEpg.label: Relation to VSPAN Source to Epg
spanRsVsrcToEpg.comment: A source relation to a set of endpoints.

spanRtProvDestGrp.label: SPAN Destination Provider
spanRtProvDestGrp.comment: A target relation to the SPAN destination group.

spanRtProvToVDestGrp.label: VSPAN VDestination Provider
spanRtProvToVDestGrp.comment: A target relation to the VSPAN destination group.

spanRtSpanSsnToFltGrpAtt.label: Session
spanRtSpanSsnToFltGrpAtt.comment: Cardinality is n-to-1 which is the default
