spanRtSpanVSrcGrp.comment: A target relation to the VSPAN source group.

spanRtVSrcGrpToFilterGrp.label: VSPAN Session
spanRtVSrcGrpToFilterGrp.comment: Relation to FilterGrp. Used by OVS agent

spanSession.label: Session
spanSession.comment: A local SPAN session is an association of a destination port with source ports. You can monitor incoming or outgoing traffic on a series or range of ports.

spanSource.label: Fabric Span Source
spanSource.comment: The SPAN or ERSPAN source, which is where traffic is sampled. A source can be an endpoint group (EPG), one or more ports, or port traffic filtered by an EPG (Access SPAN), a Layer 2 bridge domain, or a Layer 3 context (Fabric SPAN). When you create a traffic monitoring session, you must select a source and a destination. The type of session (Tenant, Access, or Fabric) determines the allowed types of sources and destinations. The destination can be either a port or an EPG. If the destination is a port, it should not be one that has been configured for other purposes.

spanSpanCont.label: SPAN Container
spanSpanCont.comment: Source Group Definition objects that are created under fv:EpP.

spanSpanLbl.label: SPAN Source-destination Group Match Label
spanSpanLbl.comment: The SPAN label is used for SPAN label parameters.

spanSpanProv.label: SPAN Destination Provider
spanSpanProv.comment: The SPAN destination provider is used for configuring SPAN destination provider parameters.

spanSrc.label: SPAN Source
spanSrc.comment: The SPAN or ERSPAN source is where traffic is sampled. A source can be an endpoint group (EPG), one or more ports, or port traffic filtered by an EPG (access SPAN), a Layer 2 bridge domain, or a Layer 3 context (fabric SPAN). When you create a traffic monitoring session, you must select a source and a destination. The type of session (tenant, access, or fabric) determines the allowed types of sources and destinations. The destination can be either a port or an EPG. If the destination is a port, it should not be one that has been configured for other purposes.

spanSrcDef.comment: The SPAN source definitions. The SPAN source is where traffic is sampled. When you create a traffic monitoring session, you must select a SPAN source and a SPAN destination. If the destination is a port, it should not be one that has been configured for other purposes.

When you create a traffic monitoring session, you must select a SPAN source group and a SPAN destination. spanSrcGrpDef.comment: The SPAN source group definitions. The SPAN source is where traffic is sampled. spanSrcTargetShadowL3If.label: Relation to SPAN Src L3If Target Shadow
spanSrcTargetShadowL3If.comment: Holds span src to l3 itnerface related information

spanSrcTask.comment: A task to send SPAN and related information across shards.

spanTaskParam.label: Relation to SPAN Src EPG Container
spanTaskParam.comment: The SPAN source to EPG task information.

spanVDestGrpDef.label: VSPAN Dest Group Def.
spanVDestGrpDef.comment: VSPAN destination group used for configuring VSPAN source group definitions.

spanVEpgSummaryDef.label: Copy of  VSPAN Epg Summary Definition
spanVEpgSummaryDef.comment: The VSPAN EpgSummaryDef is used for VERSPAN definitions.

spanVSpanProv.label: VSPAN VDestination Provider
spanVSpanProv.comment: The VSPAN destination provider is used for configuring VSPAN destination provider parameters.

spanVSrc.label: VSPAN VSource
spanVSrc.comment: The VSPAN source, which is where traffic is sampled. A VSPAN source can be an endpoint group (EPG), one or more ports, or port traffic filtered by an EPG (Access VSPAN), a Layer 2 bridge domain, or a Layer 3 context (Fabric VSPAN). spanVSrcDef.label: Abstract VSPAN Source Definition
spanVSrcDef.comment: The VSPAN VSrcDef is used for VSPAN source definitions.

spanVSrcGrp.label: VSPAN Session
spanVSrcGrp.comment: The VSPAN source group can contain a group of VSPAN sources. A VSPAN source is where network traffic is sampled. A VSPAN source can be an endpoint group (EPG), one or more ports; or port traffic filtered by an EPG (access VSPAN), a Layer 2 bridge domain, or a Layer 3 context (fabric VSPAN). When you create a traffic monitoring session, you must select a VSPAN source group and a VSPAN destination. statsAColl.comment: A superclass that configures collection rules according to the granularity of the statistics.

statsAExportJob.label: Subjob Container
statsAExportJob.comment: A superclass for the export job status of statistical data.

statsAThrP.label: Base Threshold Policy Definition
statsAThrP.comment: The superclass for the statistical threshold policy. Subclasses represent export policies for specific data types.

statsColl.label: Statistics Collection Policy
statsColl.comment: The collection configuration policy, which configures the collection policy according to the granularity of the statistics.. This is also a container for threshold policies for the statistic granularity.

statsCurr.comment: The superclass for the current data objects of the statistics.

statsCurrAgPart.comment: The superclass for the aggregation parts of the current data objects that belong to the statistics and come from the node.

statsDestP.label: Stats Destination
statsDestP.comment: The export policy. The export policy enables you to output statistics collection policy reports in an XML or JSON format. The reports are exported at a specified frequency and to a chosen export destination. Specifying the export destination can require selecting the protocol used when communicating with a remote server and entering a hostname/IP address, a remote path, the username and password the system should use to log in to the remote server, and the distinguished name for a relation to the endpoint group used by the remote host.

statsExportJob.label: Job Container
statsExportJob.comment: The model for the holding status of an export job for statistical data.

statsExportP.label: Export Policy of an User
statsExportP.comment: The export policy, which enables you to output statistics collection policy reports in an XML or JSON format. The reports are exported at a specified frequency with an option for gzip file compression.

statsExportStatusCont.label: Status Container
statsExportStatusCont.comment: A container for the data export statuses of statistical data.

statsHierColl.label: Hierarchical Statistics Collection Policy
statsHierColl.comment: The statistics collection policy, which enables you to determine how often the system collects data and to specify how far back in time data should be collected.

statsHist.comment: The superclass for historical data objects of the statistics.

statsItem.comment: The superclass for current and historical data of the statistics.

statsMonPolDefCont.label: Monitoring Policy Shadow Container
statsMonPolDefCont.comment: A container for monitoring policy shadow objects.

statsReportable.label: Statistics Policy
statsReportable.comment: A statistics policy container, which contains statistic collections and export policies for different types of statistics.

statsThrDoubleP.label: Base Threshold Policy Definition
statsThrDoubleP.comment: The concrete statistical threshold policy for a double data type.

statsThrFloatP.label: Base Threshold Policy Definition
statsThrFloatP.comment: The concrete statistical threshold policy for a float data type.

statsThrSByteP.label: Base Threshold Policy Definition
statsThrSByteP.comment: The concrete statistical threshold policy for a signed byte data type.

statsThrSint16P.label: Base Threshold Policy Definition
statsThrSint16P.comment: The concrete statistical threshold policy for a 16-bit signed Int data type.

statsThrSint32P.label: Base Threshold Policy Definition
statsThrSint32P.comment: The concrete statistical threshold policy for a 32-bit signed Int data type.

statsThrSint64P.label: Base Threshold Policy Definition
statsThrSint64P.comment: The concrete statistical threshold policy for a 64-bit signed integer data type.

statsThrTriggerP.label: Base Threshold Policy Definition
statsThrTriggerP.comment: The concrete statistical threshold policy for a trigger counter type.

statsThrUByteP.label: Base Threshold Policy Definition
statsThrUByteP.comment: The concrete statistical threshold policy for an unsigned byte data type.

statsThrUint16P.label: Base Threshold Policy Definition
statsThrUint16P.comment: The concrete statistical threshold policy for a 16-bit unsigned Int data type.

statsThrUint32P.label: Base Threshold Policy Definition
statsThrUint32P.comment: The concrete statistical threshold policy for a 32-bit unsigned Int data type.

statsThrUint64P.label: Base Threshold Policy Definition
statsThrUint64P.comment: The concrete statistical threshold policy for a 64-bit unsigned integer data type.

statsprefCont.label: Container for various Stats Preferences
statsprefCont.comment: This objects hold information on stats preferences for various features

statsprefInfo.label: Stats Preference
statsprefInfo.comment: This objects hold information on stats preferences for a particular feature

statstoreCurrDataHolder.label: Current Data Holder
statstoreCurrDataHolder.comment: Container for current data statistics chunks.

statstoreHistDataHolder.label: History Data Holder
statstoreHistDataHolder.comment: container for history data statistics chunks.

statstoreObsClassHolder.label: Observable Class Holder
statstoreObsClassHolder.comment: Container for node objects to calculate fabric health.

statstoreObsHolder.label: Observable Holder
statstoreObsHolder.comment: Container for statistics for given observable object.

stormctrlIfPol.label: Storm Control Interface Policy
stormctrlIfPol.comment: The storm control interface policy. A traffic storm occurs when packets flood the LAN, creating excessive traffic and degrading network performance. You can use the traffic storm control feature to prevent disruptions on ports by a broadcast, multicast, or unknown unicast traffic storm on physical interfaces.

stormctrlRtStormctrlIfPol.label: Abstraction of Leaf Access Policy Group

stpAEncapBlkDef.label: Abstraction of Spanning Tree Encap Block
stpAEncapBlkDef.comment: An abstraction of a spanning tree encap block.

stpAEncapCont.label: Abstraction of Spanning Tree Encap Container
stpAEncapCont.comment: An abstraction of a container for managing the spanning tree flooding segment ID range.

stpAIfPol.label: Spanning Tree Interface Policy
stpAIfPol.comment: An abstraction of an spanning-tree protocol interface policy. This is applicable to leaf ports and n1000v distributed virtual switches. Extended chassis ports have BPDU guard filter enabled by default.

stpAllocEncapBlkDef.label: Spanning Tree Encap Block
stpAllocEncapBlkDef.comment: The spanning-tree protocol encap block definition for allocated IDs and the base segment ID used for the range. These segment IDs are used for spanning tree BPDU flooding within the fabric. It is implicitly managed by the IFC.

stpAllocEncapBlkDefDependencyResolver.label: Used internally in PE for AllocEncapBlkDef dependency resolver

stpAllocEncapCont.label: Spanning Tree Allocated Encap Container
stpAllocEncapCont.comment: A container for managing the spanning tree flooding segment ID range.

stpDom.label: Domain
stpDom.comment: A container for spanning-tree protocol domain information.

stpEncapInstDef.label: Spanning Tree Encap NameSpace
stpEncapInstDef.comment: The spanning-tree protocol encap instance profile definition. The segment IDs calculated using this profile are for spanning tree BPDU flooding within the fabric. It is implicitly managed by the IFC.

stpEncapSegIdPair.label: Vlan Segment Id Pair
stpEncapSegIdPair.comment: The table created at the node for efficient lookups of the spanning tree flooding segment ID. This table is created from the spanning-tree protocol encap block definition for allocated IDs and the base segment ID used for the range pushed by IFC.

stpEntity.label: STP Entity
stpEntity.comment: The spanning-tree protocol control plane entity information.

stpIf.label: STP Interface
stpIf.comment: A container for the MCP information that is operated at a interface level.

stpIfPol.label: Spanning Tree Interface Policy
stpIfPol.comment: The Spanning-Tree Protocol (STP) interface policy defines a common configuration that will apply to one or more interfaces. STP prevents loops from being formed when the interfaces are interconnected via multiple paths. Spanning-Tree Protocol implements the 802.1D IEEE algorithm by exchanging BPDU messages with other switches to detect loops, and then removes the loop by shutting down selected bridge interfaces. This algorithm guarantees that there is one and only one active path between two network devices.

stpIfPolDef.label: Spanning Tree Interface Policy
stpIfPolDef.comment: The read-only copy of the spanning-tree protocol interface policy.

stpInst.label: STP Instance
stpInst.comment: A container for per spanning-tree protocol (STP) instance information. There is only one instance of STP running in the system.

stpInstPol.label: Spanning Tree Policy
stpInstPol.comment: The spanning Tree Protocol (STP) instance policy, which enables you to set the bridge protocol data unit (BPDU) guard policy or filter. BDPUs are packets that run the STP protocol. The specification for STP is IEEE 802.1D. The main purpose of STP is to ensure that you do not create loops when you have redundant paths in your network. Loops are deadly to a network.

stpMstDomPol.label: Spanning Tree Domain Policy
stpMstDomPol.comment: The MST domain policy enables you to assign two or more VLANs to a spanning tree instance. MST is not the default spanning tree mode; Rapid per VLAN Spanning Tree (Rapid PVST+) is the default mode. MST instances with the same name, revision number, and VLAN-to-instance mapping combine to form an MST region. The MST region appears as a single bridge to spanning tree configurations outside the region. MST fails over to IEEE 802.1D Spanning Tree Protocol (STP) when it receives an 802.1D message from a neighboring switch.

stpMstRegionPol.label: Spanning Tree Policy Region
stpMstRegionPol.comment: The MST region policy enables switches to participate in MST instances when you consistently configure the switches with the same MST configuration information. A collection of interconnected switches that have the same MST configuration is an MST region. An MST region is a linked group of MST bridges with the same MST configuration.

stpRegion.label: MST Region
stpRegion.comment: A container for multiple spanning-tree or MSTP domain information.

stpRtDefaultStpIfPol.comment: A target relation to the spanning-tree protocol interface policy.

topSystem.comment: The APIC uses a policy model to combine data into a health score. Health scores can be aggregated for a variety of areas such as for the infrastructure, applications, or services. The category health score is calculated using a Lp -Norm formula. The health score penalty equals 100 minus the health score. The health score penalty represents the overall health score penalties of a set of MOs that belong to a given category and are children or direct relatives of the MO for which a health score is being calculated.

traceroutepRsTrDst.label: Relation to Traceroute Destination

When a router in the middle of the path finds a packet with TTL = 1, it responds with an Internet Control Message Protocol (ICMP) time exceeded message to the source. This message lets the source know that the packet traverses that particular router as a hop.

trackInst.label: OTM Instance
trackInst.comment: This objects holds OTM instance information.

trackIpSla.label: Object tracking of IP SLAs operations allow clients to track the output from IP SLAs objects
trackIpSla.comment: IP SLA track object. Object tracking of IP SLAs operations allow clients to track the output from IP SLAs objects and use this information to trigger an action. Two aspects of IP SLAs can be tracked: state and reachability. For state, if the return code is OK, then the track state is UP; if the return code is not OK, then the track state is Down. For reachability, if the return code is OK or OverThreshold, reachability is UP; if not OK, reachability is Down.

trackList.label: list object allows User to track the combined states of multiple objects
trackList.comment: An object track list allows User to track the combined states of multiple objects. Object track lists support the following capabilities: * Boolean "and" function: Each object defined within the track list must be in an UP state so that the track list object can become UP. track:List.type should be specified as 'and' * Boolean "or" function: At least one object defined within the track list must be in UP state so that the tracked object can become UP. track:List.type should be specified as 'or' * Threshold percentage: The percentage of UP objects in the tracked list must be greater than the configured UP threshold for the tracked list to be in the UP state. If the percentage of Down objects in the tracked list is above the configured track list Down threshold, the tracked list is marked as Down. track:List.type should be specified as 'percentage'. The value for 'percentageUp' and 'percentageDown' * Threshold weight: Assign a weight value to each object in the tracked list, and a weight threshold for the track list. If the combined weights of all UP objects exceeds the track list weight UP threshold, the track list is in an UP state. If the combined weights of all the Down objects exceeds the track list weight Down threshold, the track list is in the Down state. track:List.type should be specified as 'weight'. The value for 'weightUp' and 'weightDown'.Mo for List type track

trackMember.label: MO for tracking list members
trackMember.comment: track list member objects, object-id is a naming property here

trackObject.label: Placeholder for object which can be of type Interface line protocol state, Interface IPv4/IPv6 routing state, IPv4/IPv6 route reachability, Object track list and Object tracking of IP SLA
trackObject.comment: This object represents object of track. The object tracking monitors the status of the tracked objects and communicates any changes made to interested clients. Each tracked object is identified by a unique number (track:Object.id) that clients can use to configure the action to take when a tracked object changes state. Object types that can be tracked: * Interface line protocol state: Tracks whether the line protocol state is UP or Down. * Interface IPv4/IPv6 routing state: Tracks whether the interface has an IPv4 address and if IPv4 routing is enabled and active. * IPv4/IPv6 route reachability: Tracks whether an IPv4 route exists and is reachable from the local switch. * Object track list: Tracks combined states of multiple objects. Capabilities supported: boolean-and, boolean-or, threshold-percentage, threshold-weight. * Object tracking of IP SLA. Specify track:Object.id

trigSchedP.label: Scheduler
trigSchedP.comment: The scheduler policy enables you to schedule a recurring or one-time window for the execution of a task. Multiple scheduler policies can be created for the same time period.

trigSchedWindow.comment: A container for absolute or recurring scheduled windows.

trigSchedWindowP.comment: Container for absolute or recurring scheduled windows.

trigSingleTriggerable.comment: A triggerable object that can be triggered only once for each instance of a scheduler window.

trigState.label: Scheduler State Container
trigState.comment: A container for the scheduler state. All triggerables must be contained under this object.

trigTest.comment: An internal object for testing if an object can be triggered.

trigTriggerable.comment: A container for a triggerable object.

trigTriggered.comment: An object to track information about a triggered object.

trigTriggeredWindow.comment: An object to track which window has been triggered.

troubleshootEpTransition.label: Represents a state transition an end point

troubleshootRsSessionScheduler.label: Relation to a Scheduler Policy
troubleshootRsSessionScheduler.comment: Relation from session to scheduler policy

troubleshootSessionTrigger.label: Session Trigger
troubleshootSessionTrigger.comment: Trigger on which the scheduler issues a callback. For internal use only.

troubleshootSpanPktUrl.label: MO to provide the Url of span capture at apic

tunnelBank.label: Bank
tunnelBank.comment: A bank. Each port interface under tunnel has two sets of banks for counting the packets and bytes. This object is for statistics only.

tunnelCons.label: Consumer Reference
tunnelCons.comment: tunnel:Cons: Used for reference counting the consumers responsible for creation of this tunnel

tunnelCtrlPfxEntry.label: Tunnel Control Prefix Entry
tunnelCtrlPfxEntry.comment: Conversational tunnels discovered matching these external prefixes are marked untrusted or trusted based on whether the sclass can be trusted or not

tunnelCtx.label: Tunnel Context
tunnelCtx.comment: Tunnel container for all tunnel related information for a given layer 3 domain

tunnelPortIf.label: Port Interface
tunnelPortIf.comment: A port interface. This object is for stats purposes only. The tunnel stats on a given physical port are counted. These port objects will be created by default wherever the tunnel can exit out. For example, all north bound tunnels will have the fabric ports created under it.

tunnelRsTunnelToLooseNode.label: External Connected Node

uribv4Entity.label: IPv4 Unicast Routing Information Base Entity
uribv4Entity.comment: A container that holds entity information.

uribv4Nexthop.label: Nexthop Record
uribv4Nexthop.comment: A URIB next hop database record.

uribv4RsRouteOwnerToNexthopAtt.label: Nexthop Record
uribv4RsRouteOwnerToNexthopAtt.comment: A source relation to a URIB next hop database record.

uribv4RtRouteOwnerToNexthopAtt.label: Route Owner
uribv4RtRouteOwnerToNexthopAtt.comment: A target relation to a URIB next-hop database record.

usegDomainCont.label: Container
usegDomainCont.comment: Container For all EPg and UsegEPg in a given Domain

validatorContractScaleCap.label: Verified scalability guide for per contract per fabric

vethInst.label: Veth Instance
vethInst.comment: This object represents whether the NIV feature is enabled/disabled on the node

vlanCktEp.label: Vlan
vlanCktEp.comment: A VLAN object created for an endpoint group with an 802.1q encap.

vlanCktEpClearEpLTask.comment: A task object to clear endpoints in a given VLAN.

vlanCktEpClearEpRslt.comment: The result of the clear endpoint task posted for a given VLAN.

vlanRsVlanEppAtt.label: Epp Attachment
vlanRsVlanEppAtt.comment: A source relation to an abstract representation of the resolvable endpoint profile.

vlanRtSrcEncap.label: Exec
vlanRtSrcEncap.comment: A target relation to the VLAN object created for an endpoint group with an 802.1q encap.

vlanRtToVlanCkt.label: Rule
vlanRtToVlanCkt.comment: Relationship to the vlan::CktEpUsed for DVS USEG feature for ref counting number of Ckts using this ruleSingle vlanCktEp will be shared by multiple base EPGs in a given BD

vlanmgrEntity.label: Vlan Manager Entity
vlanmgrEntity.comment: A container for VLAN manager entity information.

vlanmgrInst.label: Vlan Manager Instance
vlanmgrInst.comment: A container for VLAN manager instance information.

vmmAUplinkPCont.label: Abstraction of Uplink Policy Container Policy

vmmAccGrpCont.label: Access Group Policy Container
vmmAccGrpCont.comment: A container for capturing all access group policies.

vmmAttEntityPCont.label: Access Group Container
vmmAttEntityPCont.comment: A container for capturing all access groups.

vmmClusterFaultInfo.label: Concrete class to raise faults for Cluster Issues in Vmm

vmmCtrlrP.label: VMM Controller
vmmCtrlrP.comment: The VMM controller profile specifies how to connect to a single VM management controller that is part of a policy enforcement domain. For example, the VMM controller profile could be a policy to connect a VMware vCenter that is part of a VMM domain.

vmmCtrlrPDef.label: Controller Profile Definition
vmmCtrlrPDef.comment: A shadow object needed on the iLeaf to get DVS policies for a given controller instance.

vmmCtrlrPTask.comment: The task the policy manager sends to the VMM for controller creation and deletion.

vmmCtxt.label: VMM Context
vmmCtxt.comment: This object is generated and used only by internal processes.

vmmDomP.label: VMM Domain
vmmDomP.comment: The VMM domain profile is a policy for grouping VM controllers with similar networking policy requirements. For example, the VM controllers can share VLAN or VXLAN space and application endpoint groups. The APIC communicates with the controller to publish network configurations such as port groups that are then applied to the virtual workloads.

vmmDomPDef.label: Domain Profile Definition
vmmDomPDef.comment: A container for distributed virtual switch policies. This is an object in the iLeaf (switch).

vmmEpPD.label: Portgroup
vmmEpPD.comment: An endpoint group in the VMM context. This object translates to port groups and/or virtual wires in vCenter and vShield.

vmmEpValidatorPol.label: End Point Validator Policy
vmmEpValidatorPol.comment: Currently only used by OpenStack VMMs

vmmEpgAggr.label: End Point Group Aggregator Definition
vmmEpgAggr.comment: EPG Aggregator Object @@@ This is managed by PolicyMgr

vmmEventRecord.label: VMM Controller Events
vmmEventRecord.comment: A config object that is not persisted and is used to send messages from a worker thread to a doer.

vmmIntAggr.label: Internal End Point Group Aggregator Definition
vmmIntAggr.comment: Internal EPG Aggregator Object @@@ This is managed by PolicyMgr

vmmIntCustomAggr.label: Internal Custom End Point Group Aggregator Definition

vmmObject.comment: The base class of VMM classes.

vmmProvP.label: Provider Profile
vmmProvP.comment: The VMM provider profile stores the policies of VMM systems from a particular vendor (VMware or Microsoft). This container is automatically created by the system.

vmmRsAcc.label: User Access Profile
vmmRsAcc.comment: A source relation to the user account profile.

vmmRsBaseCtrlrP.label: Controller Profile
vmmRsBaseCtrlrP.comment: A source relation to the VMM controller profile. Note that this relation is an internal object.

vmmRsBaseDomP.label: Domain Profile
vmmRsBaseDomP.comment: A source relation to the VMM domain profile. The VMM domain profile is a policy for grouping VM controllers with similar networking policy requirements. For example, the VM controllers can share VLAN or VXLAN space and application endpoint groups. The APIC communicates with the controller to publish network configurations such as port groups that are then applied to the virtual workloads. Note that this relation is an internal object.

vmmRsCtrlrPMonPol.label: Monitoring Policy
vmmRsCtrlrPMonPol.comment: A source relation to the monitoring policy model for the infra semantic scope.

vmmRsDefaultCdpIfPol.label: CDP Interface Policy
vmmRsDefaultCdpIfPol.comment: A source relation to the Cisco Discovery Channel interface policy.

vmmRsDefaultL2InstPol.label: L2 Instance Policy
vmmRsDefaultL2InstPol.comment: A source relation to the Layer 2 instance policy information.

vmmRsDefaultLacpLagPol.label: LACP Policy
vmmRsDefaultLacpLagPol.comment: A source relation to the LLDP policy parameters for the interface. LLDP uses the logical link control (LLC) services to transmit and receive information to and from other LLDP agents.

vmmRsDefaultLldpIfPol.label: LLDP Interface Policy
vmmRsDefaultLldpIfPol.comment: A source relation to the LLDP policy parameters for the interface.

vmmRsDefaultStpIfPol.label: Stp Interface Policy
vmmRsDefaultStpIfPol.comment: A source relation to the spanning-tree protocol (STP) policy. The STP policy prevents loops from being formed when the interfaces are interconnected via multiple paths. Spanning-Tree Protocol implements the 802.1D IEEE algorithm by exchanging BPDU messages with other switches to detect loops, and then removes the loop by shutting down selected bridge interfaces. This algorithm guarantees that there is one and only one active path between two network devices.

vmmRsDomMcastAddrNs.label: Multicast Addr Pool
vmmRsDomMcastAddrNs.comment: A source relation to the policy definition of the multicast IP address ranges.

vmmRsEncapAllctr.label: Encap Allocator
vmmRsEncapAllctr.comment: Relation to the allocator for encap

vmmRsMcastAddrNs.label: Multicast Addr Pool
vmmRsMcastAddrNs.comment: A source relation to the policy definition of the multicast IP address ranges.

vmmRsMgmtEPg.label: Management EPg
vmmRsMgmtEPg.comment: A source relation to a set of endpoints.

vmmRsPrefEnhancedLagPol.label: Relation to Preferred Enhanced Lag Policy

vmmRsToExtDevMgr.label: Extern device mgr profile
vmmRsToExtDevMgr.comment: Association to External Device Controller Profile

vmmRsUsrAggrLagPolAtt.label: Trunk PortGroup Relationship to Enhanced Lag Policy

vmmRsUsrCustomAggrLagPolAtt.label: User Custom End Point Group Aggregator Relationship to Enhanced Lag Policy

vmmRsVmmCtrlrP.label: Vmm Controller Profile
vmmRsVmmCtrlrP.comment: A source relation to the VMM controller profile. The VMM controller profile is a policy pertaining to a single VM management domain that also corresponds to a single policy enforcement domain. A cluster of VMware VCs forms such a domain. Note that this relation is an internal object.

vmmRsVswitchExporterPol.label: Relation to VMM Netflow Exporter Policy
vmmRsVswitchExporterPol.comment: Relationship to VMM Netflow Exporter Policy

vmmRsVswitchOverrideCdpIfPol.label: Relation to CDP Interface Policy
vmmRsVswitchOverrideCdpIfPol.comment: Relationship to policy providing physical configuration of the interfaces

vmmRsVswitchOverrideMcpIfPol.label: Relation to MCP Interface Policy
vmmRsVswitchOverrideMcpIfPol.comment: Relationship to policy providing physical configuration of the interfaces

vmmRsVswitchOverrideMtuPol.label: Relation to MTU Policy
vmmRsVswitchOverrideMtuPol.comment: Relationship to policy providing physical configuration of the interfaces

vmmRsVxlanNs.label: VXLAN Pool
vmmRsVxlanNs.comment: A source relation to the VxLAN namespace policy definition.

vmmRsVxlanNsDef.label: Unresolvable Relation to VxLAN Pool
vmmRsVxlanNsDef.comment: A source relation to the namespace policy is used for managing the encap (VXLAN, NVGRE, VLAN) ranges.

vmmRtALDevToDomP.label: Logical Device Instance
vmmRtALDevToDomP.comment: A target relation to the VMM domain profile. The VMM domain profile is a policy for grouping VM controllers with similar networking policy requirements. For example, the VM controllers can share VLAN or VXLAN space and application endpoint groups. The APIC communicates with the controller to publish network configurations such as port groups that are then applied to the virtual workloads. Note that this relation is an internal object.

vmmRtAcc.label: VMM Controller
vmmRtAcc.comment: A target relation to the user account profile. Note that this relation is an internal object.

vmmRtBaseCtrlrP.label: Controller Profile Definition
vmmRtBaseCtrlrP.comment: A target relation to the VMM controller profile. The VMM controller profile is a policy pertaining to a single VM management domain that also corresponds to a single policy enforcement domain. A cluster of VMware VCs forms such a domain. Note that this relation is an internal object.

vmmRtBaseDomP.label: Domain Profile Definition
vmmRtBaseDomP.comment: A target relation to the VMM domain profile. The VMM domain profile is a policy for grouping VM controllers with similar networking policy requirements. For example, the VM controllers can share VLAN or VXLAN space and application endpoint groups. The APIC communicates with the controller to publish network configurations such as port groups that are then applied to the virtual workloads. Note that this relation is an internal object.

vmmRtCDevToCtrlrP.label: Concrete Device
vmmRtCDevToCtrlrP.comment: implicit relation to validate the controller profile

vmmRtCtrlrP.comment: A target relation to a vCenter controller in the VMM manager component.

vmmRtDomP.label: Domain
vmmRtDomP.comment: An internal object (in the comp domain scope) created as part of relation to a VMM domain profile.

vmmRtDompRel.label: Relation Holder
vmmRtDompRel.comment: A target relation to the VMM domain profile. The VMM domain profile is a policy for grouping VM controllers with similar networking policy requirements. For example, the VM controllers can share VLAN or VXLAN space and application endpoint groups. The APIC communicates with the controller to publish network configurations such as port groups that are then applied to the virtual workloads. Note that this relation is an internal object.

vmmRtEncapAllctr.label: Portgroup
vmmRtEncapAllctr.comment: Relation to the allocator for encap

vmmRtProvP.label: Provider
vmmRtProvP.comment: A target relation to the VMM provider profile. The provider profile is a container for storing the policies of VMM systems from a particular vendor (VMware or Microsoft). This container is automatically created by the system. Note that this relation is an internal object.

vmmRtVirtualAttachList.label: Policy Enforcement Point
vmmRtVirtualAttachList.comment: Relation to the available virtual attach nodes when in ivxlan mode.

vmmRtVmmCtrlrP.label: VMM Controller
vmmRtVmmCtrlrP.comment: A target relation to the VMM controller profile. The VMM controller profile is a policy pertaining to a single VM management domain that also corresponds to a single policy enforcement domain. A cluster of VMware VCs forms such a domain. Note that this relation is an internal object.

vmmSvcVM.label: L4-L7 Virtual Service
vmmSvcVM.comment: An L4-L7 services VM. Note that this is a child object of an object representing an endpoint group in a VMM context.

vmmUplinkPContDef.label: Abstraction of Uplink Policy Container Policy
vmmUplinkPContDef.comment: read only copy of uplink policy container

vmmUplinkPDef.label: Abstraction of Uplink Policy
vmmUplinkPDef.comment: read only copy of uplink policy

vmmUsrAccP.label: VMM Credential
vmmUsrAccP.comment: The user account profile is used to access a VM provider account.

vmmUsrAggr.label: User Configurable End Point Group Aggregator Definition
vmmUsrAggr.comment: User configurable EPG Aggregator Object @@@ This is managed by PolicyMgr

vmmUsrCustomAggr.label: User Custom End Point Group Aggregator Definition

vmmVNicPD.label: VNic Profile Definition
vmmVNicPD.comment: A network interface card on an L4-L7 services VM.

vmmVSwitchPolicyCont.label: VSwitch Policy Group
vmmVSwitchPolicyCont.comment: VSwitch Policy Group. Container for the vswitch policies

vnsACCfg.label: Abstract Base for Configuration Objects
vnsACCfg.comment: Contains a hierarchy of folders and parameters similar to AMItem.

vnsACCfgRel.label: Relation to L4-L7 Services Param Relation Abstract Class

vnsAGraph.label: Graph Abstract Class
vnsAGraph.comment: A service graph is an ordered set of function nodes between a set of terminals, which identifies a set of network service functions that are required by an application. Service functions within a graph are automatically provisioned on a service device that is based on an application's requirements.

vnsAL4L7ServiceFault.label: Abstract Base for Attaching Faults
vnsAL4L7ServiceFault.comment: An abstract base class for attaching faults on service managed objects.

vnsALDevIf.label: Logical Device Abstraction
vnsALDevIf.comment: An abstract object used to represent either a service device cluster or a proxy object for a logical device cluster in the tenant.

vnsALDevLIf.label: Logical Interface
vnsALDevLIf.comment: An abstraction of a logical device.

vnsALIf.label: Logical Interface
vnsALIf.comment: A logical interface representation associated with a set of concrete interfaces from the cluster.

vnsAMItem.label: Abstract Configuration Item. can be either MFolder, MParam or MRel
vnsAMItem.comment: An abstract configuration item: MFolder, MParam, or MRel. AMItem can contain the generic configuration of the device. It can be used for settings that are function-independent, such as NTP or DHCP servers.

vnsAMgmt.label: Management Interface
vnsAMgmt.comment: Management interface for the Concrete device

vnsANodeInst.label: Function Node Abstract Class
vnsANodeInst.comment: The abstraction of a node instance.

vnsAScriptRTInfo.label: Contains Runtime Info of a Script

vnsAVRoutingDeviceCfg.label: Abstract Routing Configuration per device context/vrf

vnsAbsCfgRel.label: L4-L7 Services Relation
vnsAbsCfgRel.comment: The folder/param relations added to an abstract graph.

vnsAbsConnection.label: Connection
vnsAbsConnection.comment: An abstract connection connects two abstract connectors. These connections can either be between two abstract nodes or between an abstract node and an abstract terminal node.

vnsAbsDevCfg.label: L4-L7 Services Device Shared Config
vnsAbsDevCfg.comment: A shared configuration for a logical device in the L4-L7 device cluster. This configuration can be shared across multiple logical devices.

vnsAbsFolder.label: L4-L7 Services Folder
vnsAbsFolder.comment: A folder added to an abstract graph. An abstract graph folder can contain an abstract graph folder (AbsFolder) and abstract node parameters (AbsParam), which are analogous to an MFolder and MParam. An abstract graph folder can be contained under abstract device configuration (AbsDevCfg), abstract group configuration (AbsGrpCfg), or abstract function configuration (AbsFuncCfg).

vnsAbsFuncCfg.label: L4-L7 Services Function Config
vnsAbsFuncCfg.comment: The configuration for a function. This configuration can be shared across multiple functions.

vnsAbsFuncConn.label: Function Connector
vnsAbsFuncConn.comment: An abstract function node connector is used to map a service graph interface with the device interface.

vnsAbsFuncProf.label: L4-L7 Services Function Profile
vnsAbsFuncProf.comment: An abstract function profile includes the abstract device configuration, the abstract group configuration, and the abstract function configuration. These are analogous to the function configuration, group configuration, and device configuration within a device.

vnsAbsGraph.label: L4-L7 Service Graph Template
vnsAbsGraph.comment: The abstract graph is made up of abstract nodes and used to define the traffic flow through a service function such as load balancing, SSL offload, and firewall. Abstract nodes are comprised of service nodes such as a service node balancer (SLB) or firewall (FW), abstract term nodes (the nodes that are connected to endpoint groups), and connections.

vnsAbsGrpCfg.label: L4-L7 Services Group Shared Config
vnsAbsGrpCfg.comment: The shared configuration for a function group. This configuration can be shared across multiple logical groups.

vnsAbsNode.label: Function Node
vnsAbsNode.comment: An abstract node represents a service node such as a server load balancer (SLB) or firewall (FW). An abstract node is contained in an abstract graph.

vnsAbsParam.label: L4-L7 Services Parameter
vnsAbsParam.comment: The abstract node parameters. This is contained by the folder added to the abstract graph (AbsFolder).

vnsAbsTermNode.label: Terminal Node Abstract Class
vnsAbsTermNode.comment: An abstract terminal node. Abstract terminal nodes are typically attached to the endpoint groups, and are connected to the abstract graph (AbsGraph) through an abstract connection (AbsConnection).

vnsAbsTermNodeCon.label: Consumer Terminal Node
vnsAbsTermNodeCon.comment: An abstract terminal node connected to a contract consumer.

vnsAbsTermNodeProv.label: Provider Terminal Node
vnsAbsTermNodeProv.comment: An abstract terminal node connected to contract provider.

vnsBDDef.label: BDDef
vnsBDDef.comment: A bridge domain definition for tracking allocated bridge domains. This is an internally used object.

vnsCCred.label: Credentials
vnsCCred.comment: The concrete device access credentials in the L4-L7 device cluster. The concrete device access credentials normally include a password that is not displayed and is stored in encrypted form.

vnsCCredSecret.label: Secret Credentials
vnsCCredSecret.comment: The secret for the concrete device access credentials in the L4-L7 device cluster. The concrete device access credentials normally include a password that is not displayed and is stored in encrypted form.

vnsCDev.label: Concrete Device
vnsCDev.comment: The individual service device is used to define a concrete l4-l7 service device.

vnsCDevOperInfo.label: Operational Information for Concrete L4-L7 Devices

vnsCDevState.label: State of the Device
vnsCDevState.comment: The state of the concrete device.

vnsCIf.label: Concrete Interface
vnsCIf.comment: A concrete interface in the L4-L7 device cluster. Several concrete interfaces can be associated with an interface label (MIfLbl).

vnsCIfAtt.label: Interface
vnsCIfAtt.comment: The physical interface on a concrete device. This is an internal object.

vnsCMgmt.label: Management Interface
vnsCMgmt.comment: The management interface is used to manage a concrete device in the L4-L7 device cluster. The management interface is identified by a host address and port number.

vnsCMgmtProxy.label: State of the Management Interface of Device
vnsCMgmtProxy.comment: A proxy for a device management interface.

vnsCapacityUpdate.comment: An internal object that communicates device capacity information from script wrapper to script handler.

vnsCapct.comment: The actual capacity of a device. This is an internal object.

vnsCfgDef.comment: GraphInst contains a copy of the per logical device shared configuration.

vnsCfgRelInst.label: Relation to L4-L7 Services Param Relation Abstract Class
vnsCfgRelInst.comment: A folder instance configured by the administrator.

vnsCfgRoot.label: Root Node for Configuration Tree.
vnsCfgRoot.comment: The root node for the device parameter configuration tree.

vnsChkr.label: checker for dev folder/params
vnsChkr.comment: Implicit object to trigger dev folder/param validation

vnsCloudSvcEPgDef.label: CloudSvcEPgDef
vnsCloudSvcEPgDef.comment: Internal Use only - Used to track allocated epgs

vnsClusterCfg.label: L4-L7 Service Meta Device Cluster Config

vnsConsump.comment: The amount of device capacity consumed. This is an internal object.

vnsConsumptionUpdate.comment: An internal object to notify change in the resource consumption of a device.

vnsCtrlrEp.label: Ctrlr Appliance Address
vnsCtrlrEp.comment: The controller appliance IP address and connectivity information.

vnsCtrlrEpProxy.label: State of the Management Interface of Device
vnsCtrlrEpProxy.comment: The state of the nat interface on the controller used to communicate with the device.

vnsCtrlrMgmtPol.label: Management Policy
vnsCtrlrMgmtPol.comment: The management policy for the controller appliance IP address.

vnsDevCfg.label: L4-L7 Service Meta Device Dev Config

vnsDevCfgInst.label: Per LDev Shared Cfg
vnsDevCfgInst.comment: GraphInst contains a copy of the per logical device shared configuration.

vnsDevCtxLblInfo.comment: An implicitly created object for storing configuration information to concrete device association.

vnsDevFolder.label: Device Configuration
vnsDevFolder.comment: A container to hold device specific configuration items.

vnsDevHealth.label: Abstract Base for Attaching Faults
vnsDevHealth.comment: The health of a service device, typically from 0 (poor) to 100 (good).

vnsDevIssues.label: Device Issues
vnsDevIssues.comment: An object that represents any failures encountered when configuring a service node.

vnsDevItem.label: Device Configuration
vnsDevItem.comment: An abstract class used to represent the concrete classes that store configuration information for concrete device association and that represent a configuration parameter for the service device.

vnsDevMod.label: Extension Module
vnsDevMod.comment: A device extension module. When device script handlers need extensions to their existing functionality, use this module to supplement the handlers with additional functionality.

vnsDevParam.label: Device Configuration
vnsDevParam.comment: An object used to represent a configuration parameter for the service device. For example, the type of load balancing algorithm to use when configuring a server load balancer or the enable/disable option for the application inspection of ICMP packets in a firewall.

vnsDevPing.label: CDev Ping Policy
vnsDevPing.comment: An object created to test the reachability information of a device node.

vnsDevScript.label: Device Script
vnsDevScript.comment: A device script handler contains meta information about the script handler's related attributes, including its name, package name, and version.

vnsDeviceScriptBackups.label: Container for device package debug log backups

vnsEPgDef.label: EPgDef
vnsEPgDef.comment: An object used to track allocated endpoint groups. This object is used internally.

vnsEPgDefTask.comment: An internal object to track a task on the object that tracks allocated endpoint groups.

vnsEPpContr.label: Service VDev EPP Container
vnsEPpContr.comment: A container to hold endpoint profile objects.

vnsEPpInfo.label: Service VDev EPP Information
vnsEPpInfo.comment: Internal object to store encap info (vlan ID) for an endpoint.

vnsEPpInfoTask.comment: An internal object to rack a task on the container that holds endpoint profile objects.

vnsEpInst.label: End Points that Shares the Ip and Segment
vnsEpInst.comment: The endpoint that shares the IP and segment of the node instance.

vnsEpPRedirPolConsCount.label: Number of consumers of this policy

vnsFaultUpdate.comment: An internal object created to track faults.

vnsFltInst.comment: The representation of a whitelist filter that needs to be applied on a particular endpoint group.

vnsFolderInst.label: L4-L7 folder instance
vnsFolderInst.comment: A folder instance configured by the administrator.

vnsGParam.label: L4-L7 Services Parameter
vnsGParam.comment: NodeInst contains a copy of the abstract parameter from the abstract graph.

vnsGraphIdCntnr.label: Container for Graph Ids
vnsGraphIdCntnr.comment: Contains an ID consumer for the IDs needed by the graph.

vnsGraphInst.label: L4-L7 Service Graph Instance
vnsGraphInst.comment: The instance of a service graph. All instance objects are implicit.

vnsGrpCfgInst.label: Per Function Group Shared Cfg
vnsGrpCfgInst.comment: A per function group shared configuration.

vnsHAPortGroup.label: DataPath Interface
vnsHAPortGroup.comment: HA Port Group for dynamic device

vnsHealthUpdate.comment: An internal payload structure for messaging health information.

vnsInTerm.label: In Terminal
vnsInTerm.comment: An input terminal node. For abstract graphs (AbsGraph), InTerm refers to the terminal node at the input end.

vnsInstPol.label: L4-L7 device Policy
vnsInstPol.comment: L4-L7 device policy for the controller

vnsLBVSvcGrpSvrReq.label: Normalized LB Service Group Server Configuration.

vnsLDevCtx.label: Logical Device Context
vnsLDevCtx.comment: A device cluster context points to the device cluster used to pick a specific device based on contract, subject, and function label or names. To specify a wild card, set the name to Any.

vnsLDevHint.label: Logical Device Context
vnsLDevHint.comment: Contains hints for the device cluster that must be picked for a specific node of a graph that belongs to a specific subject of a specific contract. In the absence of this hint in an abstract graph, a match for a device cluster context will be done based on the name of the contract, subject, and node. If that fails, a match will be made by wildcarding: the contract followed by the contract, and the subject followed by the contract, subject, and node. Note that matching on names and wildcarding will not be performed if a hint is found in the graph

vnsLDevIf.label: Logical Device Consumer
vnsLDevIf.comment: The logical device interface is a proxy object in the tenant that points to the logical device. This is used when the device cluster is defined in a common tenant and other tenants want to use the service provided by this device cluster.

vnsLDevIfLIf.label: Logical Interface
vnsLDevIfLIf.comment: A proxy object in tenant that points to the logical interface of a device.

vnsLDevInst.label: LDev Instance
vnsLDevInst.comment: The local state in the object that reuses encaps across graphs with the same subnet. This is an internal object.

vnsLDevInstTask.comment: An internal object to track the execution state of a task on the logical device instance that holds reusable encap information across service graphs.

vnsLDevOperInfo.label: Logical Device Operational Information
vnsLDevOperInfo.comment: The operational status information from the device gathered during service graph deployment.

vnsLDevVip.label: L4-L7 Devices
vnsLDevVip.comment: An L4-L7 device cluster is represented by a single virtual IP (VIP). The configuration is pushed down to the VIP address.

vnsLIf.label: Logical Interface
vnsLIf.comment: The logical interface is associated with a set of concrete interfaces from the L4-L7 device cluster. This is used to define the connection between a service graph and device interfaces.

vnsLIfCtx.label: Logical Interface Context
vnsLIfCtx.comment: The logical interface context points to the logical interface used to pick a specific logical interface based on the connector name. To specify a wild card, set the name to Any.

vnsLIfHint.label: Logical Interface Hint
vnsLIfHint.comment: Contains hints for the logical interface that must be picked for a specific connector of a graph that belongs to the selected device cluster. The device cluster is selected based on device cluster hint.

vnsLegVNode.label: LegVNode
vnsLegVNode.comment: A classic mode or legacy virtual node.

vnsMCap.label: Device Capability Specification
vnsMCap.comment: The features or capabilities that can be licensed on a device. The capabilities are typically restricted by license. The device is queried for actual values through the script interface.

vnsMConn.label: L4-L7 Service Meta Function Connector
vnsMConn.comment: A connector between logical functions. The attributes includes the cardinality, direction, and encapsulation type for the given connection.

vnsMCred.label: Meta Credential
vnsMCred.comment: The credentials necessary to authenticate a user into the device. For example, key is used for key-based authentication schemes. This model details the meta information for such key-based authentication of credentials.

vnsMCredSecret.label: Meta Credential
vnsMCredSecret.comment: An object that holds the password for logging into a service device.

vnsMDev.label: L4-L7 Service Device Type
vnsMDev.comment: Import a device package into the Application Policy Infrastructure Controller (APIC). The device package contains meta device type definition and definitions of the metadata for a service device type. The metadata contains vendor-specific data including the vendor name, device model, and device version.

vnsMDevCfg.label: L4-L7 Service Meta Device Shared Config
vnsMDevCfg.comment: The base level device configuration. This serves as an anchor to differentiate between different device configurations and the shared configuration (MSharedCfg).

vnsMDfctCat.comment: A representation of the category of a vendor defect.

vnsMDfctCats.comment: A container for vendor defined defect categories.

vnsMDfcts.label: L4-L7 Service Vendor Defined Defect Container
vnsMDfcts.comment: A container for vendor defined defects.

vnsMFolder.label: L4-L7 Services Meta Folder
vnsMFolder.comment: The meta folder information. The model utilizes a generic configuration consisting of MFolders and MParams. This allows the configuration to be specified as a hierarchy.

vnsMFunc.label: L4-L7 Service Function
vnsMFunc.comment: The metadata for a single function on a device. A function contains a set of connectors and a function-specific configuration tree. This managed object contains the metadata for all such operations.

vnsMGrpCfg.label: L4-L7 Service Meta Group Shared Config
vnsMGrpCfg.comment: The meta group configuration. It contains the part of the configuration that can be shared across multiple functions in a group.

vnsMIfLbl.label: L4-L7 Service Meta Interface Label
vnsMIfLbl.comment: An interface label. Interfaces can be labelled in an abstract way on devices. For example, a firewall device could implement trusted, untrusted, and management interfaces. The concrete models would specify how many of each label a given device supports.

vnsMImage.label: Specific Configuration for a Function Type
vnsMImage.comment: The image file of the function. This managed object contains details of the image file and its location. It is used to render the image on the graphical user interface (GUI).

vnsMParam.label: L4-L7 Services Meta Parameter
vnsMParam.comment: A meta parameter. The model utilizes a generic configuration consisting of MFolders and MParams. This enables configuration to be specified as a hierarchy. The metadata within this model consists of a key, a type (integer, boolean), and other attributes related to parameters.

vnsMgmtLIf.label: Logical Interface
vnsMgmtLIf.comment: A representation of the management interface of logical service node cluster.

vnsMscGraphXlateCont.label: Msc graph internal EPgs pc tag translation container
vnsMscGraphXlateCont.comment: Explicit Mo to configure pctag translation for internal EPgs>

vnsNATPATReq.label: Normalized Firewall NAT static port translation parameters

vnsNatInst.label: Translated Address of Service Node
vnsNatInst.comment: The NATed IP of a service node.

vnsNodeInst.label: Node Instance
vnsNodeInst.comment: An instance of a function node. A service graph consists of multiple function nodes..

vnsNodeInstDef.label: Function Node Abstract Class
vnsNodeInstDef.comment: An instance of the service node. This is an internal object.

vnsOutTerm.label: Out Terminal
vnsOutTerm.comment: An output terminal node. For abstract graphs (AbsGraph), OutTerm refers to the terminal node at the output end.

vnsParamInst.label: L4-L7 param instance
vnsParamInst.comment: A parameter instance configured by the administrator.

vnsRTInfo.label: Contains Runtime Info of a Script

vnsRndrInfo.label: Service VDev Rendering Information
vnsRndrInfo.comment: An internal object that stores rendered graph information.

vnsRoutingCfg.label: L4-L7 Service Meta Device Cluster Routing Config

vnsRsALDevToDomP.label: Relation from L4-L7 Devices to a Vmm Domain
vnsRsALDevToDomP.comment: A source relation to the VMM domain profile.

vnsRsALDevToPhysDomP.label: Relation from L4-L7 Devices to a Vlan Namespace Instance Profile
vnsRsALDevToPhysDomP.comment: A source relation to a physical domain profile.

vnsRsALDevToVlanInstP.label: VLAN Pool
vnsRsALDevToVlanInstP.comment: A source relation to the policy definition for ID ranges used for VLAN encapsulation.

vnsRsALDevToVxlanInstP.label: Relation from L4-L7 Devices to a Vxlan Namespace Instance Profile
vnsRsALDevToVxlanInstP.comment: A source relation to the VxLAN namespace policy. Note that this relation is an internal object.

vnsRsAbsConnectionConns.label: Relation from Service Graph Connection to Service Graph Connectors
vnsRsAbsConnectionConns.comment: A source relation to a connector. Note that this relation is an internal object.

vnsRsAbsCopyConnection.label: Relation from Service Graph Connection to CopyConnector
vnsRsAbsCopyConnection.comment: Copy connection connects to copy connector.

vnsRsBDDefToBD.label: Bridge Domain
vnsRsBDDefToBD.comment: A source relation to the bridge domain policy. Note that this relation is an internal object.

vnsRsBDDefToConn.label: Connector
vnsRsBDDefToConn.comment: A source relation to a connector. Note that this relation is an internal object.

vnsRsBDDefToLIf.label: Logical Interface
vnsRsBDDefToLIf.comment: A source relation to the logical interface associated with a set of concrete interfaces from the L4-L7 device cluster. Note that this relation is an internal object.

vnsRsBdConn.label: Connector
vnsRsBdConn.comment: A source relation to a connector. Note that this relation is an internal object.

vnsRsCDevAtt.label: Concrete Device
vnsRsCDevAtt.comment: A source relation to an individual service device that is part of an L4-L7 device cluster. Note that this relation is an internal object.

vnsRsCDevOperInfoToCDev.label: Concrete Device
vnsRsCDevOperInfoToCDev.comment: A source relation to an individual service device that is part of an L4-L7 device cluster. Note that this relation is an internal object.

vnsRsCDevToCtrlrP.label: VMM Controller
vnsRsCDevToCtrlrP.comment: implicit relation to validate the controller profile

vnsRsCDevToVm.label: Virtual Machine
vnsRsCDevToVm.comment: implicit relation to get service VM update (including VNic) from compVm

vnsRsCIfAtt.label: Relation to a Set of Concrete Interfaces from the Device in the Cluster
vnsRsCIfAtt.comment: Association to a set of concrete interfaces from the device in the cluster.

vnsRsCIfAttN.label: Relation to a Set of Concrete Interfaces from the Device in the Cluster

vnsRsCIfPathAtt.label: Relation from Concrete Interface to the Physical Port on the ILeaf
vnsRsCIfPathAtt.comment: A source relation to a path endpoint. Note that this relation is an internal object.

vnsRsCfgToConn.label: Relation from Abstract Configuration to a Connector
vnsRsCfgToConn.comment: A source relation to a connector. Note that this relation is an internal object.

vnsRsCfgToVConn.label: Relation from Abstract Configuration to a Virtual Connector
vnsRsCfgToVConn.comment: A source relation to a virtual connector. Note that this relation is an internal object.

vnsRsChassisEpg.label: Relation from a Device Cluster to an EPG

vnsRsConnToAConn.label: Relation from a Function Connector to either peer function connector or terminal node connector
vnsRsConnToAConn.comment: Optional relation used to figure out the Epg where IP are learnt for the EpAttach feature

vnsRsConnToAConnInst.label: Relation from a Connector Instance to a function/Terminal connector
vnsRsConnToAConnInst.comment: Optional relation - Required only when user wants to specify the connector to use for ep attach

vnsRsConnToCtxTerm.label: Relation from a Function Connector to Terminal Node
vnsRsConnToCtxTerm.comment: A source relation to a terminal. Note that this relation is an internal object.

vnsRsConnToCtxTermInst.label: Relation from a Connector Instance to a Terminal Node
vnsRsConnToCtxTermInst.comment: A source relation to a terminal. Note that this relation is an internal object.

vnsRsConnToFlt.label: Relation from a Function Connector to a Filter Object
vnsRsConnToFlt.comment: A source relation to a filter. Note that this relation is an internal object.

vnsRsConnToFltInst.label: Relation from a Connector Instance to a White List Filter
vnsRsConnToFltInst.comment: A source relation to a filter. Note that this relation is an internal object.

vnsRsConnToLIfInst.label: Relation from a Connector Instance to a Logical Interface
vnsRsConnToLIfInst.comment: A source relation to the logical interface associated with a set of concrete interfaces from the L4-L7 device cluster. Note that this relation is an internal object.

vnsRsConnectionInstConns.label: Connector
vnsRsConnectionInstConns.comment: A source relation to a connector. Note that this relation is an internal object.

vnsRsConnector.label: Relation to L4-L7 Service Configuration Meta Connector
vnsRsConnector.comment: A source relation to a connector between logical functions. Note that this relation is an internal object.

vnsRsDefaultScopeToTerm.label: Relation from a Function Node to a Terminal Node
vnsRsDefaultScopeToTerm.comment: A source relation to a terminal. Note that this relation is an internal object.

vnsRsDevEpg.label: Relation from a L4-L7 Devices to an EPG
vnsRsDevEpg.comment: A source relation for storing management endpoint group related information. This object indicates that the device belongs to the management endpoint group.

vnsRsDevFolderToMFolder.label: L4-L7 Services Meta Folder
vnsRsDevFolderToMFolder.comment: A source relation to an MFolder path.

vnsRsDevMgrEpg.label: Relation from a Device Cluster to an EPG

vnsRsDevPingToCDev.label: Relation from a Device Ping Object to a Concrete Service Device
vnsRsDevPingToCDev.comment: This property is managed internally and should not be modified by the user.

vnsRsDfctToCat.label: Relation to L4-L7 Service Vendor to Cisco Defined Defect
vnsRsDfctToCat.comment: A source relation to the Cisco defined category of the defect.

vnsRsEPgDefToConn.label: Connector
vnsRsEPgDefToConn.comment: A source relation to a connector.

vnsRsEPgDefToLIf.label: Logical Interface
vnsRsEPgDefToLIf.comment: A source relation to a logical interface.

vnsRsEPpInfoAtt.label: Service VDev EPP Information
vnsRsEPpInfoAtt.comment: A source relation to an internal object that stores the encap (vlan/vxlan) information of an endpoint.

vnsSvcGraphVersion.label: Service Graph Model Version
vnsSvcGraphVersion.comment: The version of the entire service graph model. This is validated against the device script APIC model version. This number is of the form x.y, where x represents the major version number and y represents the minor version number of the service graph model. Guidelines: 1. The minor version is increased whenever a backward compatible change is made. This could include adding new properties or managed objects in the service graph model. It is expected that a device script with an earlier minor version number will continue to operate (albeit at a possibly reduced capability). 2. The major version number is increased whenever a backward incompatible change is made. A device script with an earlier major version number will fail to load.

vpcIf.label: VPC Interface
vpcIf.comment: The vPC interfaces information enables links that are physically connected to two different Fabric devices to appear as a single port channel by a third device. The third device can be a switch, server, or any other networking device that supports port channels. A vPC can provide Layer 2 multipathing, which allows you to create redundancy and increase bisectional bandwidth by enabling multiple parallel paths between nodes and allowing load balancing traffic.

vpcIfTask.comment: The information returned by or provided to the application or task.

vpcInst.label: VPC Instance

vpcInstPol.label: VPC Domain Policy
vpcInstPol.comment: The node-level vPC domain policy, which is used to specify a vPC domain and is applied to both vPC peer devices, the vPC peer keepalive link, the vPC peer link, and all the PortChannels in the vPC domain connected to the downstream device. You can have only one vPC domain ID on each device.

vpcRtVpcInstPolCons.label: VPC Domain

vzAllocateSharedService.label: Re-allocate pcTag from Shared Service Pool

vzAny.label: Any
vzAny.comment: vzAny associates all endpoint groups (EPGs) in a context (fvCtx) to one or more contracts (vzBrCP), rather than creating a separate contract relation for each EPG. EPGs can only communicate with other EPGs according to contract rules. A relationship between an EPG and a contract specifies whether the EPG provides the communications defined by the contract rules, consumes them, or both. By dynamically applying contract rules to all EPGs in a context, vzAny automates the process of configuring EPG contract relationships. Whenever a new EPG is added to a context, vzAny contract rules automatically apply. The vzAny one-to-all EPG relationship is the most efficient way of applying contract rules to all EPGs in a context.

vzAnyToCollection.label: Super Class for Each any to Collection Relation
vzAnyToCollection.comment: A superclass for all relations from vzAny to contract/bundle/group.

vzAnyToInterface.label: Super Class for Each any to Collection Interface Relation
vzAnyToInterface.comment: Super class for all relations from vzAny to contract/bundle interface

vzBrCP.label: Contract
vzBrCP.comment: A contract is a logical container for the subjects which relate to the filters that govern the rules for communication between endpoint groups (EPGs). Without a contract, the default forwarding policy is to not allow any communication between EPGs but all communication within an EPG is allowed.

vzBrCPTask.comment: An internal object that tracks a task on a binary contract profile.

vzCPIf.label: Imported Contract
vzCPIf.comment: A contract interface is used as a contract consumption interface when a consumer consumes the contract by associating it to a consumption interface provided by the provider in the consumer's domain. A consumer can associate with the contract consumption interface when it is provided by the provider in the consumer's domain. Note that a contract consumption interface represents one or more subjects defined under the contract. By associating to an interface, an endpoint group starts consuming all the subjects represented by the interface. Also note that a contract can be defined under one tenant, but its interfaces can be defined in other tenants. A contract's interface will be used by the entities (endpoint groups) present in other tenants to participate in that contract. By this flexibility, tenants we will be able to participate in a single contract, which is defined at some third place. A contract interface is parented by a Tenant.

vzCons.label: Keeps track of the DN of a rule creator

vzConsCtrctLbl.label: Consumer Contract Label
vzConsCtrctLbl.comment: A consumer contract label. A contract label can be parented by the relation between an endpoint group (EPG) and security group. The EPG is associated with a group and lists all contracts it provides out of the group, as well as, optionally, contracts that it chooses to consume. If no consumption contracts are indicated, all contracts are consumed. If no provider contracts are identified, the EPG provides no contracts out of this group.

vzConsLbl.label: Consumer Label
vzConsLbl.comment: A label used by consumers to filter the providers. The label can be parented as follows: * By the consumer endpoint group. * By the relation between the consumer endpoint group and contract. * By the relation between the contract interface and contract. * By the relation between the consumer endpoint group and contract interface. A consumer with no label will consume from all the providers of the contract with no labels. A consumer with a specific label can only consume from providers matching the label.

vzConsSubjLbl.label: Subject Label
vzConsSubjLbl.comment: A consumer subject label. In general, a subject label is used as a classification criteria for subjects being consumed/provided by the endpoint groups (EPGs) participating in the contract. The label identifies a subject being consumed by a consumer. It can be parented by 2 different methods. The first method is the relation between the consumer EPG and the contract that is used for filtering the subjects. A label should match either the subject name or the label present under the subject. The second method is by subject, which is the application behind the EPG that can have different characteristics for different labels present under it. A consumer with no subject consumer label consumes all the subjects of the contract. A consumer with a specific subject consumer label can only consume subjects matching the label.

vzContractCfgReprocessor.comment: Tracks Reprocessing of Contracts in node

vzCreatedBy.comment: A container that captures information about the creator of an object.

If no consumption contracts are indicated, all contracts are consumed. If no provider contracts are identified, the EPG provides no contracts out of this group.

A consumer with no label will consume from all the providers of the contract regardless of the provider label. A consumer with a specific label can only consume from providers matching the label.

vzProvSubjLbl.label: Subject Label
vzProvSubjLbl.comment: A subject label is used as classification criteria for subjects being consumed/provided by the endpoint groups (EPGs) participating in the contract. The label identifies a subject being provided by a provider. It can be parented by 2 different methods. The first method is the relation between the provider EPG and the contract that is used for filtering the subjects. A label should match either the subject name or the label present under the subject. The second method is by subject, which is the application behind the EPG that can have different characteristics for different labels present under it. A consumer with no subject consumer label consumes all the subjects of the contract. A consumer with a specific subject consumer label can only consume subjects matching the label.

vzRuleIdTracker.label: Keeps track of the creators of those rules that are failed to deploy due to lack of free rule Ids

vzSubj.label: Contract Subject
vzSubj.comment: A subject is a sub-application running behind an endpoint group (for example, an Exchange server). A subject is parented by the contract, which can encapsulate multiple subjects. An endpoint group associated to a contract is providing one or more subjects or is communicating with the subject as a peer entity. An endpoint group always associates with a subject and defines rules under the association for consuming/providing/peer-to-peer communications to that subject.

vzSubjectToFilter.label: Super Class for Subject-Filter relation
vzSubjectToFilter.comment: Super class for all relations from subject to filter

vzTSubj.label: Taboo Contract Subject
vzTSubj.comment: The subjects for a service contract represents a sub-application running behind an endpoint group, such as an exchange server. A subject is parented by the contract.

vzTaboo.label: Taboo Contract
vzTaboo.comment: A Taboo contract provides a way for an endpoint group to specify the subjects on which communication is not allowed.

vzTabooTask.comment: A task to process a taboo.

vzToEPgSiteId.label: Identify Site ID for a ToEPg

vzToRFltP.label: Super Class for Any Relation to RFltP
vzToRFltP.comment: A superclass for all relations to RFltP.

vzTrCreatedBy.comment: Tracks the resource that created a Taboo rule.

wwnRtWwnInstPol.label: Abstraction of Leaf FC Access Policy Group
wwnRtWwnInstPol.comment: Relationship to WWN policy to pull the policy when Fc is configured on a node

xcvrOpticsFabIfPol.label: Abstraction of Optics Interface Policy
xcvrOpticsFabIfPol.comment: Transceiver Optics Interface Policy. Applicable to fabric ports

xcvrOpticsIfPol.label: Abstraction of Optics Interface Policy
xcvrOpticsIfPol.comment: Transceiver Optics Interface Policy. Applicable to access ports

xcvrRtOpticsFabIfPol.comment: Relationship to policy providing transceiver optics configuration of the interfaces type="named" removed, as relation is with abstract parent class

xcvrRtOpticsIfPol.label: Abstraction of Access Policy Group
xcvrRtOpticsIfPol.comment: Relationship to policy providing transceiver optics configuration of the interfaces type="named" removed, as relation is with abstract parent class

xcvrRtXcvrOpticsFabIfPolCons.label: Ethernet Interface
xcvrRtXcvrOpticsFabIfPolCons.comment: Relationship with the xcvrOpticsFabIfPol policy consumed by this physical interface if fabric port

xcvrRtXcvrOpticsIfPolCons.label: Ethernet Interface
xcvrRtXcvrOpticsIfPolCons.comment: Relationship with the xcvrOpticsIfPol policy consumed by this physical interface if access port
